Review: The freshest juice ever, good sauce in pasta with decent tanginess and pizzas are really flavoured but the base was really thin.

Review: Very good cafe

Name: Cafe C
Address: The Chanakya, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Khushboo Jha
Review: Coffee was amazing and I am  definitely going back for its coffee and ambience and service!

Name: Cafe C
Address: The Chanakya, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Varun Sahgal
Review:
Located at the ground floor of The Chankaya mall, this cafe C is an open sitting place in the atrium.

They have an extensive beverage menu. Happy and courteous staff members. Kamal helped us decide.

We had a flat white coffee and a cold coffee with whipped cream. We topped it all up with Belgium cake slice. Nice place to sit, have a chat and enjoy the coffee. They have food items as well but we didn’t explore much.

Flat white and the cake slice is very nice. The cold coffee is good but my choice of topping it with whipped cream was wrong. The whipped cream at Cafe C is a complete NO NO.  It’s nothing like the normal whipped cream I have at other places.

The place is too expensive and for no practical reason. It’s expensive just cos of the place it is in and not cos of the stuff they sell.

About the mall, this place is an alternative to DLF Emporio, Vasant Kunj. An epitome of luxury and excellence. High end brands like Tiffany, Saint Laurent, etc attract the HNIs around. They have a restaurant and a high end grocery store at the lower ground floor. This place is for the diplomats who stay around this place and others who visit.

Good place to pamper yourself with some luxury.

Name: Cafe C
Address: The Chanakya, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Jairus Onsafari
Review: Great chic spot for your coffee and petit delights. Cozy atmosphere and upmarket feel

Name: Cafe C
Address: The Chanakya, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Mr Vipin Kumar
Review: taking into account the entire experience, from the quality of the coffee to the ambiance, customer service, and any extra services offered by the establishment

Name: Cafe C
Address: The Chanakya, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Bhavya Sharma
Review: Great ambience and good food. I do wish they'd widen the variety of food offered and perhaps also include a couple vegan items. Love this place for high tea though.

Name: Cafe Chapter 23
Address: UGF01, Rise palm street, sector 1 greater Noida, west, near ATS roundabout, Noida
Author: Utkarsh Mehrotra
Review:
The person who serves food is very friendly and greets and behaves like he is your family member.

Ambience: Very nice ambience with artificial grass flooring and nice furniture along with a music system with surround sound. It's rooftop hence you feel quite open & fresh.

Food: The menu as updated on maps is same as you get while dining. Best Chinese taste I can say in this area. Plus the chef special are a must try.

AddOn: Wooden blocks and toys for children. They informed you can throw a party with friends space will be extended for that event.

Overall: A must visit place for foodies to enjoy the taste as well as the ambience which makes your dining experience more scintillating.

Name: Cafe Chapter 23
Address: UGF01, Rise palm street, sector 1 greater Noida, west, near ATS roundabout, Noida
Author: Anu singh
Review: Food was flavourful, a good place to hangout with friends on a pleasant evening.

Name: Cafe Chapter 23
Address: UGF01, Rise palm street, sector 1 greater Noida, west, near ATS roundabout, Noida
Author: Kraftify
Review:
The food here is very fresh & different because it is prepared without preservatives & customised according to your instruction & order.

The best part here is that
the master chef is a lady
with motherly love...❤

when you dine here the vibe is very soothing & relaxing.

The place is good for parents of children aged 2 to 10 years.

There are amenities to entertain children as well as sound & video system for all ages.

The person serving food is very cordial & friendly,
he builts & maintain relations with customers like a family member.

Overall a best place to dine out on weekends as well as throw a party for friends & children.

Name: Cafe Chapter 23
Address: UGF01, Rise palm street, sector 1 greater Noida, west, near ATS roundabout, Noida
Author: Vrinda Chitranshi
Review: A good place to hangout with friends..good ambience and music..staff is friendly and helpful. Food was also delicious.

Name: Cafe Chapter 23
Address: UGF01, Rise palm street, sector 1 greater Noida, west, near ATS roundabout, Noida
Author: Harpreet Bhatia
Review: Really amazing and delicious food with warm hospitality. If you don't like too many people around you while to sit and enjoy your food on a lovely evening, this cafe is for you. A good space to enjoy your evenings with your spouse.

Name: Dode's Cafe
Address: Plot no. 219-B, Shakti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: Thiri Aung
Review: I recently attended a party there and I am impressed by the quality of service provided. Everything was great! Will get back to this Cafe for any event organizing. 👍

Name: Dode's Cafe
Address: Plot no. 219-B, Shakti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: Election Commission 2024
Review: I did my daughter’s birthday there but I didn’t like the atmosphere and the way they treat our guest and ourself. pathetic service and pathetic food. i will not recommend anyone to do the party n celebration there. it was hot and they switched off the AC everytime during the party.

Name: Dode's Cafe
Address: Plot no. 219-B, Shakti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: sandeep kumar
Review:
We have celebrated our kid birthday at Dode's cafe last week in evening,everything was very good onwer,madam and working staff .we will surley recommend this place for birthday party celebration .
Food: we have taken both option i.e package for kids and ala carte for seniors.everone liked the  food. But after party i realised we should take took basic Pakege ,although everything was perfect, thanks Dode's cafe making our son's Birthday memorable.

Name: Dode's Cafe
Address: Plot no. 219-B, Shakti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: bhupesh Arya
Review:
Hi Dedo's

I just wanted to extend my deepest thanks for hosting such a fantastic birthday party for my daughter! The entire experience was beyond our expectations, and we are truly grateful for all the care and attention to detail that went into making her day so special.

The venue was beautifully arranged, and the food was exceptional—our guests were raving about it! But what truly stood out was the warmth and hospitality of your staff, which made everyone feel welcome and comfortable.

A special thank you to Owner for personally ensuring everything was perfect. Your dedication and passion are evident in every aspect of the café, and it made the celebration even more meaningful for us.

We are so happy we chose Dedo's Cafe for this important day and will certainly be returning in the future!

With sincere appreciation.
Bhupesh

Name: Dode's Cafe
Address: Plot no. 219-B, Shakti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: Ajay Chandra
Review: Highly recommended.  Best place to have chole bhature and specially falooda I loved it. Been there a lot of times and owners are really sweet in nature. You can get your food spice levels customized as per your appetite. Would highly recommend whoever's in and around the area.

Name: Hell and Heaven Cafe
Address: TMH Court, Ahinsa Khand 1, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: Pallavi Kumari
Review: A burger is a savory sandwich consisting of a grilled or fried patty, typically made from beef or a plant-based alternative, served between two halves of a bun. Toppings and condiments vary widely, offering endless flavor combinations to suit diverse tastes.

Name: Hell and Heaven Cafe
Address: TMH Court, Ahinsa Khand 1, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: shuchi chauhan

You can with our kids , they will also enjoy it .

Name: Hell and Heaven Cafe
Address: TMH Court, Ahinsa Khand 1, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad

Name: Hell and Heaven Cafe
Address: TMH Court, Ahinsa Khand 1, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: Tanmay Shriwas
Review: Best place, awesome adventures to do. Zipline, trampuline

Name: Kartoon Cafe Janakpuri
Address: The Fusion House, 246, janakpuri, Block C4F, Janakpuri, Delhi
Author: Paras Jhanji
Review:
A cute n beautiful cafe cum bakery located in Janakpuri. It’s on the first floor and also has a lift for easy access.

The cafe has multiple types of sitting spaces including round tables, cushions, etc. The entire vibe of the places if really colourful with various types of artwork and wall hangings. An instagrammable cafe indeed.

The cafe’s menu is well prepared and has lots of options for all kinds of meals.

The staff is pretty courteous. The ground floor has a stilt parking as well as a couple of clean washrooms.

Post billing, the staff showed their gratitude by offering a complimentary pastry. The gesture was pretty special!

Name: Kartoon Cafe Janakpuri
Address: The Fusion House, 246, janakpuri, Block C4F, Janakpuri, Delhi
Author: Daman Preet
Review:
I had a couple of visits to the Rajouri garden outlet but in janakpuri this time was my first visit.
As i entered, a cool guy came towards me and asked for pax, he looked around very politely and said it would take a couple of mins for the table. We waited for approximately 4-5 mins and settled down. We ordered a few dishes and while we were offered some games.
First we got served, pink pasta (non veg) the taste was superb , garlic bread was good and there was a good amount of chicken in it.
"But" pasta was not cooked properly i mean it was aldente.... That i didn't liked otherwise it was a good option to choose.
Then we got bbq chicken pizza, that was 😍
And lastly we got a zingi chicken sandwich as club sandwich was not available.
That was delicious 😋.

#Foodshood

Name: Kartoon Cafe Janakpuri
Address: The Fusion House, 246, janakpuri, Block C4F, Janakpuri, Delhi
Author: savneet kaur
Review:
Delicious food, value for money, unique propositions for everyone. Classy songs.
Great ambiance.
Humble staff. Overall great experience for an outing.

Name: Kartoon Cafe Janakpuri
Address: The Fusion House, 246, janakpuri, Block C4F, Janakpuri, Delhi
Author: Saloni Verma
Review:
Good:
Good service and generally good food too.

Could improve:
It was generally not brimming with people considering we went on a Saturday which is why the only thing we had an issue with is that even with a few people inside, they are a bit hesitant for people to sit on the cushioned seats on one side. They’d rather sit you down on the chairs and table which can get uncomfortable after a while - specially if it’s a group of people who are planning on sitting there for almost 6-7 hours.
The washroom is downstairs.

Notes:
They also have 3 points of entry which can be confusing or wonderful how you choose to look at it. People were always going in and out.

They have a dedicated corner for birthdays or parties I believe where you can get your pictures clicked. They are wonderful about cleaning up after - we noticed two groups celebrating birthdays and leaving balloons and trash just there. Difficult for the cleaning staff, but the staff are very polite and don’t let it show at all. We left a good tip as we were happy with our service.

Name: Kartoon Cafe Janakpuri
Address: The Fusion House, 246, janakpuri, Block C4F, Janakpuri, Delhi
Author: Manvi Saxena
Review:
The best cafe I've been for a over a while now! The watchman literally offered me to open my car's gate, and as if that was not enough, he was extremely polite and chivalric. Talking about the atmosphere, it was so chic and pleasant! I loved that the service staff was extremely polite and accommodating. We ordered cold coffee (3.8/5), watermelon juice(4.8/5) and chicken quesadilla(4.8/5). The food was good, although the cold coffee was something I didn't like in particular, perhaps it could have been thicker. Apart from that, we also ordered tiramisu cheesecake to go, and since it got a bit delayed in packaging (which was great, btw), they even offered a slice of blueberry pastry(4.2/5) on the house as a form of apology for the slight delay. Needless, to say I was impressed! ✨

I highly recommend this cafe!

Name: Brownie Cafe
Address: Arakashan Rd, Arya Nagar, Paharganj, New Delhi
Author: Onishi “Takachang3994” Takanori
Review: It was good until last year when a male employee was working, but since an Indian woman took over, the service has become terrible. After paying, I had a small amount of change, which I intended to leave, but she didn’t even bring it back (apparently assuming it was a tip). She also cleared the dishes without asking, which was extremely unpleasant. I will never go there again.

Name: Brownie Cafe
Address: Arakashan Rd, Arya Nagar, Paharganj, New Delhi
Author: shishir paudel
Review: I asked for a combo breakfast but they served me with full of tomatoes and the service was also too poor

Name: Brownie Cafe
Address: Arakashan Rd, Arya Nagar, Paharganj, New Delhi
Author: Martin Vila
Review: Great place, chill music, amazing service. Would definitely come back

Name: Brownie Cafe
Address: Arakashan Rd, Arya Nagar, Paharganj, New Delhi
Author: Will B
Review:
The food was not great, very basic and the service was also quite poor.

We ordered a croissant, banana pancakes, muesli, coffee and a smoothie only to be told that the croissant and muesli on the menu were not available so instead ordered toast and some different cereal. About 5-10 mins later they came back to our table to confirm the order rather than write it down originally.

We then waited approximately half an hour for the food to turn up despite being mostly very simple to make (toast and cereal!), the smoothie was also missing and the toast was cold, and banana pancakes were actually dry pancakes with a banana on the side.

Don't recommend it, it wasn't even that cheap.

Name: Brownie Cafe
Address: Arakashan Rd, Arya Nagar, Paharganj, New Delhi
Author: Gaurab Ray
Review:
It is a very cheap and pretty cafe with super awesome food. We had the roasted chicken pizza, grilled chicken sandwich, chicken ceasar salad, brownie frappe and mocha frappe. Each and very single item was extremely delicious and fresh!
Looking forward to visit this place soon.

Name: Time out cafe (Delivery Only)
Address: SHOP NO-SF-48, 2nd FLOOR, Nipun Wonder Mall, PLOT NO-CS-A, Swarn Shakti Ln, Shakti Khand 2, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad
Author: Prerna Garg
Review:
Ordered combo of Hakka noodles & manchurian from this place after my friends recommendation. Trust this was too good in size. And you can check from pictures how good the portion is.
Packaging was too good. It didn’t spill even a single drop. I very rarely see such incidence while ordering for Chinese. I always receive spill food. But I am amazed here to see the packaging. Quantity of both were too good. The gravy was too much. And too good in taste. Manchurian balls were huge(check pictures) and too soft. Also, I want to mention that it was very well cooked. Not the taste of Maida and all.
And from the quantity you can see it’s not for a single person. 2 person can easily full their tummy.
My experience was too good. Looking forward to order again from this place and try some other things too.😃😃😃

The texture of it was like when you bring Savorite seviyan and Your new cook makes it into glop by over cooking the noodles-showed pack of understanding how to handle the delicate noodles-they were also not flat noodles or chow chow it was some weird kind of vermicelli type white noodles

There were hardly any vegetables in it which really killed our enthusiasm coz without the right noodles there’s isn’t much we could enjoy the chilli chicken and fries with

Ambience was cool-the fact that I had to climb the stairs to get a good bite to eat or a beverage was not fun

Most importantly, the staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, going out of their way to ensure we had a great time. Shout out to Nida. I highly recommend Legato for anyone looking for great food and a warm atmosphere!

Review: it's a nice place to hangout. The food is good for it's price

Name: Cafe DTU
Address: Shop no 23, Street no 1, Pocket 9, Sector17 Extension, Shahbad Extension Jindal Market, Rohini, Delhi
Author: Animesh Jha
Review:
My recent visit to this cafe was nothing short of disappointing. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted with a lack of enthusiasm from the staff, setting the tone for a lackluster experience.

The cafe's ambiance was far from inviting. The lighting was dim, and the furniture looked worn-out and shabby. It gave off an overall sense of neglect, making me question the cafe's commitment to maintaining a welcoming atmosphere.

The menu options were limited, and the food and beverages I ordered were bland and unremarkable. It felt like the cafe had lost its touch in creating flavorsome dishes. I was hoping for a memorable culinary experience, but sadly, that was not the case.

The service was painfully slow, with long waiting times to get even the simplest orders. The staff seemed disorganized and uncoordinated, resulting in a frustrating wait for our food and drinks.

Furthermore, the cleanliness of the cafe left much to be desired. Dirty tables and untidy surroundings indicated a lack of attention to hygiene and sanitation, which is unacceptable in any dining establishment.

To add to the disappointment, the prices were unjustifiably high for the poor quality of food and service provided. I expected much better value for my money, but sadly, it was a wasted investment.

Overall, this cafe failed to meet even the basic expectations of a dining establishment. I wouldn't recommend this place to anyone seeking a pleasant dining experience. It's safe to say that I won't be returning to this cafe anytime soon. One star is all I can give, and it's a generous rating, considering the lack of satisfaction during my visit.

Name: Cafe DTU
Address: Shop no 23, Street no 1, Pocket 9, Sector17 Extension, Shahbad Extension Jindal Market, Rohini, Delhi

Review: I like the taste very much .

Address: Top Floor, 116 C, behind UCO Bank, Shahpur Jat, New Delhi
Author: Abhishek Nautiyal
Review: It's on the 4th floor and there is no lift. They don't serve aerated drinks, so if anyone is looking for a coke or something carry that in advance, the taste of food and Ambience is amazing. They serve portions in a plate. The place had AC and indoor plants to keep you cool during the hot summer.

Name: The Potbelly Rooftop Cafe
Address: Top Floor, 116 C, behind UCO Bank, Shahpur Jat, New Delhi
Author: Amogh nath pal
Review:
Potbelly Rooftop Café is a bit of an adventure before you even sit down—it’s four floors up with no elevator in sight, so if you’re not prepared for a climb, it can feel like a workout. Once you make it, though, the rooftop setting is worth it for the relaxed, quirky atmosphere.

I tried the phataka French fries, which was a pleasant surprise—a Bihari twist on the classic chili potato, packed with flavor. The Maithili thali offered an array of traditional dishes, but I have to admit, it wasn’t quite my cup of tea. I think that’s mostly because I’m new to Bihari cuisine, so the flavors were different from what I’m used to. The aam panna was refreshing, a good cooler for the Delhi heat.

Overall, the food is good, just not quite aligned with my personal tastes—but that might change if I get more familiar with the regional cuisine. Definitely a spot for those who enjoy discovering something off the beaten path.

Name: The Potbelly Rooftop Cafe
Address: Top Floor, 116 C, behind UCO Bank, Shahpur Jat, New Delhi
Author: arpita srivastava
Review: Situated at bihar niwas, this is an amazing bihari restaurant. The food is delectable. We ordered mutton champaran which was melt in the mouth, khade masale ka chicken which was spicy and warm in the throat, and litti chokha which was tangy savoury and very yummy. Along with the dishes we had parathas which were kind of dry, although the puris were nice and fresh. For drinks we had the masala chaach and aam panna which were also pretty decent. There is no concept of ala carte dishes. Hence the quantity of the main dish is less for the price you pay. But nevertheless the food  is quite nice and the ambience is neat and decent.

Name: The Potbelly Rooftop Cafe
Address: Top Floor, 116 C, behind UCO Bank, Shahpur Jat, New Delhi
Author: Neelakshi Sharma
Review: What a cute little place, though it's tucked away in the streets and has narrow stairs. Despite that, everything else is top-notch. I visited in 2017, then again in 2018, and now, after many years, it has improved significantly. The food is amazing and unique, featuring Bihari cuisine with a distinct taste. The litti chokha, poori bhaji and mutton keema burger are exceptional.

Name: The Potbelly Rooftop Cafe
Address: Top Floor, 116 C, behind UCO Bank, Shahpur Jat, New Delhi

Review:

This time, we had the Khada Masala Mutton with poori and Sarson Macchli with rice. Both were excellent.

Review:
All green tea lovers are recommended to buy their product.The quality HOG are providing is excellent in today’s market.

Just go and buy a one for you and your loved ones. ✌️

Name: House Of Gardens
Address: Gulmohur Greens Rd, Om nagar Mohan Nagar, Gulmohur Greens, Mohan Nagar, Ghaziabad
Author: Garima tyagi
Review: House of Gardens is the best Green Tea brand i have ever came across. Packaging and the quality of their tea is really premium.

Name: Mittal Tea House
Address: Shop No 8, Main Market, Block 8, Lodi Colony, New Delhi
Author: Nita B Choudhury
Review: I love this place! Since we are a family of tea lovers it's a virtual treasure trove for us. They have a large variety of teas. From white tea to Darjeeling, Assam, masala tea, Kashmiri Kahwa and so many more. There are diffusers and tea pots, timers and strainers as well as a different kinds of gift packs for all pockets. A must visit store for anyone who loves tea. The staff is very polite and helpful and they also have this service of sampling a particular tea if you are still uncertain of whether to buy it. I've been introduced to a number of exotic blends due to this.s

Name: Mittal Tea House
Address: Shop No 8, Main Market, Block 8, Lodi Colony, New Delhi
Author: Tulika Sinha
Review:
While I really enjoyed going through their collection and selecting the tea that I wanted to buy, the experience was unfortunately ruined towards the end by implicit derogatory remarks by the lady who probably owned the shop. Do not be misled by the tasting counter as if you ask for a tea tasting, you’ll receive judgemental looks and not so courteous response. The tea is put on display but we didn’t get to taste as their water was over was initial reason we were given.
The experience would have been normal had the female not said the following - “Hum ek hi cup taste karwaenge. Aisa nahi ki aap 10 cup taste kar lo. “ when I asked her the time till they provide tasting in the morning. My friend casually just mentioned that - “Anyways if you taste so many it becomes difficult to choose and you generally try 1-2”. But she then went on again with her mean remarks “no, no. Foreigners toh pi lete hain..and Wo aise bhi 20-25k ka khareed k le jaate hain na” - probably trying to highlight that they still judge customers by how much they would pay and not what they need. Not sure if this is an over reaction, but it was clearly a very rude statement to hear as a customer.

Name: Mittal Tea House
Address: Shop No 8, Main Market, Block 8, Lodi Colony, New Delhi
Author: Robin Snow
Review: Convenient for foreigners, they have new crops, Darjeeling Asam, Kashmiri and lots of fragrant infused tea. International cards accepted which is a plus. Refer to their website for the types, closed on Monday

Name: Mittal Tea House
Address: Shop No 8, Main Market, Block 8, Lodi Colony, New Delhi
Author: Sanjeev Mehta
Review: Been getting my tea here for the past 15 years. Awesome variety

Name: Mittal Tea House
Address: Shop No 8, Main Market, Block 8, Lodi Colony, New Delhi
Author: Christian Jauslin
Review: Fantastic expierence with really good quality tea. We enjoyed the best and most friendly assistance with much knowlede about Indian tea. The whole staff was wonderful helping us. We could try some of the tea. The shop is nice, clean and perfect in size. Loved the visit, would definitely go to this hidden gem again. For tea my favorit!

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: ShShop No-9, Community Centre, Pramod Mahajan Marg, Market, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Anshul Yadav
Review:
Wagh Bakri Tea 🍵 Lounge
Beverage cafe of the same name as the Indian Tea maker Wagh Bakri.
Sitting area , Cafe Atmosphere is warm , peaceful and calming.
Tea is really good, I had adrak tea.
A worth visiting place for having your hot cuppa of tea.
Cafe is also serving raw Tea of their own brand
Serving is in premium cups 🍶 and you will definitely be liking it.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: ShShop No-9, Community Centre, Pramod Mahajan Marg, Market, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Gurpreet Virk
Review:
One of the best tea lounges I have been to recently. Good option of tea and you can buy fresh tea packets as well including gourmet tea.
Service is good and the lounge inside is cosy.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: ShShop No-9, Community Centre, Pramod Mahajan Marg, Market, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Sonali Gaur
Review: Not a good ambience at all. Too hot and Ac not working . Congested space. No WiFi available. 1 Star overall .

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: ShShop No-9, Community Centre, Pramod Mahajan Marg, Market, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Shivani Meera Sharma
Review:
It was my first time to munch at a tea lounge and i must say that the experience was awesome.
We ordered adrak tea and bun maska.The tea is as good as it should be.Also,they serve one complimentary serving of khakhra with your tea.
The Ambience is pretty cool and the service was very good.
They have also in store shoppe for various kinds of tea,Tumblr and snacks etc.
Happy with my experience.
Would love to visit again.
If you're a tea lover,do give it a shot.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: ShShop No-9, Community Centre, Pramod Mahajan Marg, Market, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Netra Singhal
Review:
I loved the service, the atmosphere, the vibes of this place.
Drinks and food items all are really good. The croissant specially was delicious.
You can also buy many varieties of tea packs from here.
It's inside Anupam complex.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: M, 58, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Market, New Delhi
Author: Lubna R
Review: Good tea lounge. A nice place to sit quietly or with your group and have tea ( out of so many options of tea present). Light snacks available too. Perfect for chai lovers who are very particular about how the tea is being made. Bakery/cakes are not very tasty, average.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: M, 58, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Market, New Delhi
Author: Satyamedha Guidance
Review: This is good place for refreshing and have a tea. Here is parking issue and no wheelchair access. Mostly food is vegetarian and atmosphere here highly crowded in evening time. It is not kids friendliness.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: M, 58, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Market, New Delhi
Author: MONICA MAHAJAN
Review:
The seating is very limited, especially the comfortable ones away from the doors.
AC is very often non functional.  Few seats are covered by the AC.
Don't know why but the AC is getting repaired/ cleaned/ serviced during open hours. It's impossible to sit there with endless manpower on your head literally as the AC is overhead

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: M, 58, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Market, New Delhi
Author: Ranjeeb Sarma
Review: While the atmosphere is great and just right for the tea lovers, the service is pathetic, complicated by a single cash counter. The person "Suman" looked lost and didn't know the  queue and couldn't distinguish between an orthodox and CTC tea....perhaps they could do better to get their people trained and specially if you are selling an experience!

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: M, 58, Block M, Greater Kailash II, Market, New Delhi
Author: Kanchan Chakraborty
Review: Frappe was bad. Equally bad was Adrak Tea. Each Dry Fruit Cookie cost was ₹75 - but it was just a lump of wheat flour. Samosa Chat taste was very bad. Overall, it’s a high price bad food cafe.

Name: Tea Lounge
Address: Taj Palace, Taj Palace, New Delhi, Lobby Level 2, 2, Sardar Patel Marg, Diplomatic Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Angela Han
Review:
This beautiful place had so much potential...
The taste of pastries and tea are terrible...(I pretty much ordered everything from the menu.)
Heads up, please do not take their cookies or macaroons. That peanut butter cookie was the worst I ever had in life.
Service takes inordinate amount of time and gives very little details. Which we didn't know the tea comes with more cookies...
We often just sit here and keep on a waiting game to see who's available to assist us...

Name: Tea Lounge
Address: Taj Palace, Taj Palace, New Delhi, Lobby Level 2, 2, Sardar Patel Marg, Diplomatic Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Ruch Agg
Review: Went in the evening about 5pm. We were seated quickly. The table had not been cleared. They took ages to tidy up. We had to ask for menus which never came. Looked at the food/ pastries - all looked tired and unappetising. For instance the red velvet cake had the strangest colour. Unfortunately, we left as having waited for 30 minutes no one bothered to show up with even a menu. Very sad state of affairs

Name: Tea Lounge
Address: Taj Palace, Taj Palace, New Delhi, Lobby Level 2, 2, Sardar Patel Marg, Diplomatic Enclave, New Delhi
Author: karan bhatia
Review:
Food is top notch
Everything was perfectly satisfying
Service was a bit slow
But you'll not mind that at all when you are at a such a perfect ambience
Vibes of the place adds to the quality of food too
This place is an example

Name: Tea Lounge
Address: Taj Palace, Taj Palace, New Delhi, Lobby Level 2, 2, Sardar Patel Marg, Diplomatic Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Sunayana Khandelwal
Review: Nice place to have your morning tea. The place has cover charges.

Name: Tea Lounge
Address: Taj Palace, Taj Palace, New Delhi, Lobby Level 2, 2, Sardar Patel Marg, Diplomatic Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Aman Kumar Vikrant
Review: I had a pleasant experience at the tea lounge. The cozy ambiance provided a nice atmosphere for relaxation, and the menu offered a variety of flavorful dishes. The staff was friendly and attentive. Overall, I would recommend it for a decent dining and lounging experience.🫡🫡🫡🫶🏻🫶🏻

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: L-7, Connaught Cir, Block L, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Punit
Review:
Its such a cool, cozy place to just relax amidst the crowded cannaught place streets, take a break and have a great sip of tea. They have pretty much every kind of tea/coffee, as well as most of the popular Indian snacks & cookies.

Menu prices are fair for the taste & experience overall. You'll certainly love the vibe. Parking lot next to this lounge is Rs 20 per hour for cars.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: L-7, Connaught Cir, Block L, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Kalpana Lohumi
Review: I have never given anyone such a bad review, but this place really needs to work on its services. The quality of the food was really bad. We have ordered tea; first they offered us cold tea, and when we requested to get us warm tea, the staff reheated the same tea in the oven and did not even bother to change the cup. The cup was boiling hot while they served the tea to us. And forget the taste; it was my worst tea ever.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: L-7, Connaught Cir, Block L, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Ravinder Singh
Review: This was my first time at Wagh Bakri....apart from atmosphere everything else was not  up to the standard set by Wagh Bakri, Ordered Adrak Chai but didn't have any taste or smell of adrak. Service was also slow for me at that there was not so much crowd that for 1 chocolate muffin which i Ordered separately later, i have to wait for more than 20 minutes to have it there.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: L-7, Connaught Cir, Block L, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Ravish Kumar
Review:
Service is okay but we had to ask staff to clear the tables. Multiple tables had used crockery.
F&B is very average. We gad adrak chai but couldn't taste adrak at all. Vada pav had very small vada but tasted nice.
Decent value for money though for shareable combos.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: L-7, Connaught Cir, Block L, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Rohit
Review:
Looking for a great Masala Tea? A wonderful setup(opens till 23:30) for anyone seeking a peaceful and enjoyable tea experience. Whether you're a tea aficionado, a cookie/cake lover, or simply looking for a comfortable spot to relax with your tea, this cafe has it all. ☕️
The staff is very friendly and always happy to serve you. The place is a little expensive but the overall experience was great. I'll definitely be returning to this place whenever I need a comforting retreat.

Address: H 5/16, Block H 6, Malviya Nagar, Delhi, New Delhi
Author: Khushi Rai
Review: Amazing range of teas available, best tea café in Malviya Nagar. Enjoyed Hibiscus Iced Tea and Dark chocolate donut. They also have healthy and gluten free menu available.

Name: Farsi Tea House
Address: H 5/16, Block H 6, Malviya Nagar, Delhi, New Delhi
Author: Sibtain Hyder

Name: Farsi Tea House
Address: H 5/16, Block H 6, Malviya Nagar, Delhi, New Delhi

Review: yesterday we reach this café and order normal masala tea...test was awesome like 'apni wali chai'.

Name: Farsi Tea House
Address: H 5/16, Block H 6, Malviya Nagar, Delhi, New Delhi
Author: Ana Haider
Review: Had Turkish hazelnut cold coffee…amazing taste…perfect for caffeine lovers😋

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: SDA Market, C-21, Safdarjung Development Area, Hauz Khas
Author: Aiswarjya Mahapatra
Review: A tea lounge with a variety of teas and snacks available for both consumption and purchase. Had Masala Tea with a friend and found it definitely worth a try. Good place to relax, have a cup of tea and chat with friends. The Kahwa and Sulemani Tea are also options worth trying.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: SDA Market, C-21, Safdarjung Development Area, Hauz Khas
Author: Food Date
Review:
The Wagh bakri lounge was quite small and not hygienic.
We had tea and normal maggi for our daughter. The ginger tea is costing Rs 190. Unfortunately while having tea there was a hair in it, which went in my mouth and spoilt the entire experience & mood and taste. Ridiculous. Then the maggi was plain and not good and yet again there was a hair in the maggi too. Overall experience and mood and taste went for a toss. How can a lounge be  servicing like this???

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: SDA Market, C-21, Safdarjung Development Area, Hauz Khas
Author: Caveat Emptor
Review:
Location - 5:5
Parking - 5/5
Staff - 5/5
Cleanliness - 5/5
COVID precaution - 2/5 ( Sanitizer and temperature check is only at the front door while back door is also operational.
2. Social distancing between tables is not being followed properly )
Quality - 4/5 ( it was a decent sandwich for the price and tea was good )
Quantity - 5/5 ( Fair enough for the price )
Menu - 3/5 ( No desserts in the menu and samosa was not available at the moment )
Value for money - 4/5
Payment - paytm is also available
- There are plenty cafes in that market but wagh bakri is ruling
- COVID precautions need to be implemented strictly specially with social distancing on tables which was was not being implemented at all.
- if menu is limited then stock must be available because the menu is already small so it leaves you with really one or two options.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: SDA Market, C-21, Safdarjung Development Area, Hauz Khas
Author: Alankar Singh
Review: Lots of Tea options! Ice tea, hot tea, Green tea along with snacks and quick bites like biscuits. Very simple place. Small and peaceful tea lounge. Also you can purchase tea bags and tea packs here of different quantity and flavours. If you are chai lover. A good place to visit and enjoy various tea options.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: SDA Market, C-21, Safdarjung Development Area, Hauz Khas
Author: sheifaly suri
Review: Great relaxing place, catching up with old freinds or just want to have some good Indian Tea - this is it !! Wagh Bakri is one of the famous India Tea leaves brand. It's been exported overseas as well. I was surprised to see the Tea Lounge. In Delhi one can find these in almost every market area. The tea is good , india style , made with fresh milk. The place offers Green Tea , Drinks options as well. We went with our family while walking in the area just to relax and enjoy some coffee or Tea. If you are visiting India , I would recommend trying this out to get an actual flavour of the Indian Tea. The person in charge if quite helpful with you to decide on which options to pick. He even explained to us what might be offered as complemantry. We took tea in earthern glass, which is actually an authentic way to drink Tea in few places.

Name: Lord William Tea Lounge - Hotel Royal Plaza
Address: Shangri Las Eros Hotel The Royal Plaza, No.19, Hotel The Royal Plaza, Ashoka Rd, New Delhi
Author: Barun Ghosh
Review:
If you want brightly coloured interiors to cheer you up to face the day, then this is the place to come.
Very little availability of patisserie and the ones on display weren’t appetising at all, staff is courteous and served us what we wanted without too much delay.
Space is severely constrained at this coffee shop as its seating space is somewhat shared by the main lobby waiting area of the hotel.
However, the high ceilings and brightly coloured European motifs and marble statues staring down at you at every angle gives you a sense of space and exotica.
Crockery used is nice and colourful too and the air conditioning being part of the hotel was working fine, they have an outdoor area too adjoining the hotel pool with parasols.

Name: Lord William Tea Lounge - Hotel Royal Plaza
Address: Shangri Las Eros Hotel The Royal Plaza, No.19, Hotel The Royal Plaza, Ashoka Rd, New Delhi
Author: Rajeev Gopal
Review:
Decent lounge; let down by staff and service. Staff is drowsy. Lackluster. No energy. No smile. No interest. Music is a bit too loud. Restricted menu.

Service was a disaster. A cappuccino and a tea was ordered. The server brought two cappuccinos. One of the cup had lipstick marks on it. When this was pointed out, the lady claimed it was for someone else and took it away!!

At this point you realize that there is no point in expecting a sensible answer from the staff.

The place has potential though. Premium location. And loud cheerful decor. Can be turned into an advantage. Hopefully the management will respond.

Name: Lord William Tea Lounge - Hotel Royal Plaza
Address: Shangri Las Eros Hotel The Royal Plaza, No.19, Hotel The Royal Plaza, Ashoka Rd, New Delhi
Author: H H
Review: It's just the lobby of Royal Plaza Hotel. Gaudy,  uncomfortable, and void of people.  There was not a single other customer here nor in the adjoining pub at 7 pm on a weekend, which is exactly when a good place is full of patrons.

Address: Shangri Las Eros Hotel The Royal Plaza, No.19, Hotel The Royal Plaza, Ashoka Rd, New Delhi

Review: It was very nice place to have chai and snacks like pav bhaji, vada pav, dabeli, bhelpuri, garlic bread everything was really nice enjoyed the food here

Name: Super Chai - Cafe /Tea Lounge
Address: Ground Floor, N Block, Shop 1, Middle Circle, BMC House, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Anshita Yadav
Review: We visited this place for the first time. It's located in CP (Connaught Place), easy to reach with the help of google maps. We ordered coolers...they were delightful!!! Baked nachos and cheesy fries were well- cooked and well- served. If you are planning to visit this place, try their nachos. The ambience was also very well maintained and peaceful. The staff was very polite and well- behaved. It was indeed a good experience.

Name: Super Chai - Cafe /Tea Lounge
Address: Ground Floor, N Block, Shop 1, Middle Circle, BMC House, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Muskan Bansiwal
Review: This place is very photogenic and pretty. I tried their dessert which was very tasty. Must visit once.

Name: Super Chai - Cafe /Tea Lounge
Address: Ground Floor, N Block, Shop 1, Middle Circle, BMC House, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Kalpana Lohumi
Review:
This tiny adorable cafe will serve you the delectable chai and nibbles. The staff is courteous, and the atmosphere is comfortable. The videshi sandwich I got was quite wonderful. Being a complete desi, I enjoyed the pasta. They had experimented with a pasta sauce with an achaar flavour. Service was little slow but never mind.

The location will be ideal for after-work gatherings and banter.

Name: Super Chai - Cafe /Tea Lounge
Address: Ground Floor, N Block, Shop 1, Middle Circle, BMC House, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Gurnoor Kaur
Review: Atmosphere and ambience was really good. We ordered Chai, Bombay Masala Sandwich and Dabeli but the food quantity was very less and chai was also served in small cups. The service was also very slow.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: Plot No. 3, Dlf Mall Of India, Unit No. D-08B / D48A, Lower Ground, ZONE-1, Sector 18, Noida
Author: J Dehal
Review: Nice option for tea drinkers in mall or India. It’s newly constructed place.  Service level was prompt. Good sitting arrangement. Tea and snacks quality was good. You can buy raw tea packets from the restaurant itself. Prices are reasonable.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: Plot No. 3, Dlf Mall Of India, Unit No. D-08B / D48A, Lower Ground, ZONE-1, Sector 18, Noida
Author: DIPIN M.C.
Review:
Food was delicious. Classic tea and adrak tea was simply perfect. But the service was little lingering. I tried Mixed veg Pakora- was really tasty. Chilly Cheese Potato Fries - it will get a 4 star, taste is okay.
I will definitely recommend this for tea lovers who would also like to munch small snacks.
Only the service is bit sloppy from their side. We are bound to wait a bit longer than expected, like I had waited around 30 mins for my order to reach my table.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: Plot No. 3, Dlf Mall Of India, Unit No. D-08B / D48A, Lower Ground, ZONE-1, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Prakhar bansal
Review: Only interiors are good. Period. Super unhygienic place. Floors not cleaned at all. Hairs Food Dirt everywhere. Chairs are dirty. Food is stale. Management should really look into it.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge
Address: Plot No. 3, Dlf Mall Of India, Unit No. D-08B / D48A, Lower Ground, ZONE-1, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Rajat Khaneja
Review: A very nice tea lounge to have tea with your family or friends. Staff was extremely helpful and friendly. Service is quick and efficient. Really liked the atmosphere of this place. Taste of tea is authentic and very good. Really liked the place. Pricing is moderate. Recommend.

Name: Wagh Bakri Tea Lounge

Address: Shop No 13, Kailash Arcade, opposite mother dairy, Old Rajender Nagar market, Old Rajinder Nagar, Market, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: The Authentik Gourmand
Review:
A few days ago, I visited this quaint place in the Old Rajinder Nagar area as I was craving for some good Elaichi Chai and something to munch on with it.

Loved the cute ambience, the toothsome taste and satisfied with the quantity provided as well. This not so expensive price place is a perfect go to spot to spend some great time with amazing conversations and amazing meals on a budget.

My recommendation-

Cheese Vadapao, Rajwadi Elaichi Chai and mix pakoda.

Name: Kullhad Chai
Address: Shop No 13, Kailash Arcade, opposite mother dairy, Old Rajender Nagar market, Old Rajinder Nagar, Market, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: anand nagpal
Review: Here I tried their chai,south indian platter,north indian platter,maggi and pav bhaji.The taste and the portion size according to the price was great. Kullhad chai is famous for it's tea and the tea over here was totally worth the hype.

Name: Kullhad Chai
Address: Shop No 13, Kailash Arcade, opposite mother dairy, Old Rajender Nagar market, Old Rajinder Nagar, Market, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Rachit Jaiswal
Review: This is fabulous place for breakfast and snacks. Provide enormous varieties of food  such as pav bhaji, chilla, vada pav , pakoda, dosa and many more. I loved all the dishes i got served. There presentation is unique and taste could not be any better in any of these items. Within this price range, it is very affordable for teenagers.

Name: Kullhad Chai
Address: Shop No 13, Kailash Arcade, opposite mother dairy, Old Rajender Nagar market, Old Rajinder Nagar, Market, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Shivani
Review:
Weather demands for chai?
So i recently visited this outlet and had an amazing experience. It's a pocket friendly place, you can have variety of chai here. I loved the kesar chai must try if you're a chai lover.
We also had Bun samosa, Maratha Vada pav, Dal vada,  Plain dosa, Pav bhaji, Raw onion dosa , Grilled sandwich.
Raw onion dosa was delicious. Highly recommended, must visit.

Name: Kullhad Chai
Address: Shop No 13, Kailash Arcade, opposite mother dairy, Old Rajender Nagar market, Old Rajinder Nagar, Market, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: The Ultimate Foodie
Review:
Visited here recently with my friends Today.
Very pocket friendly place , yet you can try exciting menu.
Being chai lovers, we loved this place. They also have a huge variety of snacking options available, we tried the elaichi chai in kullhad, vegetable Maggie, Bun maska, Fusion pakore and the Kullhad Dosa ... which is served in a unique style. Overall, it's a great place to visit for all the chai lovers out there.

Name: Cha Bar
Address: N-81, Barakhamba Rd, Block N, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Shubhangi Kori
Review:
The blueberry bubble iced tea is amazing.
I tried peach fruit tea and it was equally good as well.
This place can be a bit loud, but the location is beautiful, inside a bookstore.
The price of the food is quite reasonable, although service seemed a bit slow. But definitely a place must visit.

Name: Cha Bar
Address: N-81, Barakhamba Rd, Block N, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Ashu Ranjeet
Review: An amazing combination of a cafe and bookstore in one place, where you can enjoy some delicious food and pick up a few books. Overall, the service was good, and the staff were polite. However, it can get crowded and noisy during weekends.

Name: Cha Bar
Address: N-81, Barakhamba Rd, Block N, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Kanchan Goswami
Review: This place has to be on your get together list because it's perfect for chit chats and small bites. I'm a regular here and can say their chip and dip is always a go to order cause it's so so good. They offer a wide variety of teas and good food and munching options. Please do make a booking if you're a large group cause it's always packed with crowds. I love this place all in all.

Name: Cha Bar
Address: N-81, Barakhamba Rd, Block N, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Sanskriti Agarwal
Review:
Have been to this place a couple of times.
Loved the ambience on the Oxford bookstore side, eating side is pretty simple.
The book collection is vast and trinkets are cute. Must visit for that alone.
Personally, the boba options are amazing. The raspberry, blueberry and strawberry ones in particular.
In food, I have had the veg snack platter and a pizza. Food is pretty below average. You may go here for boba and books. ✨

Author: Aaron Nyzil D'souza
Review:
Aaron's Final Rating is 4

Visited this place for the first time. It is located at Connaught Place Block N. The entrance will remind you of a school. It is a library cum restaurant, so don't get confused. All you bibliophiles and reading enthusiasts can grab a book, chai and some snacks. The staff is super busy coz in the evenings it's crowded. Food is very reasonable here, hence lot of young crowd. Overall, my experience at this hotel was good.

P. S. This place is noisy. So you can gossip without being overheard 😜

l would recommend this place.

Review:
Order :
Mango coconut boba 5/5
Mocha latte boba 4/5
Blueberry cube croissant 3/5
Creamy mushroom sandwich 5/5

The best part is you can customize the sweetness, amount of ice, toppings (boba or jellies) and the choice of milk.
I would really love to see some options in popping boba and a few more additions to the soda base flavours.

Name: Got Tea | Deer Park
Address: Ground Floor, B-6/2, Block B6, Humayunpur, Safdarjung Enclave, New Delhi
Author: isha gupta
Review:
Their bubble tea is absolutely delicious, with a perfect balance of sweetness and flavor. I especially loved the mocha latte . The croissants are flaky and buttery, and they pair perfectly with the tea.
The unique thing about this café is that they have photo kiosk and we can instantly get our pic printed.

Name: Got Tea | Deer Park
Address: Ground Floor, B-6/2, Block B6, Humayunpur, Safdarjung Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Ishpreet Thapar
Review: When I had my first bubble tea, it was at this place, and it was a delightful experience. The tea was refreshing, and the staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. It's definitely worth a visit if you're looking for a tasty treat and excellent service.

Address: Ground Floor, B-6/2, Block B6, Humayunpur, Safdarjung Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Rose Plays
Review:
Ambience is very cute. Didn't like the bubble tea too much, but the strawberry croffle was great.

Took photos from the photo booth, quite good, and it is free so.

Name: Got Tea | Deer Park
Address: Ground Floor, B-6/2, Block B6, Humayunpur, Safdarjung Enclave, New Delhi
Author: Suchi
Review:
Loved my recent visit to GotTea
We ordered a taro bubble tea and a grapefruit one, both were absolutely delicious.
Taro flavour usually gets messed up, but they did it so well, especially the sweetness it was well balanced.
Also ordered the strawberry croffle, and oh man I'm still dreaming about it. It was heavenly. Especially the strawberry cream cheese frosting with glazed strawberries, it was just perfect.
Also they've installed a photo printer, and tho me and my friends ove-used it but we created so many beautiful memories.

Address: Lobby Level, Shangri-La Eros New Delhi, 19, Ashoka Rd, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Meena Jain
Review: It’s a happy place to spend some good time here. Yes one can’t come often   It’s expensive but I guess it’s worth it. Cutting chai was good with all the masala’s. Loved the vada pao here. Green chutney was very spicy though   Gur para was not that great but namak pare were crispy n tasty. Its a cool place not stuffy and stiff

Name: Mister Chai
Address: Lobby Level, Shangri-La Eros New Delhi, 19, Ashoka Rd, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Varun Sahgal
Review:
A beautifully crafted tea restaurant on the ground floor of Hotel Shangri - La, New Delhi. The interiors are very comfortable and beautiful.  The staff is polite and caters to all your needs related to the cafe.

It has a decent menu which comes like a book which has options for both vegetarians and non vegetarians and ample sitting space.

A very nice menu with loads of Indian options and variety of tea. Though they have coffee also.

We ordered flat white, a cafe frappe and a biscoff cheesecake (eggless)

Flat white - 8/10
Cafe frappe - 6/10
Cheesecake. - 7/10

Review: I am a frequent visitor to this property. This time I have observed that there is a serious issue with the services and overall experience of this place. There is a construction going on In the property and they didn't even bothered to tell us at the entrance. They should tell the visitors or guests that there is construction going on and if you are expecting a peaceful time, then you may come next week or so.

Name: Purvanchal tea house
Address: T, 50, Muradi Rd, near jama masjid, Jamia, Batla House, Jamia Nagar, Okhla, New Delhi
Author: Mirza Adil
Review:
I recently visited your tea shop and I must say, I was thoroughly impressed with the experience. The quality of the tea was exceptional, undoubtedly among the best I've had. It was evident that only pure milk was used, without any dilution with water, resulting in a rich and flavorful cup every time.

Moreover, I was particularly impressed with the ambiance of the sitting area. Among all the shops nearby, yours undoubtedly boasts the best sitting area. It provided a comfortable and inviting environment, perfect for enjoying a cup of tea and unwinding.

What truly stood out to me, however, was the humility and warmth exuded by the staff. Their friendly demeanor and genuine hospitality were truly welcoming and added to the overall charm of the shop.

All these factors combined have left a lasting impression on me, and I find myself drawn to your shop time and time again. Keep up the fantastic work, and I look forward to my next visit.

Best regards,
Mirza
