Ambience is great and service is quick.
One point, regarding the Service Charges on bill. They add service charge by default and you have to request them to remove it.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Dlf Mall Of India, Pocket L, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Prateek Banerjee
Review:
Going to an only VEG restaurant was almost a deal breaker for me..!! Sometimes you have to let go of your pride and compromise, but let me confess this was one of my favourite dine in experience.

One of the most courteous staff I have encountered so far, a round of applause. Not only they quickly made us comfortable but for the kid they had special glasses to serve water (the kid immediately felt at ease, seeing the glass, now thats what makes it distinguishing) the queue was for almost 30min, seeing a kid with us, they offered us a seat at the bar table (no they don’t serve alcohol, but have a great variety of coolers) we tried the KambuCha needless to say it was refreshing.

We ordered a lot for two, but I guess it was worthwhile, started off with Springroll Salad and crispy tofu bao. The salad was full of flavours must try, for bao you have to develop taste (be little mindful that its not a pav or would taste like a bread, if you have not tasted it yet) bao was again amazing, I never expected the tofu to be this good (not a fan of tofu)..!!

Oh did I forget to mention about the 4 delicious chilli sauce?? What a kick each one has…!!!

There is more, afterwards we ordered mushroom crispy fries and sticky rice with broccoli and asparagus.

Mushroom fries were little too heavy (duhh!! What do you expect from a deep fried dish) this was a dish which was comparatively okaiishh..will I order again? Probably not.. anyhow the stickyrice dish was again top notch, the flavours still deep rooted inside me.

I have never tasted authentic Burmese food, but thanks to Burma Burma to atleast set the expectation high..!!

A must visit outlet..!!! Order whatever you like, I guess you will not be disappointed.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Dlf Mall Of India, Pocket L, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Shruti Kumar
Review:
Been to Burma Burma several times and have always loved this place. Our recent experience was great too and we loved it. Ordered the raw mango salad which is to die for.
Loved the uncle maung chilli noodles as well.
The coolers and bubble tea were really good too.

Went with 2 kids who don’t eat spicy. The chef recommended the creamy corn with Malabar parantha which was really delicious and kids love it. The place is kid friendly as they gave them special plates and cutlery to eat with. They also had high chairs for them.

The dessert was average. It was supposed to be cheesecake but it didn’t taste like cheese cake.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Dlf Mall Of India, Pocket L, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Naaz Khan
Review:
Burma Burma  is hands down one of my favorite vegetarian restaurants! I’ve visited a few times now, and every time, they absolutely nail it with their dishes. If you’re into vegetarian food or just love exploring new flavors, this place is a must-visit.

Let’s start with the highlights of my recent visit. First up was the Tea Leaf Salad—wow! It was an explosion of flavors and textures, with a perfect balance of tangy, crunchy, and refreshing. It’s the kind of dish that surprises you with every bite, making you appreciate the unique Burmese flavors that Burma Burma brings to the table.

Next, I tried the Lotus Stem Crispies, and they were nothing short of addictive. Light, crispy, and coated in a delicious sauce, they were the perfect appetizer to get things started. If you love a good crunch, you’ll absolutely enjoy these.

Then came the Creamed Corn with Paratha. The creamy, flavorful corn paired so well with the soft, flaky paratha—comfort food at its best! It’s one of those dishes that feels both indulgent and satisfying, without being too heavy. The flavors were subtle yet delicious, and I found myself savoring every bite.

To wash it all down, I opted for the Pomegranate Cooler. It was refreshing, not too sweet, and just what I needed to complement the rich flavors of the food. A perfect drink to keep things light and refreshing throughout the meal.

What I love most about Burma Burma is that they make vegetarian food exciting and absolutely delicious. Even as someone who’s not strictly vegetarian, I find myself craving their dishes and planning my next visit before I’ve even left. The quality and creativity of their dishes keep me coming back again and again.

The ambiance at the DLF Mall of India location is lovely too—cozy and inviting, with just the right amount of buzz to make it feel lively. The staff is always attentive and friendly, which just adds to the overall experience.

Honestly, Burma Burma is the kind of place where you can’t help but fall in love with vegetarian cuisine. Everything from the presentation to the flavors is top-notch. The only reason it’s not a full 5/5 is because I’m holding out for them to surprise me with something even more amazing next time (not that they haven’t already!).

In short, if you’re at DLF Mall of India, make sure to stop by Burma Burma. Whether you’re vegetarian or not, you’re in for a treat. Highly recommend!

Name: Chaayos Cafe - Community Center, Saket
Address: PVR Anupam Saket, Community Center, Ashok Vihar, Saket, New Delhi
Author: nairsahab
Review:
Chaayos @ Saket is conveniently located among popular outlets like Bikanewala, Domino's, McDonald's, Haldiram's, Hong’s Kitchen, KFC, Burger King and more. While the outlet is relatively compact, with only about 11 small tables and a mix of sofas and chairs, it offers a vibrant and colorful atmosphere.
As with other Chaayos locations, the standout feature here is their extensive variety of teas and snacks. Whether you're in the mood for Cutting chai, Masala chai, or lemongrass (you can opt for kullad) or one of their many iced tea flavors, there's something for every palate. I particularly enjoy their customizable Desi chai, which you can enhance with options like ginger, cardamom, cinnamon, lemongrass and many more.
In addition to their impressive tea selection, Chaayos even offers filter coffee and a range of snacks that pairs perfectly with any beverage up go for. The menu includes pakodas, palak patta chaat, parathas, poha, bun maska, sandwiches, samosa chat, and a few cakes. They also sell premix tea blends such as masala chai mix and adrak chai mix, ilaichi along with packaged teatime snacks like mathris n cookies.
Chaayos @ Saket is a refreshing spot for a tea break or chai pe charcha no matter you are solo, with ur family, friends or colleagues, offering both a great variety of teas and snacks. It's a top choice for tea enthusiasts, and I personally find it superior to Wag Barki Lounge, Saket, in terms of both affordability and variety. Highly recommended for tea lovers!

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Pacific Mall
Address: Lower Ground Floor, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Khayala, New Delhi
Author: Rajesh Punj
Review: Very poor service amd staff. Food low quality

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Pacific Mall
Address: Lower Ground Floor, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Khayala, New Delhi
Author: Karan Bir Singh
Review: I have insulted Twice with the girl staff of this outlet she is so rood and she don’t know who’s is standing in the q in the first or second she just want to insult to his Customere.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Pacific Mall
Address: Lower Ground Floor, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Khayala, New Delhi
Author: Food Date
Review: Chaayos at pacific mall, tagore garden is in basement one on the same floor as Spar. Its a small area. Seating is not comfortable. Chai is never upto the mark , no ginger whenever you ask for one. Its over done. Though they do change it but you lose your time. You can't relax. Spinach corn sandwich was decent. Bun samosa was not good. Samosa was burnt and hard. Tea wasn't good. We had it changed. Ddnt enjoy!

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Pacific Mall
Address: Lower Ground Floor, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Khayala, New Delhi
Author: Shivani Singh
Review:
Podi idli. Loved it. However they should increase the quantity of coconut chutney as it was less so idli felt dry.
Desi chai was perfect, poha also good - little oily, mint chutney bad taste.

Bun maska bhujiya good, vada pav bun ok

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Pacific Mall
Address: Lower Ground Floor, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Khayala, New Delhi
Author: Mohit Gautam
Review: Chaayos is an amazing outlet which is known for its chaai. Recently I visited chaayos @ Pacific mall to try some of its yummy food. Small cute outlet on the lower ground floor of Pacific mall with some seatings however much to my surprise the staff at the counter looked cold and rude. I went to have a Napoli sandwich however it wasn’t available so ordered a samosa chat. The snack was not hot and so did not like it that much.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Select CityWalk Mall, Saket, District Centre, S-25, Second Floor , A 3, New Delhi
Author: Aparna Gupta
Review:
Visited Burma Burma recently and had a delightful experience. The ambiance was warm and inviting, and the food was delicious with a great variety of flavors. The service was prompt and courteous. Overall, a wonderful dining experience that I would highly recommend. Just a few minor improvements are needed to make it perfect.

Rating: 4 out of 5 stars. 🌟🌟🌟🌟

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Select CityWalk Mall, Saket, District Centre, S-25, Second Floor , A 3, New Delhi
Author: Vikram Singh
Review: One of the best restaurants to go to for Khowsuey. Amazing sticky rice with mushrooms wrapped in Banana Leaves, and steamed Bao with mock meat. Do not miss the Burmese desserts.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Select CityWalk Mall, Saket, District Centre, S-25, Second Floor , A 3, New Delhi
Author: Akash Gogri
Review:
If you are a fan of Burmese cuisine and crave tasty food, Burma Burma should definitely be on your list. The restaurant serves super delicious, good quality food, and the best part is their consistency – each dish tastes just as amazing as the last time you had it. Here are some of my favorites and recommendations:
1. Raw Mango Salad
2. "Wa" Potato
3. Burma Burma "Oh No Khow Suey"
4. Jasmine Rice
5. Rangoon Vegetable Curry
6. Lemongrass Curry

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Select CityWalk Mall, Saket, District Centre, S-25, Second Floor , A 3, New Delhi
Author: mary thomas
Review: Amazing food experience. We ordered the spring roll salad, rice dumplings and khao suey. The food was fresh, and flavorsome, the spring rolls were crunchy. Khao suey was fragrant and tasty. Service was attentive. Overall, a good experience, will definitely visit again to taste more of the menu.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Select CityWalk Mall, Saket, District Centre, S-25, Second Floor , A 3, New Delhi
Author: mutton legs !!
Review: This place is amazing!! I went there first time with my family back in 2019!! AND THE QUALITY IF NOT UNCHANGED, IS EVEN BETTER. Everything about this place is perfect! The ambience, the taste, the portions, the prices. I also really love that this is a veg restaurant meaning there are more options for vegetarians who want to try out pan- asian cuisine. The staff was also very caring, they served us all the food and kept making sure it was upto our taste. And this is a very irrelevant point for foodies out there but the design of the menu cards is immaculate! my graphic designer heart was so happy seeing them! Props to whoever that designed the menu

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Connaught Place
Address: F-14/15, Rajiv Chowk, Block F, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Saswata Chakraborty
Review:
Chayoos, located in Connaught Place, is a delightful spot for tea lovers and anyone seeking a cozy escape in the bustling heart of Delhi. From the moment you walk in, the warm ambiance sets the tone for a relaxing experience. The interior is tastefully decorated, blending a modern vibe with traditional elements, making it a perfect place to unwind with friends or even spend some quiet time alone.

The tea selection is impressive, offering a wide variety of flavors that cater to different tastes and preferences. Whether you prefer classic masala chai, herbal infusions, or something more experimental, Chayoos has it all. The food menu complements the drinks well, with delicious options ranging from light snacks to wholesome meals. The paneer wraps and veggie sandwiches, in particular, are a must-try.
Only downside is it's ridiculously expensive. A cup of tea costs 130+ taxes.

What truly stands out, however, is the hospitality. The staff at Chayoos is incredibly courteous, attentive, and welcoming, ensuring that every guest feels special. They are quick to offer suggestions based on your taste and are always willing to customize orders. This level of personalized service makes you feel valued and enhances the overall experience.

Overall, Chayoos at Connaught Place is a must-visit for anyone looking for a perfect blend of great food, tea, and exceptional service. It’s a refreshing oasis amid the city’s hustle and bustle.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Connaught Place
Address: F-14/15, Rajiv Chowk, Block F, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Michael Lees
Review: Quite a nice atmosphere but very expensive for a passable massala chai. A small cup, not a pot, will set you back 135 rupees. Guess this is the main tourist area, but that's ridiculous.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Connaught Place
Address: F-14/15, Rajiv Chowk, Block F, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Muskan Bansiwal
Review: Giving two stars for desi chai and bun maska only. Otherwise food quantity is horrible. Tasteless and overpriced. Atmosphere is nice. They have quite large seating in the area.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Connaught Place
Address: F-14/15, Rajiv Chowk, Block F, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: k c
Review:
Nice ambience. The entrance and staircase needs cleaning and maintenance. The tea tastes as good as yesterday. Ms Durgesh & Staff is polite and proactive.
Our compliments and best wishes
😊

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Connaught Place
Address: F-14/15, Rajiv Chowk, Block F, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Arpit Gupta
Review:
- Located on the 1st floor, it is a place with a nice ambience and atmosphere.
- Seatings are separate but the place feels a little congested especially when there is a rush. We went on a weekday, yet there were so many people around that sometimes you were stuck.
- Food is good. Tea is awesome. Coffee is not at all good. We had Regular tea and kahwa, with matar kulcha and pav bhaji. The taste was good and the tea had its flavour.
- Since there are so many options available, it would be really better to have either a description in the menu or some staff to explain regarding the items.
- Apart from that, there are ample seats available.
Overall, a nice ambient place to be at with your friends or family to enjoy a good time. If you're going on a weekend, you might expect a rush at this place.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Janakpuri
Address: B3/172, Dharam Marg, Block B3, Janakpuri, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Preet Mahajan
Review:
"I absolutely love spending time at cafes, especially when it's raining, and Chaayos is perfect for that cozy, relaxing vibe. The Chaayos at Janakpuri is one of my favorites for several reasons. First, the service is incredibly quick, ensuring you never have to wait long for your order. The tea selection is fantastic, with a variety of flavors that cater to every palate. Their snacks are equally amazing, always fresh and delicious. On top of that, the customer service is top-notch. The staff is always friendly, attentive, and ready to assist. If you're looking for a great place to unwind with a cup of tea and some tasty snacks, especially on a rainy day, I highly recommend the Chaayos at Janakpuri."

They just have to provide parking which is not really feasible.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Janakpuri
Address: B3/172, Dharam Marg, Block B3, Janakpuri, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Ekta Garg
Review:
I was craving for Poha and gud wali chai for almost 10 days as I was out on a trip and couldnt find it anywhere.
As soon as I was back in Delhi, the first place I visited is Chaayos and loved eating Poha with chai - a total bliss, I must say.

Earlier I have tried their vada pao, mix pakora, etc which were tasty and tummy-filling.. So try them and enjoy eating the snacks.

Didnt take any pictures of the food, I was so hungry :P

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Janakpuri
Address: B3/172, Dharam Marg, Block B3, Janakpuri, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Priyanka chauhan
Review: I food and tea is all awesome.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Janakpuri
Address: B3/172, Dharam Marg, Block B3, Janakpuri, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Manish Bhutoria
Review:
Sorry didn't wanted to review so didn't clicked pictures..ordered desi cold coffee, mango icy slush, cheese chili toast and bun samosa.. Both the drinks were good but chili toast wasn't baked properly and samosa was not at all fresh which ruined the taste of the bun samosa. Had to threw the samosa after 2 bites..bcz the masala didn't tasted gr8 and the outer part was soggy ..

There was smell even at the outlet..

Sorry but didn't expected this after such prices.

Name: Chaayos Cafe at Janakpuri
Address: B3/172, Dharam Marg, Block B3, Janakpuri, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Amitoj Singh
Review: Would rate the Kulhad Chai a 6/10. I don't have ways to ponder that what spices they've put it in my chai that it straightaway hits the back of my throat. Requested a replacement...the staff is cooperative and the venue is kept plush. Would suggest for a quick breakfast. Anjali and Honey are the duo with whom you'd like talk to. (I'm talking bout the staff)

Name: Boba Bhai
Address: J4XG+636, Block A2, Block A, Rajouri Garden, Delhi

Review: very poor taste never ever go there and the staff does not how to talk to customer. The price r very high and quantity is very less . Very very poor response from the boba bhai staff NSP

Name: Cafe Delhi Heights
Address: Shop No. SH/2F/04-05, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Tagore Garden, Tilak Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Ankit Bhasin
Review:
1. "The ambiance was perfect, and the food was absolutely delicious! I can't wait to come back."
2. "Exceptional service and a fantastic menu! Every dish was a delight to the senses."
3. "I had a wonderful dining experience. The staff was friendly, and the flavors were outstanding!"
4. "From the appetizers to the dessert, everything was top-notch. Highly recommend this place!"
5. "A hidden gem! The creativity in the dishes and the attention to detail made our meal memorable."

Feel free to modify any of these lines to better fit your experience!

Name: Cafe Delhi Heights
Address: Shop No. SH/2F/04-05, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Tagore Garden, Tilak Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Sarthak Harishchandra
Review:
The ambience was good, and the staff were friendly and helpful. The service was quick, and the food was fresh, though the flavours could be slightly improved. The drinks were fine. Overall, it was a decent experience.
Ordered:
Electric Blue Lemonade [7/10]
Strawberry Lemonade [7.5/10]
Bang Bang Chicken-65 [7.5/10]
Pizza Al Greco [8/10]

Name: Cafe Delhi Heights
Address: Shop No. SH/2F/04-05, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Tagore Garden, Tilak Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Madhur Katarmal
Review: Cafe Delhi Heights, located on the top floor of Pacific Mall, is a fantastic dining spot with a cozy atmosphere and a varied menu. Their Pasta Alfredo is a creamy delight, perfectly cooked with just the right amount of seasoning. The ISBT Maggi is a unique take on a classic favorite, and it’s packed with flavors that make it stand out. Don’t miss the Veg Hot and Sour Soup—it’s a warming, spicy blend that’s both refreshing and comforting. Overall, a great place to enjoy tasty dishes with a lovely view. Highly recommend!

Name: Cafe Delhi Heights
Address: Shop No. SH/2F/04-05, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Tagore Garden, Tilak Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Rahul Barkh
Review: I visited Cafe Delhi Heights with my wife, and we had a pleasant experience. The ambiance was great, and we enjoyed the live music. We ordered kababs and fried fish, both of which were delicious. However, the service was a bit slow, and I found the prices to be a little on the costly side. Despite this, we had a good time overall and would recommend it for a relaxed dining experience.

Name: Cafe Delhi Heights
Address: Shop No. SH/2F/04-05, Pacific Mall, Najafgarh Rd, Tagore Garden, Tilak Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Mudit Tandon
Review: One of the best food and most filing quantity provided! A single dish is enough for one to fill up! The service is phenomenal, there is always someone to assist, answer and suggest and even connect to ensure we enjoy the food! The money is worth the taste as well!

Name: Sopa Kitchen (Old Dolma)
Address: Dolma House No.01, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Gurjeet Singh
Review: I am visiting here for the second time. I ordered the same Devil Momos and Veg Shabakley, and I simply loved it. These are the best momos! A must-try if you are visiting Majnu Ka Tila

Name: Sopa Kitchen (Old Dolma)
Address: Dolma House No.01, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Muskan Bansiwal
Review: Tried their Jhol momo but disappointed. Gravy was weird. Taste is not good. Atmosphere is a lil shady with no good lighting and congested seating area.

Name: Sopa Kitchen (Old Dolma)
Address: Dolma House No.01, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Sreejith Nair
Review: Dolma Cafe, though small, exudes charm. The fried rice is a highlight, bursting with flavor. However, the washroom could be improved. Despite this, the intimate atmosphere and friendly service make it a welcoming spot for a quick bite or leisurely meal away from the city's chaos.

Name: Sopa Kitchen (Old Dolma)
Address: Dolma House No.01, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Tijil Baranwal
Review:
Ordered:-
Veg. Thukpa (4/5)
Slightly light including good flavours.
Spl. Jhol Momos (1/5)
I don't know what was special about that!
It was too sour for me and the momos were below average as well, (maide ka bohot hi mota layer tha). I didn't like it personally at all.

Name: Sopa Kitchen (Old Dolma)
Address: Dolma House No.01, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: akshita gupta
Review:
Amazing place to have a full-filling meal with your date or friends. The food was amazing, pure authentic. The apple beer is a must try. The environment is cozy also you can get a nice seat near the window with the view of the street.
The price is crazy low according to the quality of food and even the quantity.
Problems- the sitting is tight not good for a group of more than 4 people.
- there is ac and fan both but still the temperature was not maintained it was too hot in this weather.

Name: Too Mikkii Tapas Cafe in Chanakyapuri
Address: Alexander M Kadakin Marg, Akhaura Block, Bapu dham, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Rad Rider
Review:
Lovely place to chill with tasty food options.
The food quality and portion size has definitely gone down since the last few years.
The mixed berry smoothie is superb and a must try. Pasta and pizza quality and quantity both has gone down however is decently average.

Name: Too Mikkii Tapas Cafe in Chanakyapuri
Address: Alexander M Kadakin Marg, Akhaura Block, Bapu dham, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Nikunj Ahuja (Phoenix)
Review:
Perfect cozy place to spend time with friends / special friend
Ambience is quiet and located at a high security area bang opposite taj hotel makes the perfect open air space to enjoy the pleasant sunsets sipping along great coffee and snacks. The tea they offer is also nice and they do have a kettle platter

Name: Too Mikkii Tapas Cafe in Chanakyapuri
Address: Alexander M Kadakin Marg, Akhaura Block, Bapu dham, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Kavya Bhola
Review: Loved the hot chocolate, the service by Rekha and other members of the staff. Asked them for French fries without salt and though it was a busy day, they managed to get my order well

Name: Too Mikkii Tapas Cafe in Chanakyapuri
Address: Alexander M Kadakin Marg, Akhaura Block, Bapu dham, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Nathan Capper
Review: Wonderful hidden gem in Dehli. Stumbled across this place and what a good turn that was. Spent time chatting and getting an impromptu Diwali blessing. The coffee is fantastic and there are some really cute playful cats that live there. Very beautiful place and the staff were so lovely. Namaste team thank you

Name: Too Mikkii Tapas Cafe in Chanakyapuri
Address: Alexander M Kadakin Marg, Akhaura Block, Bapu dham, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi
Author: Sahil Joshi
Review:
Check it out, this spot's got the most epic ambiance with vibes that'll blow your mind! Picture this: it's a totally open joint, right across from the iconic Taj, and it's all decked out with lush greenery all around. And guess what? The grub there is absolutely delish, and the best part is, it won't break the bank. Trust me, when those winter evenings roll in, this place becomes the ultimate go-to spot!

There are few cats and dogs which resides at this place so if ur really scared of them - plz beware

Name: Mr. Choy
Address: 15, opposite Blue Door Cafe, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Melitta Stark
Review:
Great Experience!!

This beautiful place is in the heart of Khan Market. It has an exceptional decor and really tasty food too. The service was good.

All in all A1 place. It's a must visit for people who love Asian cuisine.

Name: Mr. Choy
Address: 15, opposite Blue Door Cafe, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Nikhil Hajirnis
Review: The first thing that struck me about Mr Choy is pleasant ambience, not noisy, good for a couple or family. The variety of sushi offered is its other stand out quality. While most patrons were going for dim sums & sizzlers I chose Bamboo shoot prawns and the manileno rice preparation which was really tasty. The prawn temaki sushi is a conical shaped sushi and is a filling portion. But the winner for me was the drink Japanese Dragon…if you don’t mind a little sweet cocktail I would suggest you to go for it. For vegetarians the mushroom dim sums and lafeng will make for a sumptuous offering.

Name: Mr. Choy
Address: 15, opposite Blue Door Cafe, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Jonas Ax
Review:
Been meaning to jump in here for while now and I'm sad I didn't do it sooner! Came in for a late afternoon Sunday chill, ordered a couple of Bacardi Mojito's and an Aperol Fizz (Spritz misnomer?), both which was superb. After a while we got the Kung Pow Chicken, perfect amount of spice and flavour.

All in all loved it, will be back for sure.

Name: Mr. Choy
Address: 15, opposite Blue Door Cafe, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Chiranjeev Maini
Review:
Delicious food. I heard and read about this place through some of my foodie friends and trust me I made the right choice by listening to them.
The food was full of flavour and taste.
We ordered assorted dim sums (veg), some crispy rolls filled with duck, fried rice with crispy bacons and beer 🍺
Everything was excellent. The best was fried rice with crispy bacon.
The restaurant has a cozy space and can easily sit 60-70 guests (on multiple tables) at one time. You might end up waiting for a while during weekends.
The kitchen is right next door and u will get a lot of sumptuous fragrances while waiting for or eating food.
Staff is welcoming and friendly.
Good place to enjoy authentic ambiance Vietnamese food.
Shall visit again 😊

Name: Mr. Choy
Address: 15, opposite Blue Door Cafe, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Namrata
Review:
Pros:
FORTUNE COOKIE as complimentary dessert surprise.
Authentic food with right flavors.
Great taste.
Perfect dimsums.
Soothing ambience.

Cons:
NO SUSHI

Terrace seating has bar seats and one huge sofa (for group bookings usually).
The ambience is inspired by variety of Japanese stuff like cherry blossoms and Japanese symbols on wooden furniture. It's good but a little chaotic.

Service is warm, welcoming, and suggestive.

Name: AMA Cafe
Address: House no. 6, 1st Floor, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Saurabh Chandra
Review: We arrived at the cafe around 1:30 PM and had to wait nearly two hours for a table. Once seated, we tried a few items. The mix fruit pancakes were definitely the best, fluffy and served with fresh fruit, a perfect balance of flavors. The 4 cheese pizza and the croissant were both just okay, nothing special. Overall, the long wait was a bit much for a meal that had its highs and lows. The pancakes were a standout, but the rest could have been better.

Name: AMA Cafe
Address: House no. 6, 1st Floor, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Yateesh Yadav
Review: I recently visited Ama Cafe and I must say, the experience was wonderful! The food was incredibly delicious every dish I tried was full of flavor & cooked to perfection. The ambience of the cafe is calm and cozy, making it the perfect place to relax and unwind. Highly recommend visiting if you're looking for great food and a peaceful vibe!

Name: AMA Cafe
Address: House no. 6, 1st Floor, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Manya Kalra
Review: AMA Cafe is one of my all time favorites. The ambience of this place is just something else. It's cozy and warm. Usually there is some waiting time and if you're lucky, you may find a seat as and when you arrive. The service is great. Talking about the food, I would suggest you to try their Himalayan hot dalle chilli cheese fries, mushroom melt open sandwich, pasta, hazelnut frappe, burger and the RGB pizza. Almost everything tastes delicious. Do try when in Majnu ka Tilla.

Name: AMA Cafe
Address: House no. 6, 1st Floor, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Shivam Kumar
Review:
Worth the hype☑️, one of the best cafe in delhi.
The food is quite good, especially the Italian cuisine.
Pancakes, coffees and croissants are recommended.

Quality & Quantity - (4.5/5)
Taste - (4/5)
Aesthetics - (4/5)
Ambience - (4/5)

If going on the weekend you might need to wait for 45 min - 1 hr, but in the meantime you can explore the market.

Name: AMA Cafe
Address: House no. 6, 1st Floor, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, New Delhi
Author: gaurav gupta
Review:
Best place in and around Delhi for a cafe like experience without liquor ..the breads and breakfast impetuous were delicious , the cheese cakes were mouth watering and the winner for us was the cafe latte hot.

We loved the ambience and vibe of the place.

It’s a must visit place for a lazy afternoon or brunch meal

Name: Yum Yum Cha Khan Market
Address: First Floor, 69, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Shreya Das
Review: The food is nice. Taste and presentation are perfect. But when they serve sizzlers, people start coughing and sneezing, which can be managed with a good suction system. The atmosphere is good. Sitting should be more spacious. I feel it's a bit tightly packed. Still charges service charge.

Name: Yum Yum Cha Khan Market
Address: First Floor, 69, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Devendra Mishra
Review:
Delicious pan asian food.
Some waiting, especially on the weekends.
Its entrance is from the back street, and is situated on the first floor. Around 20 slightly tall stairs need to be covered. Elderly need to be aware.
Space is at a premium inside, so chairs are a bit uncomfortable and seating is a bit cramped. Even the table gets cluttered if you order a bit too much.
Portion sizes are good, prices on the higher side.

Name: Yum Yum Cha Khan Market
Address: First Floor, 69, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Amreen Randhawa
Review:
Had a wonderful dining experience at Yum Yum Cha earlier this week! It was 3 of us and we ordered the following:

Boba Tea (Original Flavour)- absolutely refreshing and so delicious!

Veg Sushi Platter- Good.

Non Veg Sushi Platter- Crab Sushi was the highlight, overall great.

(Note- you can choose any 3 varieties to make a Sushi platter)

Sashimi- Not something I usually have, but it was yum.

Thai Green Curry with Jasmine Rice- Some of the best I've had at any restaurant!

Mango flavoured mochi- Truly authentic!

Considering it's Pan Asian, the prices are on the costlier side. However, the food is excellent and justifies the price! This place is surely a must visit spot.

Name: Yum Yum Cha Khan Market
Address: First Floor, 69, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Rasna Kame
Review:
Yum Yum Cha never fails to disappoint!
Love their baos, crispy mushroom being a favourite even for us hardcore non vegetarians, their variety of sushi, the bulgogi! Another item we look forward to are the variety of dips and sauces - enjoy trying them out while waiting for the food.
Wish they get an alcohol licence too!

Name: Yum Yum Cha Khan Market
Address: First Floor, 69, Khan Market, Rabindra Nagar, New Delhi
Author: skhanna
Review: Just visited this exceptional Asian Restaurant the service is amazing with friendly and attentive staff ensuring a delightful experience. The sushi and dim sum are especially outstanding, making it a must visit for food lovers. The ambiance is vibrant and inviting with its colour full decor. A must visit Aisan Cusine restaurant

Name: Open House Cafe
Address: C Block, C 37, Block C, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: prateek tarafdar
Review:
I recently visited this cafe. The ambience and the quality of the food was good.
However the staff was a bit slow in taking orders and serving food.
The cafe also adds a mandatory service charge to the bill. I believe that tipping or paying a service charge should be at the discretion of the customer based on the quality of service received. Being forced to pay it, takes away the overall experience.
Also the music is too loud not soothing at all.

Name: Open House Cafe
Address: C Block, C 37, Block C, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Gagan Deep
Review: "Open House in CP is an amazing spot! They serve both veg and non-veg food, and everything was delicious. The drinks were great, and the sheesha added a fun touch to the experience. The ambience is beautiful and perfect for a relaxed evening out. Highly recommend if you're in Connaught Place!"

Name: Open House Cafe
Address: C Block, C 37, Block C, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Prachi Verma
Review: Visited this gem in CP recently, and I must say, the ambiance and service were top-notch. The fancy drinks were a visual treat and tasted just as good. But the highlight was the peppy paneer pizza – simply the best I've had! A must-visit spot for anyone in the area.

Name: Open House Cafe
Address: C Block, C 37, Block C, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Akash Chhetri
Review:
Took my colleagues here for an afternoon. We were 8 people and probably the highest paying table that day but the amount of times we had to call out the service staff just to ask for the menu or get them to take our orders left a really bad impression.

Ordered the drinks and I had to ask 4 times to two different servers just to get beer mugs. What kind of cafe charges upwards of 600 for a pint and doesn't even bother serving the beer in mugs.

The arrabiata fires were nice and so were the crispy corn. The smoked bbq wings felt slathered in very sweet ketchup and some remnant of bbq sauce. Defenitely not smokey. White sauce paste was not up to the mark either.

Passionfruit ginger ale was more of a grapefruit and very sweet ade with zero hints of ginger.

Overall not a good food experience with hiked up prices and average one dimensional flavours.

Add in the rude waiters with their "compulsary" service charge for an extremely shitty service leaves me with no other choice but to ask people to stay away.

Name: Open House Cafe
Address: C Block, C 37, Block C, Connaught Place, New Delhi
Author: Neelu Prajapati
Review:
First of all, I start with Negative review.
Service and waiters are pathetic. Very rude and they bought all the food order all together for 2 people, when we asked why you brought all order together, it will be cold, then he got rude and said "You should have said before".
Secondly, food and ambience is really nice and worth. Nice place to spend the time and chill.

Name: The chai story
Address: H5GV+FV9, Block G, Sarojini Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Akansha Jain
Review: So me and my sister was tired by sarojini shopping and went to this place and ordered veggie delight pizza 🍕 , iced tea and masala lemonade. Pizza and lemonade was really nice in taste but the iced tea was not good in taste . Service was quite late . Ambience is nice .

Name: The chai story
Address: H5GV+FV9, Block G, Sarojini Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Divakar Deepu
Review: I felt it was a waste of time or money, nothing had any taste. It would have been better to eat chole-bhature from a street vendor.

Name: The chai story
Address: H5GV+FV9, Block G, Sarojini Nagar, New Delhi
Author: VINAY RAJORA
Review:
Visited the chai story today and i found this is an cool place, not much decoration, lights and interior designing. Simple and beautiful place with some beautiful paintings and wall decoration.

The chai story serves you best taste, quality. You can get different kinds of chai, coffee, cold coffee, shakes , moitos and snacks like fries , sandwiches, wrappers, noodles etc.

Enough sitting place , simple chair sitting. Well maintained and clean area. Atmosphere is amazing with soft light music. No bed smell.

Visit and enjoy your chai with loved ones and make beautiful memories as they said “every chai has its story”.

Name: The chai story
Address: H5GV+FV9, Block G, Sarojini Nagar, New Delhi
Author: Priya Chauhan
Review: The food is decent with a pleasant and calm environment of the restaurant, you can definitely have a meal here after a long shopping session at sarojni nagar.

Name: The chai story
Address: H5GV+FV9, Block G, Sarojini Nagar, New Delhi
Author: FoodRavel
Review:
I visited this place seeking relief from the heat, hoping to enjoy something really cold. I ordered a cold coffee, specifically a mocha, and requested it to be extra chilled with less sugar and a strong coffee flavor. They delivered exactly what I asked for, which was great. I also asked if they could fill my water bottle, and they did, though I wished they could have provided cold water instead.

I spent a relaxing half hour at the café, enjoying my cold coffee and the time spent there. The service met my needs, and the environment provided a welcome respite from the heat. Overall, it was a satisfying visit.

Name: YouMee DLF Phase 3
Address: Level 3, Ambience Mall, Golf Dr, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Divya Misra
Review: Its emphasis on fresh, seasonal ingredients, simplicity and minimalism, and beautiful presentation make it a truly special dining experience. Whether you are trying Japanese cuisine for the first time or are a seasoned fan, there is always something new and exciting to discover. has vegetarian options as well which were also good also offer variety of drinks

Name: YouMee DLF Phase 3
Address: Level 3, Ambience Mall, Golf Dr, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Bilal Hasan
Review:
Always great food. I love Dimsum at this place. Must try Chilly Oil Prawn Wonton. It can feel pricy but it's fine since they maintain their food quality.

Service isn't good here, staff doesn't pour water and takes forever to bring food after ordering.

Name: YouMee DLF Phase 3
Address: Level 3, Ambience Mall, Golf Dr, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Rudransh RC
Review: If you're craving authentic Japanese or Asian food, YouMee is the place to go. The food and ambiance are top-notch, making it a delightful dining experience. The only downside was the seating arrangement, which could be improved. Other than that, I really enjoyed my time there and would recommend it for a tasty meal!

Name: YouMee DLF Phase 3
Address: Level 3, Ambience Mall, Golf Dr, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Shivanshu Aggarwal
Review: The food was undisputedly very good! We ordered the stone bowl and were not disappointed even though the choice of black pepper sauce was unavailable. The demeanor of the server was a bit weird even though he was not rude, but i felt a little condescension in his attitude. We went in May and the cooling was just about adequate in the outside section, it could have been better. The second item we ordered was the Chicken Ramen which was below average tasting. We also wanted to try the Salmon Avocado rolls but found them to be unreasonably expensive. Overall the food was the best part particularly the Stone bowl.

Name: YouMee DLF Phase 3
Address: Level 3, Ambience Mall, Golf Dr, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Soumya Jain
Review:
The food was prepared very authentically, had a large variety of pan asian delicacies. Would highly recommend their Drunken Noodles, they were spicy and delicious. Don't forget to check out their mango coconut bona tea.

The interiors are really pretty.
It was pretty busy for a Thursday afternoon, would recommend booking in advance.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Tower C, DLF Cyber Hub, Shop No - 6 Ground Floor, DLF Cyber City, DLF Phase 3, Gurugram
Author: Marci Fradkin
Review: Strongly recommend making a reservation. I have gone to them all over India and I’m still is one of my favorite restaurants. The flavors are so incredible so fresh and surprise me every time the drinks are creative and yummy and very refreshing. I love this place so much I wish they would open one in the United States, my friend was with me and as we were leaving I said pretty good for a vegetarian place. She goes oh my God I didn’t even think about it. It was so delicious.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Tower C, DLF Cyber Hub, Shop No - 6 Ground Floor, DLF Cyber City, DLF Phase 3, Gurugram
Author: Amit B
Review: Great place to try Burmese food. Better to read about the dishes in reviews before going as for first timers it could be very confusing when looking at the menu. Our server explained each dish n it's history with extensive detail.  Samosa soup was awesome. Other dishes were also great n different in taste. All ingredients, veggies, spices etc tasted very well n fresh. Though it was jam packed being Sat night the service was very quick. Great pure veg  place. They don't reserve over phone in advance. On weekends one can see wait time of 20-30 mins.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Tower C, DLF Cyber Hub, Shop No - 6 Ground Floor, DLF Cyber City, DLF Phase 3, Gurugram
Author: Prakkash Manohar
Review: Great food. Great ambience. There was a waiting time of 1 hour when we went at 8 pm on Sunday. They don't take reservations on weekends. I definitely recommend trying the Crunchy Tofu steamed bun or the bao. It was delicious. Noodles were good and had a refreshing flavour with the different sauces and condiments. Desserts were great as well. Staff was helpful and polite.

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Tower C, DLF Cyber Hub, Shop No - 6 Ground Floor, DLF Cyber City, DLF Phase 3, Gurugram
Author: Shashwat Gupta
Review: This establishment is an essential destination for aficionados of Southeast Asian cuisine. The appetizers, soups, and main courses were all exceptional, but the desserts – they shone like gold in a sea of sand. Highly recommended!

Name: Burma Burma Restaurant & Tea Room
Address: Tower C, DLF Cyber Hub, Shop No - 6 Ground Floor, DLF Cyber City, DLF Phase 3, Gurugram
Author: Food Date
Review:
It’s a nice restaurant in DLF cyber hub. Delish good food and good service. Expensive dishes and less quantity though. No discounts or offers. We had a smoked spicy guava drink. Nice presentation and nice refreshing drink but less quantity. Then tried rice pancakes served with peanut chutney. 6 small pieces in rs 350. Tasty though. The bao buns are 140 a piece vegetarian but were soft and nice. Filling could have been better. Tried veg noodles which were again good but they could add more vegetables.
They give some condiments platter with peanuts, garlic, spring onions, Batista onions, dried chilli flakes, lemons. Then another platter with sauces like hibiscus sauce, tomato onion peanut sauce etc.
good presentation overall .

Name: The Bento Cafe
Address: 4th Floor, Dlf Mall Of India, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Anupam Singh
Review: The bento Cafe situated inside DLF mall noida offers a unique variety of food, their spicy udon noodles with spicy Thai curry 🍛 😋 is a bliss to have, the atmosphere is amazing, food is on point and service is heartwarming 😊, I had a great time, my overall satisfaction is 4 🌟.

Name: The Bento Cafe
Address: 4th Floor, Dlf Mall Of India, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Manal Amir
Review: We went to this place after doing some shopping. The sizzler attract us so we thought to try out that only. It was for 400+ which is costly as compared to the quantity. The sizzler had chicken manchurian, noodles and two spring rolls. Taste was good. Service was also good. FOLLOW MY FOOD UPDATES ON INSTAGRAM @KHAATIPEETIDILLI

Name: The Bento Cafe
Address: 4th Floor, Dlf Mall Of India, Sector 18, Noida
Author: saumya tripathi
Review:
Amazing taste and menu options.

We ordered hakka noodles and noodles sizzler combo which came with hakka noodles, chilly paneer,spring roll , some kimchi salad and french fries

Very good place to have Chinese food at the Mall of India

Name: The Bento Cafe
Address: 4th Floor, Dlf Mall Of India, Sector 18, Noida
Author: Rahul Negi
Review:
The is situated at DLF Mall of India’s food court. Service is good and food quality and quantity is good as well.
1. Veg. Tangra Chilli Garlic Noodles (cost only ₹135, it is spicy and delicious noodles.)

2. Veg. Bento Box Meal (This bento have a choice of noodles “Japanese Chilli Cashew Udon Noodles” (noodles were spicy and delish) & Mains “Thai Basil Curry” (a scrumptious curry) & “Thai Som Tum Salads” (refreshing). Optional “Veg Spring Rolls” (it wasn’t good) & Coke. This entire meal was delicious and very filling)
3. Honey Chilli Potatoes (It was delicious but slightly soggy rather then crispy potatoes)

Name: The Bento Cafe
Address: 4th Floor, Dlf Mall Of India, Sector 18, Noida
Author: ankur meena
Review:
I wished to have some Thai food, so decided to give The bento cafe a try. It's located in the food court (top floor) of DLF Mall of India.

I tried it's veg bento box meal. It had Hakka noodles , served with red Thai curry and a complimentary Thai som tum salad.

Noodles were good, Thai curry was decent and surprisingly, salad was just fab.

#overall, pretty decent experience, nothing to write home about. Maybe I expected a bit more

Name: Fire & Bubble | Club & Restaurant | Huda Gymkhana Club
Address: Fire & Bubble Gymkhana club sector 29 Opposite to signature tower, Gurugram
Author: Bhavna Singh
Review: quality of the food is very good but takes so much time to bring only 2 glasses of sprite they took 10-12 minutes for 2 glasses of sprite but the food is delicious and atmosphere is also good

Name: Fire & Bubble | Club & Restaurant | Huda Gymkhana Club
Address: Fire & Bubble Gymkhana club sector 29 Opposite to signature tower, Gurugram
Author: Tripti Chaturvedi
Review:
Very good staff.
Food & services are appreciative.

Name: Fire & Bubble | Club & Restaurant | Huda Gymkhana Club
Address: Fire & Bubble Gymkhana club sector 29 Opposite to signature tower, Gurugram
Author: Vivek Dhatwalia
Review:
Such a nice place
For family gathering...

Name: Fire & Bubble | Club & Restaurant | Huda Gymkhana Club
Address: Fire & Bubble Gymkhana club sector 29 Opposite to signature tower, Gurugram
Author: Mukul Gera
Review: Amazing food and hospitality. Vivek serves food with heart and ambience is amazing. Must visit.

Name: Fire & Bubble | Club & Restaurant | Huda Gymkhana Club
Address: Fire & Bubble Gymkhana club sector 29 Opposite to signature tower, Gurugram
Author: Jitesh Jain
Review: Amazing place for parties. Food is very delicious and the service is of the top class. I went with my family and we had a great time. I had to go and thank the chef personally, food was that good. All and all an awesome place.

Name: Cafe legato
Address: UNIT 108, FIRST FLOOR Living Style Mall Jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola Vihar, near jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Allan Kapten
Review:
I had an amazing experience at Legato! The pizza was absolutely delicious, with a perfect blend of flavors and a delightful crust. The ambience was cozy and inviting, making it a perfect spot to relax and enjoy a meal.

Most importantly, the staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, going out of their way to ensure we had a great time. Shout out to Nida. I highly recommend Legato for anyone looking for great food and a warm atmosphere!

Name: Cafe legato
Address: UNIT 108, FIRST FLOOR Living Style Mall Jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola Vihar, near jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: PARAMVEER BHILWARA
Review:
Legato cafe is hardly 10 to 15 minutes away from metro station.We went for breakfast and all the items we tried tasted good.
Classic Ice cream blend coffee(without any additional flavour) was one of great choice. Coffee tasted differently but it kept me confused if it was coffee or orange juice but I liked it.
All the items we tried were delicious.  spend good quality time. Want to comeback here soon to spend good quality time with my friends.
Overall had good experience with food, service and atmosphere
Parking space available but availability mostly depends on peak hours.

Name: Cafe legato
Address: UNIT 108, FIRST FLOOR Living Style Mall Jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola Vihar, near jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Pradeep Mocherla
Review:
A cozy little place. Power packed with a vast variety Kitchen. Was very pleasantly surprised with the way so much was packed into such a small place.

The Ginger chai I was offered outside the menu was one for the ages. Very nice and I had it sugarless too as the milk was so pure and creamy.

Keep up the great work! All the best ✨✨✨

Name: Cafe legato
Address: UNIT 108, FIRST FLOOR Living Style Mall Jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola Vihar, near jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Anmol Agarwal
Review: Impeccable service, delicious food, polite staff/owner, beautiful ambience and good prices. We went as a family of 4 and the food was beautifully presented and was absolutely scrumptious. This place is 100% worth a try, you won't regret it. Many thanks to the Legato staff who were very generous and polite. Would definitely go again.

Name: Cafe legato
Address: UNIT 108, FIRST FLOOR Living Style Mall Jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola Vihar, near jasola, Pocket 6, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: ABHINAV
Review: This place is amazing. Legato is a perfect place for chilling out. I really like the atmosphere of this cafe. If you're looking for a place to relax in that area, this would be the best place to go. Food is amazing, Coffee is awesome. Owners are wonderful. Certainly my absolute favourite go-to spot as a local.

Name: The Belgian Waffle Co
Address: Shop No 103,1st Floor, District Center, V3S Mall, Veer Savarkar Block, Laxmi Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Wanderer
Review: I was never find of expensive store bought desserts, especially commercialized brands like this. But damn this is so good. My sister in law loves these, as does some of my wife's friends. I mooch off them.

Name: The Belgian Waffle Co
Address: Shop No 103,1st Floor, District Center, V3S Mall, Veer Savarkar Block, Laxmi Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Himanshi Thakur
Review: This is the fourth or fifth visit of mine. Ever since I first visited the taste of the waffles here got me hooked to it. They're totally worth it!.

Name: The Belgian Waffle Co
Address: Shop No 103,1st Floor, District Center, V3S Mall, Veer Savarkar Block, Laxmi Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Suraj
Review: Yesterday i visit Belgian waffle. And i tried their Triple cookie waffle & Banoffee sundae. This place is ideal place for waffle lovers. Friendly staff with fast service. I really loved it. Excellent presentation. Affordable price menu. Overall great experience. Highly recommended place.

Name: The Belgian Waffle Co
Address: Shop No 103,1st Floor, District Center, V3S Mall, Veer Savarkar Block, Laxmi Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Pulkit Grover
Review: It was a great experience had triple chocolate waffle and it was so fresh and delicious in taste. Cold coffee could have been better as it was filled with ice more than milk and coffee.

Name: The Belgian Waffle Co
Address: Shop No 103,1st Floor, District Center, V3S Mall, Veer Savarkar Block, Laxmi Nagar, New Delhi, Delhi
Author: Saaniya Jackson
Review: waffle was good just Maintain some hygiene.. ..they are making the waffles at front of me and the Chocolate Pipe they are Using was so sticky and old  .keep putting the knife in the Nutella jar and then spreading on the waffle . They are not making the waffle cooked.  Throwing uncooked in the dustbin ..the guy packaging the waffles also collecting cash  very rude not polite at all  , the other guy wash the milkshake jug just from  water , sink is so dirty  how u maintain hygiene by this not upto the mark not impressed.. a company giving these staff a well settled platform to work they are not Fully Trained at all ..

Name: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT Yamuna side New Sakya House 192, 193, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Active Media
Review: Double shot capachino was awesome as well as the peach tea, and Thai green curry was the best and must try dish, grilled sandwich was served with French Fries was good. Rest will visit again for Thai Green Curry which was awesome and had a authentic taste, just yummy. They have outside as well as indoor sitting options.

Name: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT Yamuna side New Sakya House 192, 193, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Dips Barua
Review:
This cafe, alongwith few others, are at the back side of Majnu Ka Tila or adjacent to the Yamuna River bed area. Loved the glass partition which provides ample light during daytime and gives a very cosy feeling. It was quite early in the morning when I visited and had the cafe and service to myself only ;) :D
It was submerged almost to the ceiling of the cafe during the 2023 Delhi flood.
I had a meat soupy noodles (quite a large bowl), a cup of hot coffee to begin with and one cold coffee to end with.

Name: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT Yamuna side New Sakya House 192, 193, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Ivan Emmanuel
Review:
You can definitely miss this place out if you're not familiar with MKT!!
One can find this cafe if they take the lane next to Tee Dee and go towards a basement all the way till the end

The cafe though really nice !! I like the vibes.
We had already had our lunch so this was just a Stopover for some desserts.
Mud cake was delicious!!! Nice and hot with ice cream on top !! Something about this was  just so addicting.
We also has the carrot and banana cake, they were alright in my opinion. Reasonable priced, staff is polite and i would recommend this place since this isn't crowded like the other eateries in the vicinity

Name: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT Yamuna side New Sakya House 192, 193, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Devesh Shah
Review:
I went there around 12 noon and it was nice to go there. Payment can be done via cash and online payment methods like - Phone pay, Paytm, G pay, etc. Nice taste of all the things Available in the reasonably pirces. You must go there to have your snacks mainly their momos and chinese foods. Also, the services are good and people are very polite here.

On the other hand, I'd must say that the interior was awesome and creative. I have mentioned many pictures of the same. Please review them for references.

Please follow my account to get more honest reviews for restaurants and places. Also, you can tell me where should I go to review the place/restaurants. I'll try my best to fulfill your request ASAP.(Free of cost)
Thanks,
Devesh Shah

Name: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: MILA CAFE & RESTAURANT Yamuna side New Sakya House 192, 193, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Kritika Vashistha
Review: Very nice place . Great food . Tried bubble tea and veg chowmien here , both were very nice

Name: Shake Square - Kamla Nagar
Address: No. 12 UB, Bungalow Road, Jawahar Nagar, Kamla Nagar, Delhi
Author: The Unusual Travel
Review:
Most affordable and pocket-friendly place to hangout in CP.

Shake Square, the legendary shakery since 1971, established in the heart of New Delhi who introduced their personalised flavoured milkshakes to its people of Delhi has become a cult in this city expanding its wings in various parts of India.

Name: Shake Square - Kamla Nagar
Address: No. 12 UB, Bungalow Road, Jawahar Nagar, Kamla Nagar, Delhi
Author: RAVINDRA PRATAP SINGH
Review:
quality of shake is normal.. after having shopping in kamla nagar one may visit this place... sitting is available here on the first floor but only 2 tables.. and on ground floor stools are there..
sometimes shakes here are tastes as cough syrup especially the butterscotch one...

prices are reasonable.

Name: Shake Square - Kamla Nagar
Address: No. 12 UB, Bungalow Road, Jawahar Nagar, Kamla Nagar, Delhi
Author: Aman Sah
Review: Shake square is a perfect place for quick bite with friends along with that the prices of various items like shakes, garlic bread are affordable and staffs are also cooperative. I surely recommend to have garlic bread and shake as it totally worth of it.

Name: Shake Square - Kamla Nagar
Address: No. 12 UB, Bungalow Road, Jawahar Nagar, Kamla Nagar, Delhi
Author: sumeet saraf
Review: They use inferior quality, i ordered through swiggy, milk was spoilt and taste disgusting, i raised a concern in swiggy and spoke to store aswell but no proper response provided

Name: Shake Square - Kamla Nagar
Address: No. 12 UB, Bungalow Road, Jawahar Nagar, Kamla Nagar, Delhi
Author: SHUBHAM KUMAR
Review:
This drink was really good but payment system is very poor...
They don't have online payment system...
So i faced little difficulty in paying the bill

Name: Cafe Dolma - Since 1984 | Majnu ka Tila
Address: 6/7 floor, Tsampa House Building, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: SHWETA JHA
Review: What a beautiful place 💕 Went to this cafe with my girl gang, it's a must visit for everyone going to majnu ka tilla. The decor, the ambience, it's just perfect. The staff is very friendly & very polite. We ordered Ema Datshi, Ting Mo, Veg peri peri pasta, Iced latte , cold coffee blend and cold coffee with ice cream. All the items were decent in taste. Overall a very pleasant experience.

Name: Cafe Dolma - Since 1984 | Majnu ka Tila
Address: 6/7 floor, Tsampa House Building, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Saurabh Chandra
Review:
Visited Cafe Dolma recently and while the ambience was fantastic, the food was quite underwhelming. I tried the coffee and Jhol momos, and both left much to be desired in terms of flavor and presentation. The coffee was average and the momos lacked the authentic punch I was hoping for.

That being said, the cafe truly shines with its beautiful and well-thought-out aesthetic. There are two indoor seating areas, one with a foosball table and gaming machines, and another near the bar, offering a cozy atmosphere. Additionally, there are two outdoor seating areas, including a rooftop with a stunning view of the Yamuna bridge, which adds a special touch to the overall experience.
If you’re looking for a spot with great vibes, Cafe Dolma is worth a visit. Just don't expect to be blown away by the food.

Name: Cafe Dolma - Since 1984 | Majnu ka Tila
Address: 6/7 floor, Tsampa House Building, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: anindita chakravarty bhattacharya
Review:
Go to this place for it’s ambience.
Food was average. The lemon chicken that we had ordered tasted stale.
Chicken noodles was colorful not tasteful.
The Manchow soup, lesser said the better.

The chicken burger was the winner among the lot. It tasted much better than the rest.

Name: Cafe Dolma - Since 1984 | Majnu ka Tila
Address: 6/7 floor, Tsampa House Building, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Anshu Shah
Review: The cafe was so awesome. There are many types of food varieties. The atmosphere was very nice and the view of the rooftop was very beautiful The best cafe to visit in Majnu ka Tila

Name: Cafe Dolma - Since 1984 | Majnu ka Tila
Address: 6/7 floor, Tsampa House Building, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Kirti Molpariya
Review: This cafe has amazing environment, I go here with my friend you must try their shakes I feel amazing to have but I really disappointed by noddles such a disgusting tast I never recommend noddles

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: Ambience Mall 3, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Pratz B
Review: Great ambiance love the quirky neons and the pink hello kitty vibe. The staff is polite and the service is quick. They have a good range of drinks and deserts. The food was good and it tasted great. My only negative feedback would be the the cheesecake was very average.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: Ambience Mall 3, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Techno wash
Review: Located on the 3rd floor of the Ambience Mall, an open sitting arrangement, alright of a casual dining. Vibrant place with a big menu, drinks, Boba Tea, Mochi, cakes, cookies & other bakery items. The food was great & flavorful. Loved the Ramen bowls. Big menu that includes small & large plates with sides. Gyoza Ramen & Katsu curry were superb. Prices are fair & quantity is sufficient. Well-made food is light on the stomach.  Mochi was delicious, the best I could find in India. Must try for the love of Japanese food.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: Ambience Mall 3, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Ishita Khurana
Review: I've always loved cute cafes, and Harajuku is one of them. We enjoyed baos and flat noodles.  The staff was friendly and recommended good food. Really enjoyed the food, Boba tea was okay, I prefer tapioca pearls, but the store bought Boba was fine. Overall, it is 4/5 recommended place to visit.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: Ambience Mall 3, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Shahzeb Khan
Review:
This Cute Japanese Themed Cafe in Ambience Mall is worth your time

Me and my sister Tried the Veg Naruto Ramen and Prawn Gyoza Both of which were good.

For Beverages We ordered 2 Jasmine Boba Teas Grapefruit and Strawberry Flavoured
Would Highly Recommend the Boba Tea
Plenty of Dessert options Do try some of them if you get the chance

Excellent Atmosphere and Decor and The service and the staff are highly professional
Kindly Note that the items are quite expensive so order wisely

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: Ambience Mall 3, Ambience Island, DLF Phase 3, Sector 24, Gurugram
Author: Priya Gupta
Review:
The only recommendation here would be Jiggly Nutella pancakes! The entire rating is for that 😅 #musttry

Open cafe with limited seating and no privacy and its in centre of the mall floor with no confined space.

Sushi was way overpriced and yet nowhere near or what was expected!

Name: Modi Mania cafe 24X7
Address: Shop no :- G25 A, PP TOWER, Netaji Subhash Place, Shakurpur, Delhi
Author: MOHIT ARORA
Review: The outlet, while small, offers decent dining choices. The food was tasty, and the prices were fair. However, the establishment appears a bit dated. Overall, it's a decent vegetarian option.

Name: Modi Mania cafe 24X7
Address: Shop no :- G25 A, PP TOWER, Netaji Subhash Place, Shakurpur, Delhi
Author: AYUSH JAIN
Review: Service was very bad and the quality of food was just okay not so good. Management is zero out of ten. Delay in service.

Name: Modi Mania cafe 24X7
Address: Shop no :- G25 A, PP TOWER, Netaji Subhash Place, Shakurpur, Delhi
Author: kartikay naswa
Review:
Delicious food at affordable prices serving 24/7 is such a blessing, especially for late-night cravings. Love to come here during nightouts.

Whether it's their amazing garlic bread, perfectly cooked pasta, delicious pizzas, or juicy burgers, everything on the menu is spot on.

North Indian dishes like dal makhani and garlic naan are absolutely divine.

Special shoutout to Pradeep from the management staff, who goes above and beyond to ensure warm hospitality.
............................................................................
Must order🍕🍔:- Dal Makhani, garlic naan, lachha prantha, Tandoori sandwich.

You can sit in Air conditioned dining or in fresh air with rotating cooling fans and speaker music 🎶

Name: Modi Mania cafe 24X7
Address: Shop no :- G25 A, PP TOWER, Netaji Subhash Place, Shakurpur, Delhi
Author: Nitish Kumar
Review: This is place is only running because it operates till late night. Ordered a wrap & burger and it was good for nothing

Name: Modi Mania cafe 24X7
Address: Shop no :- G25 A, PP TOWER, Netaji Subhash Place, Shakurpur, Delhi
Author: Rakhi Anand
Review: Best place to go for late night cravings. It's on main road netaji Subhash place(NSP). Tasty food. They have both open and closed sitting available. Whatever is ordered was great in taste.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: First Floor, Select Citywalk Mall, Saket District Centre, District Centre, Sector 6, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Sonali Bharti
Review: This café is a hidden gem with a cozy vibe. The decor is simple but stylish, with soft lighting and lots of plants that make it feel super welcoming. The coffee is great, and they have a nice selection of pastries and light snacks. It’s a perfect spot to chill, get some work done, or just hang out with friends. Definitely a go-to for a relaxed, aesthetic experience.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: First Floor, Select Citywalk Mall, Saket District Centre, District Centre, Sector 6, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Shreyansh Rathi
Review:
Pretty decent food considering the price point of this cafe. However, I firmly believe they can still improve on their dishes and especially the sitting. To have an open sort of sitting like a food court is not ideal with the price point they offer.

Overall good experience.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: First Floor, Select Citywalk Mall, Saket District Centre, District Centre, Sector 6, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Subhojeet Kar
Review: A must visit cafe in select city walk saket, we ordered one nutella jiggle pancake,one orange kaafir and one avacado spicy sushi. All of these costed us around 1700/- a little pricy i felt but the food was great and staffs were nice too... But if u think its a gated cafe then you might be wrong it's open like a food court in a mall and we waited for like 30 min for seat and 30 mins more for food. Overall a good experience, a must visit.

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: First Floor, Select Citywalk Mall, Saket District Centre, District Centre, Sector 6, Saket, New Delhi
Author: Kunal Singh
Review: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe in Select City Walk is a delightful spot for anyone craving Japanese-inspired treats. I tried their Jiggly Pancakes with Nutella, and they were absolutely amazing—fluffy, light, and perfectly balanced with the rich Nutella flavor. The presentation was beautiful, and the cafe's vibrant, quirky decor adds to the whole experience. A must-visit for dessert lovers!

Name: Harajuku Tokyo Cafe
Address: First Floor, Select Citywalk Mall, Saket District Centre, District Centre, Sector 6, Saket, New Delhi
Author: shaifali tyagi
Review: I went with my family for Lunch here and the food was really tasty and had authentic taste. Would love to visit here again. The drinks were really refreshing and the star was Brown sugar Bobba Tea 😍We ordered Yasai roll which so refreshing, the Ramen bowls were so flavourful and the Tiramisu jiggly pancake was melt in mouth. I have never tried such soft pancakes before.

Name: Laphing Noodle Corner
Address: 36/37, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Haqq se Foodie
Review: I brought 3 plates Laphing from here. The service was really slow and taste was not good at all. The sheet was thick and no flavour of garlic was there. Red chilli paste used, was very drying. I reached home within 20 mins of taking it away from here and the taste got ruined.

Name: Laphing Noodle Corner
Address: 36/37, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Our Hipster
Review:
Never try laping. Taste is too bad.
Momos are also average and the worst part is they don't have water...you will feel spicy then you have to purchase the water bottle also.

Pure lutere

Name: Laphing Noodle Corner
Address: 36/37, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Anshu Shah
Review: Nice place to try  laphing and bubble teas.

Name: Laphing Noodle Corner
Address: 36/37, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Uttam Saini
Review: Nice food for my first time 1 tried such food I don’t have anything to compare with but still it was amazing tasting momos and laughing point the staff was of friendly nature, low prices even impressed us

Name: Laphing Noodle Corner
Address: 36/37, New Aruna Colony, Majnu-ka-tilla, New Aruna Nagar, Delhi
Author: Kritika Joshi
Review: These guys serve the best laphing in Majnu ka Tilla. This dish is very spicy and one should definitely try this for once. There are different types of laphing. I suggest you to try dry laphing and wai wai laphing. It is a vegetarian dish.

Name: First Coffee
Address: G7PM+X85, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Varun Sahgal
Review:
A cosy coffee shop at the Pacific Mall, Jasola.  It’s at the entrance of the mall.

Ample sitting inside and also in open.  The menu is good and you have to stand at the counter to decide what you want to buy.

Limited food menu and as a cafe, it’s precooked so basically you get a re-heated item.

We ordered a wicked Oreo Mocha crumble and a java jolt chip cold frappes.

Mocha crumble - 7/10
Java jolt - 7/10

Name: First Coffee
Address: G7PM+X85, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Aakash Sharma
Review:
I ordered the house blend untamed espresso, and unfortunately, it was the worst coffee I've ever had. It was both bitter and sour, indicating a lack of refinement in their coffee blend. For a true coffee enthusiast, this establishment falls short.

It appears their target audience may be newcomers to coffee, who might accept anything labeled as coffee. The shakes they offer, heavily masked with flavors and sugar, hardly qualify as coffee.

I wouldn't recommend this place to anyone who genuinely appreciates a well-crafted cup.

Name: First Coffee
Address: G7PM+X85, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Komal Katyayan (Speech Therapist)
Review:
I recently visited the First Coffee, Jasola, and I was amazed with the overall experience. The handling doesn't seem rushed, and they gave attention to small details of my beverage. Moreover, the staff appeared interested and welcoming, which pushed the enjoyment of placing an order. Smiles were friendly, and the atmosphere felt warm and welcoming. I was fortunate to experience a welcoming and friendly environment that can enhance the dining experience significantly.

I'm looking forward to visiting soon and wishing the startup luck and success and also requesting to open more outlets in South west Delhi.

Name: First Coffee
Address: G7PM+X85, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Royallika Ahuja
Review: I'm thoroughly impressed with First coffee! Their expertly roasted beans yield a rich, smooth flavor that's perfect for any time of day. From the piping hot cappuccino to the bold kick of their cold brew, every sip is a delight. The cozy atmosphere of their cafes and friendly baristas make it a go-to destination. Whether you're a coffee connoisseur or just need a quick pick-me-up, First Coffee consistently delivers.

Name: First Coffee
Address: G7PM+X85, Jasola, New Delhi
Author: Dhruva Sharma
Review: It’s a good place with latest hip hop playlist that will never let you get bored. Exquisite coffee with decent price, much better than any other coffee shop.

Name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters | GK 1
Address: 53, Hansraj Gupta Road, South, Phase 1, Block R, Greater Kailash I, New Delhi
Author: Raj Palsingh
Review:
Really beautiful Coffeeshop here. Artisan sourdough bakery and Coffeeshop at the Orion hotel.

Had their flat white for 2 days. Well made coffee.

One of the days had a decadent sourdough croissant with dark chocolate and salted caramel. Super delicious. The amount of sugar and calories made it feel somewhat sinful, but sometimes indulging isn’t a bad thing.

On the second day I had a flat white with something off their new menu which was a sourdough bread with chicken inside the bread. Really enjoyed it with the coffee.

Highly recommend and you can find deals for delivery as well.

Name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters | GK 1
Address: 53, Hansraj Gupta Road, South, Phase 1, Block R, Greater Kailash I, New Delhi
Author: Omar Ahmed
Review:
Blue Tokai are known for their great (strong) coffee. This outlet is not an exception to that.
The GK1 outlet is a very cozy café. Perfect place for unwinding with coffee, frappe or other non-coffee drinks. You can choose to do so with a book or a small party of 2-5 people.
The menu is updated to include seasonal offerings. At the peak of summer 2024, I was introduced to the Watermelon Cold Brew and I absolutely loved it !

My go-to picks are the Trioccino and the Cold Brew and I highly recommend them.

The staff are extremely helpful. I ordered an Iced Café Mocha which tasted a little off. The barista was more than happy to make me another and decided to not even charge me to compensate for any inconveniences.

Must Visit !

Name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters | GK 1
Address: 53, Hansraj Gupta Road, South, Phase 1, Block R, Greater Kailash I, New Delhi
Author: Aishwarya Isha
Review: The place feels overcrowded. Absolutely no privacy if someone wants to have a private talk/moment. Plenty of mosquitos both inside and outside. Extremely loud as well. One needs to shout and talk otherwise you can't hear anything over the machine's noise. I had better expectations.

Name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters | GK 1
Address: 53, Hansraj Gupta Road, South, Phase 1, Block R, Greater Kailash I, New Delhi
Author: Neelav Samrat De
Review:
A decent Blue Tokai outlet on GK1 main road. Parking can be a hassle as it’s a busy road. The snacks options are good and tasty and expensive.
The coffees of Blue Tokai have always been good. The D’Maitres are young warm guys serving up your orders.
I had a Cheese bun (Korean?) and an americano and paid 500/- for that with GST. Ambience is good with both indoor and outdoor seating. Outside you can smoke as well.
Overall a decent place to enjoy that brew.

Name: Blue Tokai Coffee Roasters | GK 1
Address: 53, Hansraj Gupta Road, South, Phase 1, Block R, Greater Kailash I, New Delhi
Author: Carl Farrelly
Review:
Blue Tokai has become something of a favourite of mine during my time in Delhi. I visit every morning I wake up in this city, and I've tried almost every item on the breakfast and coffee menus.
Highly recommended.

Name: City Coffee Shop
Address: P75G+685, A-Block, Main Road, Brijpuri Pulia Main Road, Ziauddin Pur, Bhagirathi Vihar, New Mustafabad, Delhi
Author: parveen verma
Review: The atmosphere is electrifying and coffee offered is having good taste and varieties will surely amaze you..a place to be with friends for a great hangout..!

Name: City Coffee Shop
Address: P75G+685, A-Block, Main Road, Brijpuri Pulia Main Road, Ziauddin Pur, Bhagirathi Vihar, New Mustafabad, Delhi
Author: Sehaj Sandhu
Review: Everything is best. The best experience i ever had. The food is good. The staff is very descent.

Name: City Coffee Shop
Address: P75G+685, A-Block, Main Road, Brijpuri Pulia Main Road, Ziauddin Pur, Bhagirathi Vihar, New Mustafabad, Delhi
Author: Sree Kovurri
Review: Best experience for couples abd family. With great taste n very good service. Staffs are also very helping.

Name: City Coffee Shop
Address: P75G+685, A-Block, Main Road, Brijpuri Pulia Main Road, Ziauddin Pur, Bhagirathi Vihar, New Mustafabad, Delhi
Author: Bhavya Narayana Swamy
Review: Its very nice place for having tasty foods, i had a good time here..

Name: City Coffee Shop
Address: P75G+685, A-Block, Main Road, Brijpuri Pulia Main Road, Ziauddin Pur, Bhagirathi Vihar, New Mustafabad, Delhi
Author: vasim akram
Review: Coffee was amazing I liked it. Whenever i have leisure time I expend quality time here with my loved ones.

Name: Third Wave Coffee
Address: Unit No GF-22, 23-ABC/24, Peach Tree Rd, Block C, Sushant Lok Phase I, Sector 43, Gurugram
Author: Azaz Malik
Review:
Cafe Hopping at Third Wave Coffee☕️
Always delivering the perfect brew every time! Your dedication to high-quality coffee and creating a cozy atmosphere has truly elevated our coffee experience.
From the rich espresso shots to the smooth, perfectly frothed lattes, every cup is a testament to your craft.

you’re not just serving coffee; you’re creating a community of coffee lovers! 💚☕

Can’t wait for our next cup! Cheers to many more!

Name: Third Wave Coffee
Address: Unit No GF-22, 23-ABC/24, Peach Tree Rd, Block C, Sushant Lok Phase I, Sector 43, Gurugram
Author: DRISHTI GANDHI
Review: Amazing coffee with amazing atmosphere service boy Tanishq is also very good in behaviour

Name: Third Wave Coffee
Address: Unit No GF-22, 23-ABC/24, Peach Tree Rd, Block C, Sushant Lok Phase I, Sector 43, Gurugram
Author: Anjali Singh
Review: You'll love this newly opened Third Wave cafe in shushant lok-1. They actually chose the right place for their store as this area is lively till late night Due to the majority of youngsters living nearby. The place is very soothing with green interiors. The ambience is very calm and serene. They have provided a power point just below every seating, so you can come here and enjoy your coffee while you work. Clean environment and cheerful staff. Food options are limited but yes they are worth the price. If you are a black coffee lover, they also offer coffee tasting. Try their tandoori chicken sandwich for sure.

Name: Third Wave Coffee
Address: Unit No GF-22, 23-ABC/24, Peach Tree Rd, Block C, Sushant Lok Phase I, Sector 43, Gurugram
Author: Mollshree Jain
Review: I love the place's atmosphere and vibes. I can totally come here to chill or work. The music is fantastic. Coming to the service, it is spot on. Especially the baristas here are great like Tanishq. Great place to hang out overall.

Name: Third Wave Coffee
Address: Unit No GF-22, 23-ABC/24, Peach Tree Rd, Block C, Sushant Lok Phase I, Sector 43, Gurugram
Author: Athul Mohan
Review:
Perfect place if you want to relax with a cup
of coffee, sit with your laptop and do some casual work. The coffee varieties are awesome, the service is great. Not a huge variety of options to eat a proper meal, but snacks are good enough. Also, the place is spacious and the WiFi is decent.

Name: United Coffee House
