Name: The House Cafe Akaretler
Address: Sinanpaşa, Süleyman Seba Cd. No:16, Beşiktaş
Author: Denis Demichev
Review: Waiters are super slow, do not speak English. No menus are printed so you will need to use their website to show what food you need in Turk. Some items like water is not there so you will need to use sign language.

Name: The House Cafe Akaretler
Address: Sinanpaşa, Süleyman Seba Cd. No:16, Beşiktaş
Author: Fatimah Alqahtani
Review: A wonderful experience  , friendly staff delicious food  , prices are reasonable

Name: Vefakar Cafe & Bistro
Address: Kalenderhane, Cemal Yener Tosyalı Cd. No:68, Fatih
Author: Helena S
Review: Very good prices. Lots of local people come here. Okay drinks. Good atmosphere. Good wi-fi

Name: Vefakar Cafe & Bistro
Address: Kalenderhane, Cemal Yener Tosyalı Cd. No:68, Fatih
Author: Ben Ben
Review: First time in Turkey I wanted to try the famous shisha and I went there, It was the best shisha that I have ever tried in my life exactly as I wanted it and very very cheap price for it that I didn't believe it when I payed. Good vibes nice and beautiful service. ❤️🤍

Name: Vefakar Cafe & Bistro
Address: Kalenderhane, Cemal Yener Tosyalı Cd. No:68, Fatih
Author: Luís C
Review: I found this spot by chance during the afternoon while visiting the old xi century orthodox church, a mosque now, whose back wall they share and that makes an excellent decor element. I ordered a coffee and water but I noticed the menu had many possibilities at reasonable prices. Not the most traditional Turkish spot but the fact that it seems to follow some Occidental standards may have helped making me feel more comfortable. The 4 stars because I ordered a double but by the standards I am used to it was a simple expresso and because I asked for sparkling water and got none seems to understand what that is.

Name: Vefakar Cafe & Bistro
Address: Kalenderhane, Cemal Yener Tosyalı Cd. No:68, Fatih
Author: Matt Alef
Review: One of best places i have visited in Istanbul don’t the pizza they are specialist Pizza, for the seating is very comfort with ventilator that will beat the hot summer heat and welcoming atmosphere from the waiters.

Name: Vefakar Cafe & Bistro
Address: Kalenderhane, Cemal Yener Tosyalı Cd. No:68, Fatih
Author: Iman Veljan
Review: Very nice place, delicious food, nice staff, affordable prices

Name: Timboo Cafe Vadi İstanbul
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 109C/167, Sarıyer
Author: Maria Ledesma-Kap
Review:
Nice place for big groups. Staff are very accommodating though service is lacking. For instance, our guest arrived late, as soon as she sat down we ordered her drink- a can of iced tea, doesnt take 2 min. to take that out & serve it, but hers came out after she finished her food & a reminder from the wait staff… they have food ideal for groups of 4-6 people. The platter contains 6 mini burgers, wraps, fries, buffalo wings etc. The plate of nachos was also good. Celebrated my daughter’s birthday here, they were nice enough to allow a cake from outside & they even played a birthday song when they took the cake out. Some dishes are spicy, it would have been good if that was indicated on the menu or if we were fore-warned by the staff. Chicken wings were too spicy for the teens & it was left untouched.
Definitely a place teens would appreciate… now if only they included pizzas in the menu… perfect!

Name: Timboo Cafe Vadi İstanbul
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 109C/167, Sarıyer
Author: Aziz J
Review: I was a loyal customer, but I went there with my family today.. The food was good... 20% service was added to the total, as shown in the attached invoice. I do not know why the customer is forced to pay fees like this... This is absolutely unacceptable, and I will make complaint about it.

Name: Timboo Cafe Vadi İstanbul
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 109C/167, Sarıyer
Author: Cem Özkaynak
Review: If you're looking for a great kid-friendly restaurant with modern American food options like burgers, sides, pasta, and more, look no further than Timboo! With a fun and welcoming atmosphere, your kids will love dining here as much as you will! And with plenty of delicious options to choose from, everyone in your family is sure to find something they'll love!

Name: Timboo Cafe Vadi İstanbul
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 109C/167, Sarıyer
Author: R Eren
Review: Very good food! Staff and quality of the food all is great. Highly recommended!

Name: Timboo Cafe Vadi İstanbul
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 109C/167, Sarıyer
Author: Ali
Review: Regular food, good service, located in vadi Istanbul…the prices is high comparing with the food quality.

Name: AFİFE LADY CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: Başak mahallesi Yeşilvadi caddesi ahşap çarşı No:2/2G, Başakşehir
Author: S Tek
Review:
Nice and spacious place, we went for breakfast, we ordered omelette and menemen..

Food was really delicious and the service was fast and professional..

Prices were reasonable for the quality and service..

I will visit again..
Highly recommended..

Name: AFİFE LADY CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: Başak mahallesi Yeşilvadi caddesi ahşap çarşı No:2/2G, Başakşehir
Author: Layla Kishli
Review:
The place had good days, but I find it to be old and needs restoration especially the pink sofas and photography area.. they need changing or at least cleaning..
Breakfast was fine relative to its price..

Name: AFİFE LADY CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: Başak mahallesi Yeşilvadi caddesi ahşap çarşı No:2/2G, Başakşehir
Author: Muhammed Talib
Review:
I had a very good experience with breakfast before..
clean& Delicious

But after that I visit it more than one time, and I had a trouble with the staff when i ordered a breakfast for two persons, while i have three guest.. so the staff started negotiated me said; “you can’t order a 2 persons breakfast while you are more” !!! 😅 till I convinced them that some of my guest will never eat, I promise!! 😂

It’s really embarrassed me with my guests!! My guests were embarrassed too.. and this situation was repeated more than once.. so

After that I didn’t do it again 🙅🏻‍♂️

Old people say in the proverb: “If you are not generous, do not open a restaurant”

Hope things get better, good luck⬆️

Name: AFİFE LADY CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: Başak mahallesi Yeşilvadi caddesi ahşap çarşı No:2/2G, Başakşehir
Author: Shi
Review:
Good points:
1. The environment is nice
2. The staff is kind and friendly
3. You can smoke inside and there is an open area as well

Bad points:
1. The furniture is old
2. I didn t like some kinds of their food, especially the ones covered with sauce, so always tell them not to put sauce on the main dish.

Name: AFİFE LADY CAFE & RESTAURANT
Address: Başak mahallesi Yeşilvadi caddesi ahşap çarşı No:2/2G, Başakşehir
Author: nesrin alwhaidi
Review: The pasta was so delicious the steaks also was amazing the service was great, good experience 😊👍🏻

Name: BLACK MOON CAFE
Address: Altunizade, İcadiye Bağlarbaşı Cd. No:32/B, Üsküdar
Author: burcu kurt
Review: I've tried their chocolate before and it was very good. This time I came for coffee and dessert. Filter coffee is thirty times better than the ridiculous coffee shops around. Dessert was also delicious. Not to mention the smiling faces of the girls. I always come to this sweet place from now on 💕

Name: BLACK MOON CAFE
Address: Altunizade, İcadiye Bağlarbaşı Cd. No:32/B, Üsküdar
Author: Muhammed Said Çimen
Review: The chocolates and the music playing inside are wonderful. One of the rare cafes in Istanbul that relaxes people.

Name: BLACK MOON CAFE
Address: Altunizade, İcadiye Bağlarbaşı Cd. No:32/B, Üsküdar
Author: Mehmet Şehit
Review: Just opened. Nice, clean looking place. Probably because it's newly opened, it's affordable and tastes great. However, the business owners are also cultured and pleasant people. While you're making small talk, you suddenly find out that the editors have a bigger role in the production of a cult movie than the director, and all your dreams can come crashing down.

Name: BLACK MOON CAFE
Address: Altunizade, İcadiye Bağlarbaşı Cd. No:32/B, Üsküdar
Author: Ejder Mutlu
Review: It's a new place, it has a peaceful ambiance... I especially love the soft, deep jazz-blues tones... you know, music genres change throughout the day... I loved Ms. Emine's desserts, both the cup tiramisu and the cheesecake were delicious... I love this place. I loved the place, I recommend it to you too.

Name: BLACK MOON CAFE
Address: Altunizade, İcadiye Bağlarbaşı Cd. No:32/B, Üsküdar
Author: Gülsüm Mirzanlı
Review: Even though it is a newly opened place, the warmth of the environment attracts you. If you go, be sure to try their desserts, they are delicious. 😍

Name: Arasta Cafe & Restaurant
Address: Sultan Ahmet, Kabasakal Cd. No:22, Fatih
Author: Angie Klonis
Review: Jumbo Shrimp and Lamb chops, super delicious!!! Excellent service, friendly, lovely!

Name: Arasta Cafe & Restaurant
Address: Sultan Ahmet, Kabasakal Cd. No:22, Fatih
Author: Clint Nada
Review: I had a late-night dinner at this restaurant in Istanbul, and it was one of the few places open at that hour. I ordered the Adana kebab and chicken chops. The food was good, but it didn't stand out as exceptional. What sets this place apart is the option to enjoy hookah. Just a tip, if you're not a fan of the hookah smoke, be sure to choose a table far away from those who are smoking.

Name: Arasta Cafe & Restaurant
Address: Sultan Ahmet, Kabasakal Cd. No:22, Fatih
Author: Kevin C
Review: We ordered a cappuccino, latte macchiato and a fruit platter. I still don’t mind that the capuccino was not really the best one out there (strong coffee with only a light hint of milk in) what really bothered me was that we paid around 750 Turkish lira for a platter of fruit while it shows 75 on the menu card. Truly got scammed there and we know that this would be the last visit here.

Name: Arasta Cafe & Restaurant
Address: Sultan Ahmet, Kabasakal Cd. No:22, Fatih
Author: Madeline M
Review: Really good menemen, they cooked the eggs into the sauce really well and soaking it up with the bread was divine. It wasn't the scrambled eggs with tomato sauce on top that other places have. Also we saw the cutest street cat hanging out out front, 10/10 vibes.

Name: Arasta Cafe & Restaurant
Address: Sultan Ahmet, Kabasakal Cd. No:22, Fatih
Author: Shaheen Ali
Review: We were looking at the menu outside. Waiter came over with the actual menu and offered us a seat. Promised he didn't charge for seating and we could leave without  buying anything. No pressure. Done the same with another couple and they left . We decided to stay and didn't regret it. Food was delicious. Service was excellent. Atmosphere was good. Would recommend you try it. Only complaint. Portions were big.

Name: Miran Nargile Cafe
Address: Alemdar, Biçki Yurdu Sk. no:1 D:101, Fatih
Author: Aisling Douglas
Review: Loved the food and the hosts. Sweet decor and I loved the outdoor seating. First place we ate after checking into our hotel.

Name: Miran Nargile Cafe
Address: Alemdar, Biçki Yurdu Sk. no:1 D:101, Fatih
Author: Waleed Alghamdi
Review: The staff is pushy and gives you hustler/scammer vibes , he didn’t let me sit where I want and gave ridiculous excuse that I then later see him violate , he just left the better seats to push other tourists to come in, since I came for this restaurant myself he didn’t see it worth giving me the good spots, beer was poured in dirty glass the beer foam was bubbly not smooth which most likely happens when there’s oily fingers in tip of glass, I asked for another round of charcoal for the shisha, the waiter just flipped my old coal and not replaced it even though it’s been almost an hour, they just really feel like hustlers and scammers so be aware.

Name: Miran Nargile Cafe
Address: Alemdar, Biçki Yurdu Sk. no:1 D:101, Fatih
Author: Rafa
Review: Stop here! Perfect place for a tea and a narguile. Nice staff and comfy seats for two or ten. It's the best spot between Sofia and the Grand Bazar.

Name: Miran Nargile Cafe
Address: Alemdar, Biçki Yurdu Sk. no:1 D:101, Fatih
Author: Noor Hamadi
Review: The pies were good. Nice cozy place. They have shisha

Name: Miran Nargile Cafe
Address: Alemdar, Biçki Yurdu Sk. no:1 D:101, Fatih
Author: B star
Review: I went there , the waiter was so rude . First told me to sit and after without anything said to me leave the shisha finished . Honestly I was waiting for 15 minutes ordered my things . And then that happened. Don’t recommend this place also they charge double than other places. Will never go back there .

Name: Balon Cafe
Address: Caferağa, Kadıköy Sahili, Kadıköy
Author: Claire H
Review: We enjoyed the Kasarli Kofte, meat platter and Manti.  So much good food! Highly recommend.  Being right on the water made it extra special.

Name: Balon Cafe
Address: Caferağa, Kadıköy Sahili, Kadıköy
Author: Christine Aykac
Review: Good view, good service and good atmosphere! If you need a break, relax, maybe play a card or backgammon,  this is the place.

Name: Balon Cafe
Address: Caferağa, Kadıköy Sahili, Kadıköy
Author: Ayla Ahmadova
Review: They have places to sit on the seaside. The view is nice. Good to drink a cup of tea

Name: Balon Cafe
Address: Caferağa, Kadıköy Sahili, Kadıköy
Author: Khurram Hameed
Review:
Great view from Bolan cafe
Highly recommended for lunch and dinner as well

Name: Balon Cafe
Address: Caferağa, Kadıköy Sahili, Kadıköy
Author: Maria U-a
Review: The most picturesque spot with friendly staff and tasty breakfast! Enjoy your morning coffee with waves kissing stones at your feet and sunlit Sultanahmet in front of you!

Name: Nihan Cafe & Food
Address: Bostancı, Mehmet Şevki Paşa Cd. No:2/d, Kadıköy
Author: HaKan
Review: This is absolutely the ”gem” of Bostanci! If you wish to experience an exquisite selection of the Turkish food and hospitality, then this is your address to try both of them. It is relatively a small cafe, but the taste is great! The Chef, Nihan Hanim is a talented one who pays attention to details by taking care of the taste, presentation and of course the most important aspect: the hygiene! The Co-owner, Ümit Bey is also at his best to make sure your dining experience is a smooth and well remembered one. If I were the Micheline Star inspector, I wouldn’t hesitate granting the well deserved famous star! It’s definitely worth a try, if you want to get away from the greasy kebab options elsewhere!

Name: Nihan Cafe & Food
Address: Bostancı, Mehmet Şevki Paşa Cd. No:2/d, Kadıköy
Author: Ghazal Zo
Review: Everything is so delicious and fresh! I especially recommend the apple cinnamon sticks. The staff are very friendly and welcoming.

Name: Nihan Cafe & Food
Address: Bostancı, Mehmet Şevki Paşa Cd. No:2/d, Kadıköy
Author: Omid Kayhani
Review: Beautiful cozy cafe with fresh and delicious food and desserts that taste like home. They always greet you with smiles when you go in. Just amazing.

Name: Nihan Cafe & Food
Address: Bostancı, Mehmet Şevki Paşa Cd. No:2/d, Kadıköy
Author: Ali Sancilio
Review:
Honest shop!!!
The operators are a sweet married couple who prepare everything fresh by hand. You can see and taste it.
Although I have breakfast included in the hotel, my mother and I are coming for the fourth time.

It's better to support a family business than Tschibo & Co.

Name: Nihan Cafe & Food
Address: Bostancı, Mehmet Şevki Paşa Cd. No:2/d, Kadıköy
Author: Behcet Karacalar
Review: It is an excellent business that is like a medicine to the Bostancı neighborhood. Reasonable prices and delicious tastes. As if that were not enough, when the managers are excellent, you enjoy both the food you eat and the time you spend. Greetings to Ms. Nihan and her beloved husband from here 👋

Name: Cookah Cafe & Bistro
Address: Yeşilköy, Seyit Ali Sokağı No:28, Bakırköy
Author: Dr mohammad
Review:
It used to be called double apple cafe , with excellent service & food .
For me it is a must hub prior to any flight in Atatürk airport.
The food is really delicious, the staff are extremely friendly.

Name: Cookah Cafe & Bistro
Address: Yeşilköy, Seyit Ali Sokağı No:28, Bakırköy
Author: Mete Kara
Review: In one word, perfect food, presentations, everything is beautiful. I can say that it is the only place in Yeşilköy where you can rest your mind.

Name: Cookah Cafe & Bistro
Address: Yeşilköy, Seyit Ali Sokağı No:28, Bakırköy
Author: Mevlut Ali Kayra
Review: I recommend the red wine and medium rare tenderloin. After eating it, you will understand why I gave it 5 stars...

Name: Cookah Cafe & Bistro
Address: Yeşilköy, Seyit Ali Sokağı No:28, Bakırköy
Author: Nazmi Kaplan
Review: The firefish cocktail made by Mr. Ilker, who runs a good place, was great, thank you.

Name: Cookah Cafe & Bistro
Address: Yeşilköy, Seyit Ali Sokağı No:28, Bakırköy
Author: Mehmet Yamaner
Review:
Decent atmosphere, service is fast and friendly
The cuisine is very successful

Name: Çaycım Cafe
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Mecidiyeköy Mahallesi, Selahattin pınar caddesi, Mecidiye Aralığı Sk. no:4, Şişli
Author: m. g.
Review:
Wow, what an atmosphere!

Highly recommended.
I went there to try shisha with a local friend, and what an experience it was.

Loved every second I spent there, amazing shisha and fantastic tea.

Name: Çaycım Cafe
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Mecidiyeköy Mahallesi, Selahattin pınar caddesi, Mecidiye Aralığı Sk. no:4, Şişli
Author: Rezza Mohebbi
Review: Cozy place, friendly staff, good tea.

Name: Çaycım Cafe
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Mecidiyeköy Mahallesi, Selahattin pınar caddesi, Mecidiye Aralığı Sk. no:4, Şişli
Author: Meryem Çelik
Review: The best tea shop in Istanbul, you don't want to get up when you go there :)

Name: Çaycım Cafe
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Mecidiyeköy Mahallesi, Selahattin pınar caddesi, Mecidiye Aralığı Sk. no:4, Şişli
Author: Ertuğrul Barış AKGÜN
Review: Warm and friendly atmosphere, very affordable prices, friendly service.

Name: Çaycım Cafe
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Mecidiyeköy Mahallesi, Selahattin pınar caddesi, Mecidiye Aralığı Sk. no:4, Şişli
Author: samet eroğlu
Review: tea is super

Name: Cafe Cadde
Address: Erenköy, Caddebostan Mahallesi, Bağdat Cad. No:349, Kadıköy
Author: A. Isa C.
Review:
It’s a nice location, the place has a good atmosphere.
I ordered tacos and salad, tacos were delicious ❤️, as a Mexican I can say that I was very surprised. The salad was also very delicious.
The staff are friendly and kind. A bit pricey. Toilets are clean. Would happily return.

Name: Cafe Cadde
Address: Erenköy, Caddebostan Mahallesi, Bağdat Cad. No:349, Kadıköy
Author: Honey Ar
Review: This charming restaurant, a long-standing feature on beautiful Bagdat Street, offered a memorable dining experience. Celebrating my friend’s birthday there, we were delighted by the thoughtful surprise of two delicious desserts, each adorned with a candle. The appetizers, particularly the bread served with cream cheese and olives, were exceptional. Additionally, the tiny sweet treats served after the main course were a delightful touch. The overall quality of the food, complemented by an impressive selection of wines, makes this place highly recommendable. I would definitely suggest it to everyone for its delightful atmosphere and exquisite cuisine.

Name: Cafe Cadde
Address: Erenköy, Caddebostan Mahallesi, Bağdat Cad. No:349, Kadıköy
Author: Esin Çelebi Bowyer
Review:
Delicious and nicely presented food in a lovely atmosphere.
On a sunny February day in Bagdat street, it was a perfect spot to meet up with a friend and have a perfect brunch.
It ticked all the boxes for us. Highly recommended.

Name: Cafe Cadde
Address: Erenköy, Caddebostan Mahallesi, Bağdat Cad. No:349, Kadıköy
Author: Daniel Braun
Review:
You get exactly what the name says: A cafe + a street. Both are very lively, full of people, good mood and holiday atmosphere.
We have been there for breakfast:
- great bread variety
- good menemen

You get very good food for a reasonable price.
If you search silent places where waiters are there for you are ready to pay more, then you should check out other places.

Will surely go there to try their pizza.

Name: Cafe Cadde
Address: Erenköy, Caddebostan Mahallesi, Bağdat Cad. No:349, Kadıköy
Author: M. A. (Manochino)
Review: I'm normally not really a fan of places that offer the so-called "world cuisine". You usually get all kinds of food ranging from burgers to pizzas, pastas to salads, meat dishes to mexican food. In the past I wasn't a fan of this place but this time around, we were impressed. The breakfast was awesome. The coffee and tea was nice. The service was sweet. All in all, the experience was great. Thank you!

Name: Cafe osmanoglu
Address: Molla Gürani, Aksaray Mektebi Sk. 9/A, Fatih
Author: Hunadah Alhariri
Review:
Lovely cafe and shisha place. I didn’t try the food, but the staff are extremely friendly and accommodating. There is an African vibe in the place, which adds to its charm. The prices are very reasonable for Istanbul standards. The shisha itself was also very good. It is definitely recommended place for lengthy periods. I used it as a working spot but I had my AirPods on because the music can be a bit loud. But, in all cases, definitely great
Value for money.

Name: Cafe osmanoglu
Address: Molla Gürani, Aksaray Mektebi Sk. 9/A, Fatih
Author: David Max Starčević
Review: Found this place during my death march in Istanbul and I am happy i did. Cant believe how rich portion was. I ordered chicken skewers and got three pieces, bowl of rice, bunch of roasted vegetables, french fries, salad, basket of white bread and piece of flat bread. Everything was well seasoned and delicious. And the price was very reasonable, little more then 300 liras. Recommended 👌

Name: Cafe osmanoglu
Address: Molla Gürani, Aksaray Mektebi Sk. 9/A, Fatih
Author: Sanjay J Kamath
Review: Located near Yusufpaşa metro station. They serve very good Nargile and variety of food and drinks. This place is frequented by lots of Africans and Arabs. Service is good, a very nice place to relax and enjoy.  We visited this place twice already

Name: Cafe osmanoglu
Address: Molla Gürani, Aksaray Mektebi Sk. 9/A, Fatih
Author: Mozzie
Review:
The food it's so delicious and the hookah too.
Staff's super nice and kind!!
I recommend with pleasure

Name: Cafe osmanoglu
Address: Molla Gürani, Aksaray Mektebi Sk. 9/A, Fatih
Author: Uffe Pedersen
Review: Very Nice cafe. Nothing fancy, just very nice

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Nathoma A
Review: Cute small place to fill your tea cravings. The tea was lovely. And a very nice and honest owner.

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Daniel Rozen
Review: Highly reccomended for tourist. True local tea spot with local prices and really good tea. Try it!

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Photo Journalist
Review:
Nice small neighborhood tea/cay joint (as it seems like most cay places in Turkey are for men only) in case you are a woman and mixed group just up the hill a few more stores and enjoy the "Köşk Boşnak Böregi ve Tatli" restaurant for quick and cheap meal with tea/cay.
If I’ve helped, thank you for the “like”!

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Ondřej Štěpán
Review: We hid from the rain at the owner's place and it was the best decision. His tea and coffee were the best we had in Istanbul.

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Maximilian Lösel
Review: Really honest and pleasant shop for a simple, very good çay!

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Jade Gao
Review:
Very rude staffs, I paid 10% service charges but didn’t get the equivalent service:
1. We asked the man staff if it’s possible to add some hot water in the tea pot, the staff asked back: you want a tea or hot water? After we answered hot water, he said no… since no hot water option why have to ask back?
2. We used restaurant’s toilet for a second time got a female staff followed, she suspected and tried to prevent us go to the toilet, even we told her we paid in the tea house with 10% “sevice” charge already, she still asked us with a rude tongue like: did you?
The environment is ok, tea is too bitter with little water which can’t add more, makes the tea flavor even worse, not worthy at all.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Samer Mohd
Review: Very nice place to sit relax and have some tea, coffee and desserts. After long walks around the market, this is the perfect open space area to sit. Highly recommend visiting this place. Very nice and clean with good service. They have mini boutique shops inside as well. Nice sitting area and nice ambiance. Wide selection of teas and desserts. Must visit if around the area of misr market and grand bazaar.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Ammar Salman
Review: Nice vibe and good set-up.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Yusri Sallim
Review:
Nice ambient in the area despite the buzzling area external of the building

The macarons never fail to appease me. The taste was just nice without heavy on the sugar. Shd go for the pistachio dream cake and all in all go for the chai to quench the sugary taste..haha at least you do not have to feel guilty to taking on all the desserts

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Julian Asher
Review:
Very special tea house right in the centre of the busy area
This is a genui oasis tucked in the shopping lanes by the tourist area. Worth a visit to escape the busy streets. Good tea coffee and food and drinks. Served to the table and QR code to help show the menu to all international travellers.
Lots of choices and a great place to have a tea or cold drink in the hot summer.

Name: Nail Baba Tea House
Address: Alemdar, Ticarethane Sk. No:14/A, Fatih
Author: Marcus Hooper
Review: Nail Baba is the most wonderful old world unpretentious teahouse right in the middle of the kitschy tourist hell of Sultanahmet. We stumbled upon it by accident.  Not only was the coffee superb but it was like the prices hadn't gone up in 12 months, which in Istanbul is significant. The owner was a genuinely beautiful man with the kind of gentle Turkish charm that is far less common these days.

Name: Nail Baba Tea House
Address: Alemdar, Ticarethane Sk. No:14/A, Fatih
Author: GPS- Gurmin Pander Speaks
Review: Authentic place, untouched. Love the ambience, tea and Turkish coffee ❤️ The owner was super friendly and warm too ❤️

Name: Nail Baba Tea House
Address: Alemdar, Ticarethane Sk. No:14/A, Fatih
Author: Charlie Welsby
Review: We stumbled across Nail Baba while walking through Istanbul. Although only a few tables and small chairs Nail Baba is an incredibly kind and generous man who we were lucky to meet. Although he spoke very little English, it didn't stop him from having conversation with us with some help from others translating. The tea was great and we hope to come back again some day.

Name: Nail Baba Tea House
Address: Alemdar, Ticarethane Sk. No:14/A, Fatih
Author: Ettore Mengozzi
Review: Best place in town. Authentic coffee and tea and the owner is very polite and kind. Highly recommended

Name: Nail Baba Tea House
Address: Alemdar, Ticarethane Sk. No:14/A, Fatih
Author: Haley Bach
Review: Best Turkish coffee I’ve ever had.

Name: Yel Çay evi
Address: Sinanpaşa, Köyiçi Mektep Sk. 1 B, Beşiktaş
Author: Maryam Akbari
Review: Tasty local tea with good price

Name: Yel Çay evi
Address: Sinanpaşa, Köyiçi Mektep Sk. 1 B, Beşiktaş
Author: Yasemin Yıldız
Review: It is a very friendly and sweet place, the attention and care is very nice. I definitely recommend you go. Should be your first stop if you visit Beşiktaş

Name: Yel Çay evi
Address: Sinanpaşa, Köyiçi Mektep Sk. 1 B, Beşiktaş
Author: RoBU
Review: The best tea I've ever had in my life. It's not even in Rize. No one should think that I am exaggerating. I barely said anything. Cevdet Bey's attention made me feel like I was in a 5 star hotel. It is a very warm and friendly place.

Name: Yel Çay evi
Address: Sinanpaşa, Köyiçi Mektep Sk. 1 B, Beşiktaş
Author: Halis Avgül
Review: The place is very clean inside and out and the attention is very good. I really prefer you to go there.

Name: Yel Çay evi
Address: Sinanpaşa, Köyiçi Mektep Sk. 1 B, Beşiktaş
Author: Yıldırım Aymaz
Review: There is definitely no one in Beşiktaş who makes such good tea 👍

Name: Murat Tea House
Address: Cevatpaşa, Komiser Reşat Dayı Cd. No:10, Bayrampaşa
Author: Mesut UĞURLU
Review: A pleasant tea house

Name: Murat Tea House
Address: Cevatpaşa, Komiser Reşat Dayı Cd. No:10, Bayrampaşa
Author: Okan Karatas
Review: Yes, the tea is legendary, but its colorful things are also legendary, kiwi, oralet, bird's nose, and linden special for the winter months 👍🏻

Name: Murat Tea House
Address: Cevatpaşa, Komiser Reşat Dayı Cd. No:10, Bayrampaşa
Author: Ali Okan

Name: Murat Tea House

Author: KADİR KORAY GÜLERYÜZ
Review: It's a place where you can have a nice conversation and drink tea with your friends. I also recommend the liver.

Name: Murat Tea House
Address: Cevatpaşa, Komiser Reşat Dayı Cd. No:10, Bayrampaşa

Address: Aksaray, Çakırağa Cami Ar., Fatih
Author: Burhan Yıldırım

Name: Siya Cafe
Address: Şehit Muhtar, Süslü Saksı Sk., Beyoğlu
Author: Aisha Habli
Review: Casual break while in busy Taksim,  strong çay that gives you a boost! Convenient and central! Go for a coffee and kind hospitality!

Name: Siya Cafe
Address: Şehit Muhtar, Süslü Saksı Sk., Beyoğlu
Author: OKAN KUMBARACI
Review:
Menemen and tea are great. A tea house where you can sit and enjoy at an affordable price, like a safe haven in the middle of the restless streets. The operators are at work. There is no one to disturb you inside. Illegal tea is legendary, you should definitely try it.

A real Turkish traditional tea house with reasonable prices. Nice and peaceful atmosphere

Name: Siya Cafe
Address: Şehit Muhtar, Süslü Saksı Sk., Beyoğlu
Author: Ferhat Demircan

Name: Rize Çay Evi
Address: Katip Mustafa Çelebi, Bahçeli Hamam Sk. No:7, Beyoğlu
Author: Jimmyller GR
Review:
Very nice hot and tasty tea.
The atmosphere is amazing and really friendly you can enjoy and feel it like your own home!

Name: Rize Çay Evi
Address: Katip Mustafa Çelebi, Bahçeli Hamam Sk. No:7, Beyoğlu
Author: JohnS 98
Review: A really nice place to have your tea, very friendly staff and nice atmosphere.

Name: Rize Çay Evi
Address: Katip Mustafa Çelebi, Bahçeli Hamam Sk. No:7, Beyoğlu
Author: Todd Hulbert
Review: Fantastic Türk kahvesi and Cay. Liked it so much I stayed for 2 coffees and a tea. Highly recommend.

Name: Rize Çay Evi
Address: Katip Mustafa Çelebi, Bahçeli Hamam Sk. No:7, Beyoğlu
Author: Andrea
Review: A cute spot for drinking tea!

Name: Rize Çay Evi
Address: Katip Mustafa Çelebi, Bahçeli Hamam Sk. No:7, Beyoğlu
Author: Olcay Akdoğan
Review: His tea is always good and his conversation is a 10.

Name: Belediye çay bahçesi
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Fatih
Author: Lulu M
Review: My family and i did try turkish tea and tiramisu, the cake and tea were good. But the people are rude. Its selfservice coffee shop, i didn't know there was no any sign too. The way they talked to us was rude. Im not recomend this place. You could find many coffee shop with good sevice arround this one.

Name: Belediye çay bahçesi
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Fatih
Author: Haval Sadiq
Review:
They service hot and cold drinks in addition to breakfast.
It is located in the middle of the downtown near the Great Bazaar.

Name: Belediye çay bahçesi
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Fatih
Author: Yasser Noaman
Review:
I just tried the tea and it was really Nice
Also very cheap

Name: Belediye çay bahçesi
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Fatih
Author: Tamim Arif
Review:
Excellently located self-serve tea shop! After walking around, and visiting Yeni Cami Mosque, Belediye çay bahçesi beckons to take a break, sip some tea, and watch the people walk by!

It has a very nice covered outdoor atrium. You start by standing in line to place and pay for your order, take your slip down the counter where the staff complete your order and hand you a tray.

Then off to find an empty table, sit down, and relax while sipping tea. For us it was a bonus to be able to sit in the covered patio to avoid being drenched by a passing rain shower!

Name: Belediye çay bahçesi
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Fatih
Author: Feruz Babashov
Review: Tea is good here!

Name: Hepgüven Tea House
Address: Derviş Ali, Nurettin Tekke Sk. no:19b, Fatih
Author: Furkan Turunç
Review: A decent environment awaits you with brother Berkay's friendly and sincere atmosphere, which will be like your home, where you can spend friendly and brotherly time and warm you up with various hot and cold drinks...

Name: Hepgüven Tea House
Address: Derviş Ali, Nurettin Tekke Sk. no:19b, Fatih
Author: Aliosman KILIÇ
Review: It's a very friendly and comfortable place. I loved the oriental corner. The tea service is fast and tasteful. It's a must-visit place.

Name: Hepgüven Tea House
Address: Derviş Ali, Nurettin Tekke Sk. no:19b, Fatih
Author: Emir Ertal
Review: We are addicted to it, it is a very nice place, we visit every evening, thank you very much to Mr. Berkay for his service.

Name: Hepgüven Tea House
Address: Derviş Ali, Nurettin Tekke Sk. no:19b, Fatih
Author: Bilgi
Review: Very authentic, very friendly. Whenever I go to Fatih, I can't drink tea anywhere else.

Name: Hepgüven Tea House
Address: Derviş Ali, Nurettin Tekke Sk. no:19b, Fatih
Author: Yasemin bulut
Review: How delicious can tea be this much? You can eat mixed toast as if you were eating a baklava. I'll stop by again🌸

Name: Tarihi Havuzlu Kıraathane
Address: Şehsuvar Bey, Kadırga Meydanı Sk. No:2, Fatih
Author: Ahmed Al-Akki
Review: This historical Café is the only surviving Ottoman café that was frequented and managed by the local firemen, and whose origins go back to the Janissaries. Its old basin and marble fountain bear witness to its colourful past. When the Janissary corps was suppressed in 1826, the cafes they owned were closed and the firemen's duties were taken over by the regular army, and then by the district authorities.

Name: Tarihi Havuzlu Kıraathane
Address: Şehsuvar Bey, Kadırga Meydanı Sk. No:2, Fatih
Author: Mundo asbl BrusselsArtVocal
Review:
Nice little café with a heart of shade. Inside is a beautiful Ottoman fountain.
Have a coffee at Arif's. He'll tell you all about the area and its history. He's a native of the area.
A must-see if you want to take a break with the locals.

Name: Tarihi Havuzlu Kıraathane
Address: Şehsuvar Bey, Kadırga Meydanı Sk. No:2, Fatih
Author: EBRAR NUR ÜNAL
Review: This is a shabby tea house run by an old gentleman, who is very quiet, unlike the surroundings. Both the garden and the inside were very beautiful. We took a small tea break to rest after the walk. We took a short, sweet break from our trip with the conversation of the uncle who served the tea.

Name: Tarihi Havuzlu Kıraathane
Address: Şehsuvar Bey, Kadırga Meydanı Sk. No:2, Fatih
Author: Selin Özmen
Review: The rental house in Kadirga, which is quite shabby and therefore beautiful. Its interior is very old and large, I think it is under protection due to the historical pool. We sat outside because it was blowing. Tea is 10 TL. The 90-year-old brother's conversation was very sweet, he brought us the tea himself because he didn't have a helper that day. He explained how this rental house was used as a set in many TV series and movies :) Even a scene was shot in Ezel.

Name: Tarihi Havuzlu Kıraathane
Address: Şehsuvar Bey, Kadırga Meydanı Sk. No:2, Fatih
Author: İBRAHİM KILIÇ
Review: A coffeehouse inherited from grandfather to grandchild from past to present. Mr. Arif, who is currently running the business, is a very pleasant conversationalist and a nice person. It is a place frequented by photographers as well as having many visitors and movie and TV series shootings. Since it has a decorative pool and a pump in the past, it has been called by names such as "Pool coffee house or tulumbalı".

Name: Tespih Çay Evi
Address: Güneşli, 1256. Sk No:3, Bağcılar
Author: Emre Antika
Review: Everything is very good, I advise you not to leave without eating toast from Mr. Rıdvan.

Name: Tespih Çay Evi
Address: Güneşli, 1256. Sk No:3, Bağcılar
Author: Denizkan Turşin
Review: The guys are really very nice and respectful. I liked it very much. I will visit again next time, if I have the chance.

Name: Tespih Çay Evi
Address: Güneşli, 1256. Sk No:3, Bağcılar
Author: Mehmet Aras
Review: One of the best examples of family atmosphere and family business. Thanks to the waiter Barış Bey and the manager Rıdvan Bey..

Name: Tespih Çay Evi
Address: Güneşli, 1256. Sk No:3, Bağcılar
Author: Hasan Kaya
Review: I recommend Güneşli tesbih tea house to everyone with its number one tea and service.

Name: Tespih Çay Evi
Address: Güneşli, 1256. Sk No:3, Bağcılar
Author: Talha Karaman
Review: When it comes to Tespih tea house, the first thing that comes to mind is delicious toast and beyond perfect tea. An invaluable and decent environment with the sincerity of Rıdvan.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Carmel Saleh
Review: absolutely terrible experience. i entered and was waiting for about 10 minutes and no one had taken my order. so naturally, i went up to the cashier and asked her to take it and she informed me that she was “busy” with other customers. keep in mind that there were literally only TWO other people together who already had their orders and there was another service provider aside from her. i waited another 10 minutes and when she finally came to take my order she informed me that there were no tapioca pearls and it would be ready after 20 minutes. as a boba store, how do you not have enough of the main thing people come to get? aside from that, had she simply taken my order before i wouldn’t have had to wait 20 minutes only to be told that they had no tapioca pearls. extremely unprofessional and inefficient. wish that there was a zero star option.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Maya
Review: ive been here a couple times and i want to come here more!! i love the matcha drinks and bubble tea, also the mochi is authentically made (not with icecream). i saw a couple reviews saying the mochi was bad and hard to eat, which i find hard to believe!! the lady who owns the place handmakes every mochi herself and makes it the authentic way. the mochi is stretchy soft and has a really nice texture.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Mehray C
Review: The milk tea was very good, but the new waiter didn't seem to be very attentive. I ordered red bean milk tea and was given pearl milk tea. Before I finished my drink, the waiter was talking on the phone while trying to take my drink away, and asked me "can I take it away" after putting the trash in the cup. Taste is fine, service is not!

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: osama
Review:
Hands down the best bubble tea shop in istanbul!
So many drink options and great authentic made mochi, should definitely try matcha here because it’s professionally made.
The owner is the nicest and friendly staff.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Sude CAL
Review: I loved the mochi, tried the oreo one, a little basic and too sweet but it was exactly what i was expecting. Boba tea, its not my cup of tea but my friend liked it.

Name: Bi Çay Kartal
Address: Esentepe, İnönü Cd. 85a, Kartal
Author: Mustafa Berk Çelik
Review: My brother Erbay's tea is completely different, I no longer like the tea at home. Edit: Erbay is gone, I came back through the door, I don't know how the place is, but I will never drink tea again :(

Name: Bi Çay Kartal
Address: Esentepe, İnönü Cd. 85a, Kartal

Review: Have some tea. Do we need another word? I like. I sat and enjoyed it and will sit again.

Address: Esentepe, İnönü Cd. 85a, Kartal

Review: I came with my wife. We drank tea and played backgammon. I would also like to mention the manager, Mr. Erbay, who provided a seriously decent environment. It seems like he will make us a regular with his pleasant conversation and smiling face. Interestingly, some people wrote that tea is not good. As a person who uses tea professionally at home, I rank it first in the category of the best tea to drink outside. Be sure to stop by.

Name: Bi Çay Kartal
Address: Esentepe, İnönü Cd. 85a, Kartal

Review: The atmosphere inside is wonderfully authentic, the owner is very friendly and helpful. With the wood stove inside, you feel like you are in your warm home. There is nothing to say about the taste of tea, the freshest taste is here. Roads are built and people come here just to drink fresh tea 🥰

Review: This is a small family-run alley shop that serves only coffee and tea (12.5 lira). They are fast, friendly and won't mind that you take your time with your hot drink. They are so skillful in serving those dainty little glasses of tea during peak hours. Pls support local business and keep tradition alive!

A nice tea garden. If you sit outside, you can bring your own food with you and only can order drinks from the cafe. A termos of tea, which is enough for 5-6 people costs around 100 lira as of May 2022. You also can order pizza, toast, etc.

The view is nice, inside are chickens, cats, a kids playground, toilet, and next to it a big area that kids can play.

Name: Çengelköy Çay Evi

Author: özgür hasan altuncu
Review: Brother Erol is one of a kind. His tea is unique, his conversation is unique. A true family business. I suggest you don't get ripped off in Çengelköy, definitely visit Erol brother's place. The place is like a museum. He is a very good Fenerbahçe player. Be sure to get to know Erol, one of the beautiful faces of Istanbul.

Name: Çengelköy Çay Evi
Address: Çengelköy, Çengeloğlu Sk. No:19, Üsküdar
Author: alikara232
Review: I went there twice, you got into an argument. If I go to the third place, I will definitely be a police officer here. Like, is it Erol? The uncle with the wig on his head is waiting for you to walk up to him and say 1 tea please. He says this is a tea house. My uncle, when you make a sign from where you sit in a tea house everywhere in the world, it means "1 tea" It means "please". The bottom line is that he can't pour tea and give it to you, so you just wait. Is the tea nice? It's nice and the price is 7 TL.

Name: Çengelköy Çay Evi

It's a nice place, but you may not be able to find a seat. There are 4 tables outside, only 2 inside, so if you find a place, sit down immediately, don't miss out.  .

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Osmanağa, Örmen İşhanı, Halitağa Cd. No 37B, Kadıköy
Author: Ridoh Rylands
Review:
Monster Teashop in Kadıköy is a vibrant and trendy spot that brings a unique twist to the traditional tea experience. Nestled in the bustling streets of Kadıköy, this cozy café is a haven for tea enthusiasts and anyone looking to enjoy something a little different.

As soon as you enter, you're greeted by a quirky and playful atmosphere that sets Monster Teashop apart. The décor is modern and fun, with colorful accents and creative touches that make the space feel lively and inviting. It’s the kind of place where you can relax, chat with friends, or even get some work done while sipping on a delicious beverage.

The menu at Monster Teashop is diverse and exciting, offering a wide range of teas that cater to every palate. From classic milk teas and fruity blends to more adventurous options like their signature monster-themed drinks, there’s something here for everyone. Whether you’re a fan of boba or prefer your tea straight up, each drink is crafted with care and bursting with flavor.

One of the highlights of Monster Teashop is their creative presentation. The drinks are as visually appealing as they are tasty, often served with colorful toppings, fresh fruits, or even fun, edible decorations that add a touch of whimsy to your tea time.

The staff at Monster Teashop are friendly and enthusiastic, always ready to recommend a new flavor or customize your drink to suit your taste. Their passion for tea and commitment to quality make every visit enjoyable.

In summary, Monster Teashop in Kadıköy is a must-visit for tea lovers and anyone seeking a fun and unique café experience. With its creative drinks, lively atmosphere, and excellent service, it’s a perfect spot to unwind and enjoy something special.

Rating: 4.7/5 - Monster Teashop offers a delightful blend of creativity, flavor, and fun, making it a standout destination in Kadıköy for tea enthusiasts and café lovers alike.

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Osmanağa, Örmen İşhanı, Halitağa Cd. No 37B, Kadıköy
Author: Minimo
Review:
As a bubble tea lover coming from Tashkent, can safely say that monster teashop is Turkeys best one,

The customization options are spectacular and quite detailed from the sugar and ice levels,to the toppings and till the milk options
,which is something quite nice for people that have struggles digesting whole milk like me.

The drinks are very high quality and you can taste the authentic aromas,
However the only small issue I  personally noticed are the matchas,
As a strong matcha lover I have gave the matcha in monster tea shop several chances,
from bland matcha to more different flavored ones such as mango.
However despite my efforts,
I always manage to get disappointed,
due to the matcha not properly dissolving or tasting very bitter.
Anyways that's my take on the matchas however their other bobas are spectacular,
I can recommend the taro bubble tea the most.

All in all,great boba shop,beautiful culturally accurate environment and drinks and the employes are mostly very friendly and knowledgeable
; ]

Name: Çaycı - Trump Towers
Address: Kuştepe Mah. Mecidiyeköy Yolu Cad. Trump Towers AVM Kat:B3 No:54, Şişli
Author: Güven Karyeniç
Review:
I had tea at the branch on Büyükdere Street (which I couldn't find listed on the maps). Of course, we will not expect any taste or quality from chain businesses that do not claim to be assertive, but an assertive business (whose goodness I have no doubt about) who uses mottos such as "fresh tea" and "hot tea" in its shop and tries to give "tea lessons" with philosophical jokes on the graphics on its walls, is about brewing beverages. It's a shame he missed the basic rules...
*****
Apart from this dilemma regarding "tea", the shop is seriously successful in terms of all factors...
In this sense, I congratulate the operator
******
Famous chain businesses try to brew tea with pure water and give you something that is both irrelevant and extremely harmful to health, and the buyer and seller are happy with it.
In this context, I think this business should either stop talking assertively or seek advice about WATER, the primary element of brewing beverages, and base its claim on solid foundations...
*****
There may be people who say "everyone knows how to criticize, tell the truth if you can", but if so, I will try to guide those who ask for help.

Review: We came to the place, the atmosphere is great 😍 they are making fresh doner kebabs and I said doner bread, wow, how delicious would it be? 👏🏻👏🏻 I loved the doner, it's delicious, the bread is crispy and hot, I highly recommend it 👌🏻👌🏻 It also has a closed section, this place is beautiful from the outside ☺️ ☺️ coffee, dessert 🍫 tea, hookah, everything is there. Whenever I come to Bakırköy, I will definitely come here. It is a beautiful cafe inside and out. The prices are also very reasonable.

Name: Havuzbaşı Çay Bahçesi
Address: Zeytinlik, Ebuzziya Cd. No 18, Bakırköy
Author: Eren Öztürk
Review: We ordered menemen, the color and taste were very bad, we ordered margarita pizza, it was no different from kashar pita.

Name: Havuzbaşı Çay Bahçesi
Address: Zeytinlik, Ebuzziya Cd. No 18, Bakırköy
Author: Elen Zafer
Review: Even though the business is a very bad establishment, when I realized that I had forgotten my bag and my personal belongings there and went there, they were able to easily say that they found it but threw it in the trash. They didn't even apologize. I definitely do not recommend it. The people at the cash register are also very disrespectful. Forgotten items should have been handed over to the police.

Name: Tea Or Coffee
Address: No:A, Cihangir Mahallesi, Oba Sokak No:17, Beyoğlu
Author: Illa Proskurova
Review: Best matcha latte you will find in Cihangir, staff is really friendly and atmosphere is amazing

Name: Tea Or Coffee
Address: No:A, Cihangir Mahallesi, Oba Sokak No:17, Beyoğlu
Author: D J
Review: If you’re staying in the Cihangir area and you’re looking for a relaxing coffee spot, this is the place. They have one of the best coffee I’ve ever had. They have delicious pastries for breakfast and very friendly staff. Also the atmosphere in this shop in the morning is so great! Highly recommend.

Name: Tea Or Coffee
Address: No:A, Cihangir Mahallesi, Oba Sokak No:17, Beyoğlu
Author: siti hajar
Review:
1 ice coffee, 1 Brownee and 1 green tea with milk  for 440 Tk Lira.

Really don‘t know whether this price normal  or not.

Unfortunately, the prices will be not be seen anywhere and later be informed only at the counter.

Name: Tea Or Coffee
Address: No:A, Cihangir Mahallesi, Oba Sokak No:17, Beyoğlu
Author: Sevda Ghasemzadeh
Review:
Such a cute & cozy cafe in Cihangir.
Staff are really friendly and they also speak English.
Prices are normal comparing to other places.

Name: Tea Or Coffee
Address: No:A, Cihangir Mahallesi, Oba Sokak No:17, Beyoğlu
Author: Sofiya Vostrikova
Review:
My best and favourite coffee shop in Cihangir🥹
Cappuccino is amazing, staff are friendly and atmosphere is super cozy
Almost 6 months my every morning starts with cappuccino from tea or coffee🥰

Name: Tea Shop
Address: Abdurrahmangazi, Fatih Blv. No:67, Sultanbeyli
Author: Erkan Akyüz
Review: Its name is Tea Shop, meaning tea. They give stale tea and they say that it is refreshed every half hour. The crocodile has never drank such bad tea lately. You ask for a cup of tea after shopping and they give you stale tea. Be informed as you go

Name: Tea Shop
Address: Abdurrahmangazi, Fatih Blv. No:67, Sultanbeyli
Author: morris
Review: It is a place located inside Atlas Park shopping mall. I bought a latte and a dessert. The dessert tasted very nice and fresh. Coffee is good, but it would be better if it was served in a glass cup rather than a cardboard cup. The prices are very reasonable compared to other places (I say this as a student!) It varies depending on the type of coffee, personally, I bought the latte for around 100 lira, and the dessert was 140 lira. The design of the cafe is also very cool 🤭

Name: Tea Shop
Address: Abdurrahmangazi, Fatih Blv. No:67, Sultanbeyli
Author: Ahmet Erdem
Review: I came for the first time and the iced cappuccino I bought was neither iced nor cold, it was lukewarm. Even though we were at the front of the queue, the cashier took the customer behind us and said nothing. While the coffees were being prepared, the presentation was bad, there was no smiling face, the employees were completely sullen, it was a bad experience.

Name: Tea Shop
Address: Abdurrahmangazi, Fatih Blv. No:67, Sultanbeyli
Author: FS12
Review: I really like the minimalist decor of the tea shop, but my experience today was disappointing. The filter coffee I ordered was the worst I've ever had. In addition, a chair was broken, which posed a safety risk. I hope management will address these issues to provide better experiences for future customers.

Name: Tea Shop
Address: Abdurrahmangazi, Fatih Blv. No:67, Sultanbeyli
Author: Emre Akyüz
Review: First of all, as seen in the photo, I do not recommend drinking anything from a glass cup or consuming anything that requires washing up, because obviously they are not washed. Coffee and other products may be preferred as standard. There is also a section suitable for working, so you can choose it comfortably (but buy your coffee in plastic cups).

Name: Tea Shop
Address: Kocatepe, Kocatepe Mah. Şehir Parkı Cad. 12. Sok., Yıldırım, Bayrampaşa
Author: Turgut Demir
Review: Very dirty and expensive

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Vişnezade, Süleyman Seba Cd. No: 76/B, Beşiktaş
Author: shiva Hemmat
Review: Absolutely love this place ! The team is fantastic—friendly, helpful, and creates a peaceful vibe. The place is spotless, and the diverse range of delicious bubble tea options is a game-changer. A must-visit spot for bubble tea lovers!

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Vişnezade, Süleyman Seba Cd. No: 76/B, Beşiktaş
Author: Cheryl Soh
Review: I have to says it’s the best and closest to authentic bubble tea I’ve had in Turkey thus far. Prices are also very reasonable, as of March 2022 basic bubble teas are 20-23TL, and additional 3TL for pearls. But their bubble teas can be a little sweet even though i opted for 30% sugar which is the second lowest option. Their brown sugar bubble tea is good!

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Vişnezade, Süleyman Seba Cd. No: 76/B, Beşiktaş
Author: Fatimah Mo
Review:
The place exceeded my expectations
This is the first boba shop i go to in turkey so i cant really compare it to other boba shops here but it does feel like asian boba shop in far east asia

Overall experience:9/10
Drinks: we had two
Brown sugar boba : 8.5/10
The blue drink : 8/10

The Ratings are my personal preference, but they are equally delicious and well-made

Hygiene : 8/10
Hospitality:7/10 friendly in general but one of them was mean when we tried to take some photos .. we understand that they dont prefer having photos taken while they prepare the order but it would’ve been nice if he said it in another way
And therefore i wont post them

Prices: 8/10 very affordable we had the brown sugar boba in tall and the blue one in medium both total around 5$

Ps. They charge more if you take your drink with no ice … because they would have to use more ingredients

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Vişnezade, Süleyman Seba Cd. No: 76/B, Beşiktaş
Author: Marsela Qevani
Review:
Best boba in İstanbul and best costumer service.
Please bring avocado as everytime i come i ask for the avocado strawberry drink but its been months and there is no avocado.
The Atmosphere is quiet but no air conditioning, please fix this issue so we can stay more at the place.

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Vişnezade, Süleyman Seba Cd. No: 76/B, Beşiktaş
Author: Kazem Ghazaleh
Review: I am surprised at the 4 star rating, the place looks very nice and modern, staff was welcoming and gave good information about the products, and the dirty brown sugar I had was very good, I really liked the place and will be coming back soon

Name: Cofy Tea
Address: Osmanağa, No:, Pavlonya Sk. No:15, Kadıköy
Author: Jim Bennett
Review:
This is a traditional Turkish  coffee shop where games are played. Frequented by a dedicated group of locals, this Kahvehane (forgive the spelling) serves tea and coffee whilst giving you the opportunity to play Okey and Backgammon.
It's a genuine part of authentic  Istanbul culture and history. Nice guys.

Name: Cofy Tea
Address: Osmanağa, No:, Pavlonya Sk. No:15, Kadıköy
Author: Alon Z
Review: Nice and really trying staff although the language barrier was a bit challenging,quite decent coffee and nice place to seat outside

Name: Cofy Tea
Address: Osmanağa, No:, Pavlonya Sk. No:15, Kadıköy
Author: Tuna Emek
Review: It's a terrible place. I don't recommend you go. It does not suit Kadıköy.

Name: Cofy Tea
Address: Osmanağa, No:, Pavlonya Sk. No:15, Kadıköy
Author: Doğukan Konak
Review: Not much of interest but their coffee was good

Name: Cofy Tea
Address: Osmanağa, No:, Pavlonya Sk. No:15, Kadıköy
Author: Ertan Hekimler
Review: Their environment is very cute. Their tea is very nice. A nice environment to come with family and friends. Prices are average. Suitable. It is recommended.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Carmel Saleh
Review: absolutely terrible experience. i entered and was waiting for about 10 minutes and no one had taken my order. so naturally, i went up to the cashier and asked her to take it and she informed me that she was “busy” with other customers. keep in mind that there were literally only TWO other people together who already had their orders and there was another service provider aside from her. i waited another 10 minutes and when she finally came to take my order she informed me that there were no tapioca pearls and it would be ready after 20 minutes. as a boba store, how do you not have enough of the main thing people come to get? aside from that, had she simply taken my order before i wouldn’t have had to wait 20 minutes only to be told that they had no tapioca pearls. extremely unprofessional and inefficient. wish that there was a zero star option.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Maya
Review: ive been here a couple times and i want to come here more!! i love the matcha drinks and bubble tea, also the mochi is authentically made (not with icecream). i saw a couple reviews saying the mochi was bad and hard to eat, which i find hard to believe!! the lady who owns the place handmakes every mochi herself and makes it the authentic way. the mochi is stretchy soft and has a really nice texture.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Mehray C
Review: The milk tea was very good, but the new waiter didn't seem to be very attentive. I ordered red bean milk tea and was given pearl milk tea. Before I finished my drink, the waiter was talking on the phone while trying to take my drink away, and asked me "can I take it away" after putting the trash in the cup. Taste is fine, service is not!

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: osama
Review:
Hands down the best bubble tea shop in istanbul!
So many drink options and great authentic made mochi, should definitely try matcha here because it’s professionally made.
The owner is the nicest and friendly staff.

Name: Tea Taste 茶知味
Address: Sinanpaşa mahallesi şehit dursun bakan sokak 14A Sokak, D:14A, Beşiktaş
Author: Sude CAL
Review: I loved the mochi, tried the oreo one, a little basic and too sweet but it was exactly what i was expecting. Boba tea, its not my cup of tea but my friend liked it.

Name: Bubble King Tea Shop
Address: Mecidiye, Müverrih Sadettin Sk. No:2/A, Ortaköy
Author: Shaxos Travel
Review:
Drinks: Banana milk tea, coconut milk tea and Lychee fruit tea. All 3 were deeeelicious

Service: Excellent, the last who served us was warm, friendly and genuine.

Atmosphere: The shop itself is small but looks like its more for drinks to go.

Overall highly recommended and prices reasonable.

Shaxos Travel on IG and YT

Name: Bubble King Tea Shop
Address: Mecidiye, Müverrih Sadettin Sk. No:2/A, Ortaköy
Author: Bartu Hunduroglu
Review: The first time I tried bubble tea, was amazing! I tried the melon one for the first and second time as I remember and that was absolutely tasteful. But third and last time, I tried coconut milk and I can't even describe how delicious it was! You just have to try. The owner lady is always friendly to you. Cheers!🙏

Name: Bubble King Tea Shop
Address: Mecidiye, Müverrih Sadettin Sk. No:2/A, Ortaköy
Author: Turkey Consultants
Review: Arguably the best bubble tea in Istanbul. The bubble balls are made of tapioca flour. The taste of most of their flavors is great.

Name: Bubble King Tea Shop
Address: Mecidiye, Müverrih Sadettin Sk. No:2/A, Ortaköy
Author: C L
Review: I'm from Taiwan 🇹🇼 and this is the first time I taste the real flavor of home here in Istanbul. The owner is Chinese Thai and she's very kind and talkative. Thank you it was a lovely experience. Strongly recommend

Name: Bubble King Tea Shop
Address: Mecidiye, Müverrih Sadettin Sk. No:2/A, Ortaköy
Author: BemyBell _
Review: I would highly recommend both administration and taste from my experience of trying bubble tea in all of istanbul, I rate this shop as the best I really like it and will definitely come back there again and the price is affordable the price is worth with what u get must try!!

Name: Bubble Tea Tapyoka AsianFoodStation
Address: Arap Cami, Makbul İbrahim Paşa Cami, Dikişçi Sk., Beyoğlu
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Tea Shop Metropol
Address: Atatürk Mah. Ertuğrul Gazi Sok. No:2DC/3, E2 Blok Metropol AVM, Ataşehir
Author: Efe Öztürk
Review: You can also join with your pets. Everybody smiles and all the personals are grateful. Thanks for everything.

Name: Tea Shop Metropol
Address: Atatürk Mah. Ertuğrul Gazi Sok. No:2DC/3, E2 Blok Metropol AVM, Ataşehir
Author: Mehmet Kurtaran
Review: Tea & Coffee 👍

Name: Tea Shop Metropol
Address: Atatürk Mah. Ertuğrul Gazi Sok. No:2DC/3, E2 Blok Metropol AVM, Ataşehir
Author: Ece Çakay
Review: It has a clean room, a warm and dynamic staff, fun and good taste in music.

Name: Tea Shop Metropol
Address: Atatürk Mah. Ertuğrul Gazi Sok. No:2DC/3, E2 Blok Metropol AVM, Ataşehir
Author: Yiğit Demir
Review: It's a quiet, relaxing environment and the atmosphere is very good.

Name: Tea Shop Metropol
Address: Atatürk Mah. Ertuğrul Gazi Sok. No:2DC/3, E2 Blok Metropol AVM, Ataşehir
Author: benim benim
Review: A very successful business, very high quality service, excellent workmanship, congratulations

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Cevizlik Mahallesi, Morsümbül Sokak, Morsümbül Apt, No: 1/3A, Bakırköy
Author: Sema Guler
Review:
Disappointing drinks & service.
We ordered the English Milk Tea which basically tasted like milk with no tea & we ordered the strawberry matcha latte which just tasted like strawberry milk. Looks nothing like the pics on the menu.

The cashier lady asked if we had matcha before which we have had numerous times but what they served was not matcha, it was strawberry milk.

The cashier lady was also rude & cold and talks to you like she’s looking down at you.. almost wanted to not order our drinks.

Do not recommend.

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Cevizlik Mahallesi, Morsümbül Sokak, Morsümbül Apt, No: 1/3A, Bakırköy
Author: Rawand
Review: The brown sugar milk tastes like normal milk so bland and ugly. The chai one is good not that good but good. Basically overrated and wont be trying again 🤙🏻

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Cevizlik Mahallesi, Morsümbül Sokak, Morsümbül Apt, No: 1/3A, Bakırköy
Author: Israa AlBargoothy
Review: "Monster" is a fantastic spot for bubble tea enthusiasts. The Brown Sugar Bubble Tea was rich and flavorful, perfectly balanced with just the right amount of sweetness. The Matcha was smooth and authentic, a true delight for matcha lovers. The ambiance was inviting, and the service was friendly. I'll definitely be returning for more of their delightful beverages.

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Cevizlik Mahallesi, Morsümbül Sokak, Morsümbül Apt, No: 1/3A, Bakırköy
Author: Liz Pupa
Review: i always get my boba from here and it always tastes great. my favourite flavors are avocado, taro, brown sugar and banana. (they never have avocado though)  i do not recommend matcha and blue aegean sea.

Name: Monster Teashop
Address: Cevizlik Mahallesi, Morsümbül Sokak, Morsümbül Apt, No: 1/3A, Bakırköy
Author: Alaa Dallah
Review: The place decore is simple modern. The service is nice, the staff are friendly and speak english too. The music was loud. Our drink took some time because the boba were still cooking so the employee put double boba for us as an apology.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Jade Gao
Review:
Very rude staffs, I paid 10% service charges but didn’t get the equivalent service:
1. We asked the man staff if it’s possible to add some hot water in the tea pot, the staff asked back: you want a tea or hot water? After we answered hot water, he said no… since no hot water option why have to ask back?
2. We used restaurant’s toilet for a second time got a female staff followed, she suspected and tried to prevent us go to the toilet, even we told her we paid in the tea house with 10% “sevice” charge already, she still asked us with a rude tongue like: did you?
The environment is ok, tea is too bitter with little water which can’t add more, makes the tea flavor even worse, not worthy at all.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Samer Mohd
Review: Very nice place to sit relax and have some tea, coffee and desserts. After long walks around the market, this is the perfect open space area to sit. Highly recommend visiting this place. Very nice and clean with good service. They have mini boutique shops inside as well. Nice sitting area and nice ambiance. Wide selection of teas and desserts. Must visit if around the area of misr market and grand bazaar.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Ammar Salman
Review: Nice vibe and good set-up.

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Yusri Sallim
Review:
Nice ambient in the area despite the buzzling area external of the building

The macarons never fail to appease me. The taste was just nice without heavy on the sugar. Shd go for the pistachio dream cake and all in all go for the chai to quench the sugary taste..haha at least you do not have to feel guilty to taking on all the desserts

Name: Beta Tea House
Address: Rüstem Paşa, Tahmis Sk., Fatih
Author: Julian Asher
Review:
Very special tea house right in the centre of the busy area
This is a genui oasis tucked in the shopping lanes by the tourist area. Worth a visit to escape the busy streets. Good tea coffee and food and drinks. Served to the table and QR code to help show the menu to all international travellers.
Lots of choices and a great place to have a tea or cold drink in the hot summer.

Name: TEAYATRO Tea And Art Shop
Address: Sinanpaşa, İlhan Sk. 18A, Beşiktaş
Author: Sacha D
Review: Very nice owner who made delicious food and let us try some more turkish specialities. Calm and green place to enjoy some sun.

Name: TEAYATRO Tea And Art Shop
Address: Sinanpaşa, İlhan Sk. 18A, Beşiktaş
Author: Rolf Wiedenbrück
Review: This is a wonderfull Restaurant with a greatfull garden. If you want enjoy a good Coffee ore Tea and cake with friends ore alone you must go here. Wonderfull and friendly Personal and Service. I give the 5 Stars only why i cant give more. Normaly i will guve 10 Stars . Friendly greetings to the peoples from Rolf.

Name: TEAYATRO Tea And Art Shop
Address: Sinanpaşa, İlhan Sk. 18A, Beşiktaş
Author: Arvid
Review: Best study place in Beşiktaş, beautiful garden and a bit hidden

Name: TEAYATRO Tea And Art Shop
Address: Sinanpaşa, İlhan Sk. 18A, Beşiktaş
Author: Nicky Dekker
Review: Didn't eat here but the tea was good. However you definitely come here for the cottage core garden seating area. Great vibes. And at night it comes to life with fairy lights.

Name: TEAYATRO Tea And Art Shop
Address: Sinanpaşa, İlhan Sk. 18A, Beşiktaş
Author: Pragnya Sharma
Review: Good place for a tea lover. They have cozy seats and a good variety of herbs that they know how to blend to make amazing teas. I'd recommend you spend some time at the counter choosing your herbs for a great experience. Staff is nice and helpful.

Name: Tea Time
Address: Aydınevler, Siteler Yolu No:28, Maltepe
Author: Sherif AbouTaleb
Review:
Very nice place to have Turkish coffee or some drinks, location at the mall rooftop.
Friendly staff and nice atmosphere.
Highly recommended.

Name: Tea Time
Address: Aydınevler, Siteler Yolu No:28, Maltepe
Author: Michaël Kiss
Review: Excellent cakes and even better coffee. Great for a quick stop between all your shopping.

Name: Tea Time
Address: Aydınevler, Siteler Yolu No:28, Maltepe
Author: Glr Erg
Review: In short, it's a disgrace, you want a menu, they don't give you a menu, they already made a price list in a secluded corner, you sit in the first place, after all, since it's a cafe, you don't get up and know the prices inside the cash register, we paid 165 TL for the buffet lemonade, half of it is ice, half a glass of water, 2 pieces in total is 330 TL, it's like a joke. No one will leave for the second time, they are trying to slap everyone once, it's a pity, I hope you go bankrupt soon.

Name: Tea Time
Address: Aydınevler, Siteler Yolu No:28, Maltepe
Author: Ugur Fevri
Review: My father had a heart attack. I thought I'd go out and take it around. We entered this place so that he wouldn't wander around too much. When the bill came, the man would have a heart attack again. I paid the bill without revealing it. Now, I won't say the prices are expensive, but you can eat and drink the same things for the same prices on the upper floor. Prices are high for this place. Unnecessary.

Name: Tea Time
Address: Aydınevler, Siteler Yolu No:28, Maltepe
Author: Gözde Gürsel
Review: It used to be a very beautiful place. The atmosphere is the same. We drank tea, the tea was really delicious and enjoyable, but we ordered cheesecake and the bill for 2 teas and 1 cheesecake was around 160 TL. It was a bit shocking :)

Name: Tea and More Cafe / Tea&More
Address: Yenibosna Merkez, Starcity avm
Author: Deari Kadr
Review: Great 😊

Name: Tea and More Cafe / Tea&More
Address: Yenibosna Merkez, Starcity avm
Author: Isil Somyurek
Review: A business that says instant coffee has to be made with milk, we cannot serve it plain 🤣 we experienced it ourselves today.

Name: Tea and More Cafe / Tea&More
Address: Yenibosna Merkez, Starcity avm
Author: Basel Daghstani (Şamil Dağıstanlı)
Review:
Beautiful and comfortable terrace overlooking the public road and the surrounding residential area.
fast service
And delicious taste.

Distinct from the rest of the mall's restaurants and cafes.

Name: Tea and More Cafe / Tea&More
Address: Yenibosna Merkez, Starcity avm
Author: ABDULSALAM ALSAQR
Review: A wonderful cafe and the staff are friendly and excellent 😍

Name: Tea and More Cafe / Tea&More
Address: Yenibosna Merkez, Starcity avm
Author: Görünmez Anam
Review: Immaculate Tea is also fresh and delicious, thank you guys 👍👍👍

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Nathoma A
Review: Cute small place to fill your tea cravings. The tea was lovely. And a very nice and honest owner.

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Daniel Rozen
Review: Highly reccomended for tourist. True local tea spot with local prices and really good tea. Try it!

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Photo Journalist
Review:
Nice small neighborhood tea/cay joint (as it seems like most cay places in Turkey are for men only) in case you are a woman and mixed group just up the hill a few more stores and enjoy the "Köşk Boşnak Böregi ve Tatli" restaurant for quick and cheap meal with tea/cay.
If I’ve helped, thank you for the “like”!

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Ondřej Štěpán
Review: We hid from the rain at the owner's place and it was the best decision. His tea and coffee were the best we had in Istanbul.

Name: ÖZGÜN TEA HOUSE
Address: Alemdar, Alayköşkü Cd. No:17, Fatih
Author: Maximilian Lösel
Review: Really honest and pleasant shop for a simple, very good çay!

Name: Tea Amo Sirkeci
Address: Hobyar, Mimar Kemalettin Cd. Konyalı Han No: 1A, Fatih
Author: Betül Duman
Review: The best boba tea in İstanbul 🤌 Especially, you should try the caramel tea with boba! Prices are reasonable and not over, especially in İstanbul. You should try this place 👑

Name: Tea Amo Sirkeci
Address: Hobyar, Mimar Kemalettin Cd. Konyalı Han No: 1A, Fatih
Author: Trang Nguyễn
Review:
After several days eating Turkish foods, I was happy to have found Tea Amo. The staffs were friendly and the foods were tasty, I liked the fried rice with shrimp and the bubble tea. The venue seemed a bit small and the arrangement seems to be not very logical.
There were only one table for 2 people, other table are for quite big group from 4 people, which made it a bit difficult to find a place for yourselves if you don’t want to share the table with strangers. I think they could save a lot of spaces and have better arrangement.
Price is quite cheap. I paid around 2,5€ for a cup of bubble tea.
They ran out of bubble peach milk unfortunately.

Name: Tea Amo Sirkeci
Address: Hobyar, Mimar Kemalettin Cd. Konyalı Han No: 1A, Fatih
Author: Grandmaster
Review:
Nice atmosphere, cozy interior.
Variety of choice of tea.
Tea is tasty, but too much ice, I understand the retailer wants use less of ingredients, but drink can’t contain 75-80% of ice. I would never buy again

Name: Tea Amo Sirkeci
Address: Hobyar, Mimar Kemalettin Cd. Konyalı Han No: 1A, Fatih
Author: Asisten Liburan Travel Consultant
Review:
Glad found this hidden gem in Sirkeci area !

Many guests are from South East Asia, because the food & drinks taste like home !

Comfortable seating area with many electric plug, perfect for working or just hang out with friends.

Will be back again next time

Name: Tea Amo Sirkeci
Address: Hobyar, Mimar Kemalettin Cd. Konyalı Han No: 1A, Fatih
Author: Rina M
Review:
last time i went to turkey with my family and my tour guide fudhail bring me to eat here sebab dia kata portion besar and best luckily, yes the portion is big and affordable for students, i love their chicken gumush sebab sedap! Atmosphere so far so good, love the services and staff were friendly! I would recommend to my friend next time if they visit Istanbul 👍🏻👍🏻

Name: Hep Coffee and Tea
Address: Cumhuriyet, Abide-i Hürriyet Cd no:22, Şişli
Author: Nizar Akkawi
Review: Hep Coffee and Tea in Istanbul offers a delightful escape from the bustling streets, with its serene ambiance and aromatic brews. From the moment you step in, you're greeted by friendly staff who are passionate about their craft. The menu boasts a variety of coffee and tea options, each expertly prepared and bursting with flavor. Whether you're a coffee aficionado or a tea enthusiast, there's something for everyone here. The cozy atmosphere invites you to linger, making it the perfect spot to catch up with friends or simply unwind with a good book. With its impeccable service and exceptional beverages, Hep Coffee and Tea is a must-visit destination for any caffeine connoisseur in Istanbul.

Name: Hep Coffee and Tea
Address: Cumhuriyet, Abide-i Hürriyet Cd no:22, Şişli
Author: Hugo Sugg
Review: Really beautiful decor with very good cappuccino and chilled music! Very reasonable price at 80tl and a hidden gem just one metro stop away from hectic Taksim. I recommend if you want a caffeine hit.

Name: Hep Coffee and Tea
Address: Cumhuriyet, Abide-i Hürriyet Cd no:22, Şişli
Author: Ari Zingillioglu
Review:
Ordered a Cappuccino in my personal mug for take away. Quite quick service. It was a Friday evening and it wasn’t too busy.

Plenty of indoor and outdoor seating option. Looked very convenient and spacious place to me even though I was in a hurry. The place is spaciously designed with nice plant and wall decoration.

The milk temperature of the coffee drink was a bit hot. The milk could be less boiled. Other than this little detail, the coffee was good.

I also noticed the pre-grinded Turkish Coffee jar. To have a better fresh Turkish coffee taste, the beans should have been kept in a sealed jar or a sealed bag, and to be grinded right before cooking the coffee.

Reasonable pricing for all coffee drinks.

Overall rating 4.4/5 for a coffee shop.

Name: Hep Coffee and Tea
Address: Cumhuriyet, Abide-i Hürriyet Cd no:22, Şişli

Review: I like the hotel and the cafe. I have been here several times. I love the location. The atmosphere of the cafe is nice.

Review: So nice and elegant ! The ambiance is chill, the coffee is great, it is possible to come and work calmly ! Keep in mind guys that it is self service :) I'm still not use to go and bring my coffee to my table when the waiter is done preparing, I guess it's a Turkish way of Life. Author: Marcus Hooper
Review: Nail Baba is the most wonderful old world unpretentious teahouse right in the middle of the kitschy tourist hell of Sultanahmet. We stumbled upon it by accident.  Not only was the coffee superb but it was like the prices hadn't gone up in 12 months, which in Istanbul is significant. The owner was a genuinely beautiful man with the kind of gentle Turkish charm that is far less common these days.

Name: Nail Baba Tea House
Address: Alemdar, Ticarethane Sk. No:14/A, Fatih

Although he spoke very little English, it didn't stop him from having conversation with us with some help from others translating. The tea was great and we hope to come back again some day.

Name: Poppin' Bubble Tea & Coffee - Suadiye
Address: Bağımsız Bölüm, Suadiye mah. Ülkü sok. No:13-15A 26, Kadıköy
Author: Rafaël Vera Evertman
Review:
It’s a lovely tiny bar to drink good boba at. The atmosphere is nice and the service is amazing!

I got myself a Brown Sugar Boba as usual. The price for a large drink was very reasonable compared to what I’m used to. The taste of it the milk was something I needed to get used to, since it was already sweet instead of fresh milk being used. Also no brown sugar syrup is used as shown in the picture. The tapioca and coffee jelly were nice and flavorful.

Service wise, at one of the competitions I got  way too much ice so my Turkish friend explained that I wanted a bit less than normal. I think the person behind the counter misunderstood since I got no ice. This made the milk a bit lukewarm, which I disliked halfway through my drink. My friend asked to put 2 cubes inside but she also refilled the milk to make it pretty much a new drink! So very good service!!

I wasn’t a big fan of the milk so 4/5 stars, but I’m sure their popping boba will taste good as well.

Name: Poppin' Bubble Tea & Coffee - Suadiye
Address: Bağımsız Bölüm, Suadiye mah. Ülkü sok. No:13-15A 26, Kadıköy
Author: Blueford
Review: Okay off the bat, this store is very cute. There is so many spaces to take pictures, which is a bonus in my book. I really liked how they named the drinks off of celebrities! Like there was Michael Jackson, Bob Marley, and many more. The drink I got was the Beyonce, because the barista told me it was sweet. He was right the bubble tea was very sweet. To me it tasted like cotton candy. The variety of popping tapioca was very good. The only problem was the tapioca pearls where still cooking when i arrived, but I guess i cant blame them for that because i did go at 12:00 the exact time the store opened. If i could do anything differently it would be to go there at around 1:00 so everything would be prepared.

Name: Poppin' Bubble Tea & Coffee - Suadiye
Address: Bağımsız Bölüm, Suadiye mah. Ülkü sok. No:13-15A 26, Kadıköy

Review: Celebrity named drinks. Creature comforts. Mochi. Yet another place that feels like home.

The chocolate served with Turkish coffee tastes extremely bad. It may be better if it is not given at all.

The place becomes a place for the sake of the Barista friends (Semih, Hüseyin) who do their job very well. Thanks to them, the place has regular customers.
