Author: nu
Review:
We came here just to buy the asian things, but turn out we’re having our iftar here, and we’ve ordered less than 20 minutes i guess and the food is ready! alsooo we gave 5 stars cause the owner was friendly and kindhearted, anyway their food also delicious!! gonna repeat here again! luvvv the ayam geprek cause it’s sambal wasn’t tooo spicy and tasty!!

-nasi ayam geprek
-nasi padang
-matcha

so anyone from indonesia, malaysia and others want to try indofood you can try this restaurant! kedai restoran melayu that very highly recommended.

thankyou mba devi 🥰

Name: ASIAN RESTO & BUBBLE TEA (indonesian restaurant)
Address: Turna Sokak No:3A İnönü Mah. Elmadağ, Şişli
Author: Mustaeen
Review:
My first time trying Indonesian cuisine but the staff explained the menu quite well that helped me order the food I wanted.

Now, let's talk about the food - an exquisite blend of flavors that left me craving more. The Batagor, a traditional dish of fried fish dumplings with peanut sauce, was a delightful explosion of textures and spices. Each bite was a symphony of crispy exterior and tender, flavorful filling.

Equally impressive was the Nasi Rendang, a classic Indonesian dish of slow-cooked beef in rich, aromatic spices served with boiled rice. The tender meat, infused with layers of complex flavors, melted in my mouth, leaving me completely satisfied yet longing for another serving.

Name: ASIAN RESTO & BUBBLE TEA (indonesian restaurant)
Address: Turna Sokak No:3A İnönü Mah. Elmadağ, Şişli
Author: Bonnie Stevenson
Review: Super tasty Indonesian food and really lovely owners. We stayed nearby so we went back for dinner 3 times during our trip and each time loved our meals! Highly recommend this place! The fried Chicken noodles and the chicken satay were my favourite, but I also had the chicken japchae and chicken fried rice and both were super yummy.

Name: ASIAN RESTO & BUBBLE TEA (indonesian restaurant)
Address: Turna Sokak No:3A İnönü Mah. Elmadağ, Şişli
Author: Russell H
Review:
This place was amazing. We went in not knowing what to expect, and found a homey, friendly, and delicious place! Demi the chef served up some classic Indonesian food like beef rendang, nasi goreng (fried rice), and a side of fried chicken. Because it was rainy I ordered a tummy warming clear beef broth. Also I can’t forget the crispy fried tofu with scallions! Amazing!

All the food was served hot and fresh! I have to make a comment about the “salsa” or sambal.  anything it touched turn into spicy gold! Seriously so much flavor and spice in one teaspoon.

This place also doubles as an Indonesian market with tofu, fish sauce, sweet soy sauce, milk tea packets, pandang, etc…I am sure some of the southeast Asians feel nostalgia vibes walking in there.

Finally, a comment about service: the server and Demi the chef were very friendly and open about their business. We got to chat with them a little afterwards about the local Indonesian community of restos in the area.

The prices were amazingly and surprisingly low given the runaway inflation rates and rising rent. I don’t know how they do it but they manage to keep the home cooked foods at home cooked prices.

We will definitely be back!

Name: ASIAN RESTO & BUBBLE TEA (indonesian restaurant)
Address: Turna Sokak No:3A İnönü Mah. Elmadağ, Şişli
Author: Vladimir Zarodysh
Review: It's very tasty and relatively affordable food considering the fact that the restaurant is located very close to Taksim Metro station. Everything was super fresh and enjoyable. Amazing combinations of flavor. The owner was also very polite and friendly :) Don't hesitate to check out this restaurant.

Name: Mama Ram Spice & Tea Shop

Price/quality wise very good!! If I’m ever back in Istanbul there is a high chance of me coming back!

Address: Caddebostan, Caddebostan İskele Sk. No:12/a, Kadıköy

Review:
Yum 🤤
One of the best boba tea places in Istanbul (and Türkiye!) — I’m so happy we have an authentic bubble tea place near Bagdat!

The place is spacious (lots of space inside and an outdoor terrace), the menu is huge, and the decor is modern.

We ordered the classic Black Sugar boba and immediately after we tried it, we went back and ordered another drink (British Tea with cream foam). Both were delicious, had just the right amount of sweetness, and were so flavorful!

The boba bubbles are perfect - chewy and not too sweet. The boba baristas (?) definitely know what they’re doing!

I highly recommend!! It’s a great place to stop and relax :)

How can a boba place not have boba? There is an incredibly long line, and everything is really slow. Personnel doesn’t speak English at all, could barely explain anything, even though the menu is in English. It stands in no comparison with what you get in the US.

Address: Reşitpaşa, Kongre Cd. 81/A, Sarıyer
Author: Pinky Cat
Review:
(replied back to owner at the bottom.)
Terrible TASTE, Terrible SERVICE and fake.

If I could I would give it a zero. Big liars. I went there for bubble tea. It was horrible. It was around 20 lira, which even in London is cheaper than that. Literally he gave me cold tea with popping bubbles that tasted rotten. And no milk. Just cold tea. I'm baffled tbh. I had bubble tea from professional Asian place and know what it's supposed to taste like. Who gives bubble tea with no milk? No toppings no sugar options. Nothing. The place is so far and barely found a place to park the car.

Oh and as I went in, I asked if they sell bubble tea and they laughed talking to each other saying 'another one for bubble tea.' Who talks like that in the presence of customers?

Edit after owner reply:

I really don't want to make this personal but you mentioned in your reply that you recommend the tapioca with iced tea?? Lol I wasn't even offered that recommendation, and did you just say that's 'certainly not bubble tea'?? I'm seriously triggered right now... Why do you say you have bubble tea then? If its not? I went in and asked if they have bubble tea! And he said yes, and I certainly didn't ask for tapioca with iced tea. I asked for bubble tea, and if you don't have it, don't give me something else. Im a shy person so I gave the money and left. I'm not stupid for you to reply with such a reply making it sound you're not at fault. A sorry would have been sufficient. And no, i won't be visiting you soon thank you very much. Lol being laughed at by ur staff once is enough.

Address: Cihangir, Beylikdüzü Osb/Avcılar
Author: Balkan Sağlam
Review: I drank the best moccha of my life from my friend's hand....number 10...

Name: Caffe De'Moccha Tea Coffee Shop
Address: Cihangir, Beylikdüzü Osb/Avcılar
Author: Kartal Yuvada
Review: The place is as beautiful as its name. It is a place with a bright future.

Name: Caffe De'Moccha Tea Coffee Shop
Address: Cihangir, Beylikdüzü Osb/Avcılar
Author: Huriye Oğus
Review: Great place for a tea and coffee break

Name: Caffe De'Moccha Tea Coffee Shop
Address: Cihangir, Beylikdüzü Osb/Avcılar
Author: Çağdaş
Review: I loved your toasts, master MUSA...

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Cevizli, Tugay Yolu Cd. No:69 Piazza, Kat:0, Maltepe
Author: Saniye Uyar
Review: Passion fruit (:

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Cevizli, Tugay Yolu Cd. No:69 Piazza, Kat:0, Maltepe
Author: Traveller
Review: Since we've never tried it before, we were curious and bought it. It is very nice, but the mixture we prepared seemed too sugary to me. Its straws are also huge, it is very enjoyable to drink and blow bubbles in your mouth. I recommend ☺️

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Cevizli, Tugay Yolu Cd. No:69 Piazza, Kat:0, Maltepe
Author: Aysse Uyar
Review: I've had it before in different places, but I haven't tried it from you. The friend at the cash register must have noticed my inexperience and asked me questions and made me taste the balls in order to prepare them according to my taste. Thank you very much, I liked it very much

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Cevizli, Tugay Yolu Cd. No:69 Piazza, Kat:0, Maltepe
Author: Barış Ayna
Review: One of your wonderful qualities is that you prepare chewy buns by hand daily. I think your product range is suitable for everyone looking for a different taste.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Cevizli, Tugay Yolu Cd. No:69 Piazza, Kat:0, Maltepe
Author: Wiskicoklet
Review: I loved the tapioca, I come and drink it every day, the service is top notch, they change it if you don't like it, but they asked me questions beforehand and suggested the flavor that appealed to my taste, so there was no need to change it, I loved it.

Name: Moncai Bubble Tea - Nidapark
Address: Çınar, Nidapark Küçükyalı Mahalle B06, Rıfkı Tongsir Cd. NO:113 Faa, Maltepe
Author: Sevde Cebeci
Review: My boyfriend and I tried taro milk for the first time and we liked it very much. The employees were also very friendly and warm-hearted. Thank you. We will definitely come back to try the others🥰

Name: Moncai Bubble Tea - Nidapark
Address: Çınar, Nidapark Küçükyalı Mahalle B06, Rıfkı Tongsir Cd. NO:113 Faa, Maltepe
Author: sercan cantürk
Review: Friendly staff and an architectural wonder. We came with my wife and siblings to a friendly atmosphere. Bubble Teas are already incredible. We tried chocolate, strawberry, mango and taro milk and liked it 👍🏽

Name: Moncai Bubble Tea - Nidapark
Address: Çınar, Nidapark Küçükyalı Mahalle B06, Rıfkı Tongsir Cd. NO:113 Faa, Maltepe
Author: İrem CEYLAN
Review: Their taro and tapioca boba is excellent. It might be one of the best bobas I've had in Turkey. The employees are extremely kind and sweet.

Name: Moncai Bubble Tea - Nidapark
Address: Çınar, Nidapark Küçükyalı Mahalle B06, Rıfkı Tongsir Cd. NO:113 Faa, Maltepe
Author: Zeynep Tunbiş
Review: My bubble tea place, which I have been addicted to for a long time, was opened very close to my house. As always, the taste, performance and ambiance are very nice. Mr. Furkan is friendly and kind, I will always come :)

Name: Moncai Bubble Tea - Nidapark
Address: Çınar, Nidapark Küçükyalı Mahalle B06, Rıfkı Tongsir Cd. NO:113 Faa, Maltepe
Author: Dilara Cantürk
Review: I tried chewable bubble tea for the first time in Turkey. It is very delicious and has different options. Fruits are my favorite, the atmosphere is also very nice

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Boyner içi, Pınar Mahallesi, Katar Cd, İstinye Park AVM No:11, Sarıyer
Author: Marlena
Review: After searching for the perfect bubble tea around Izmir, this is the first place we've found that's as close as the real thing in Asia. With real chewy tapioca pearls and that authentic tea aftertaste. It would be great to see the addition of Taro flavour. I recommend Brown sugar in the meantime. Note that they use oat milk and only serve medium size for 125 TL, which is reasonable. It's a nice touch that they let you taste the tea before sealing it off, I got low sugar and decided to get more sugar after tasting it.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Boyner içi, Pınar Mahallesi, Katar Cd, İstinye Park AVM No:11, Sarıyer
Author: Cennet Öztürk
Review: I love subtle approaches and the little cards you distribute feel special, something sweet. The guy working at the cash register and the others are also friendly, which I think is very important. Lastly, your drinks are really delicious.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Boyner içi, Pınar Mahallesi, Katar Cd, İstinye Park AVM No:11, Sarıyer
Author: Sude Alparslan
Review: Good options for the summer, cool and sweet drinks, if you like sugary and fruity desserts, come here like me, I went with my wonderful family, they loved the food and drinks, I will come again.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Boyner içi, Pınar Mahallesi, Katar Cd, İstinye Park AVM No:11, Sarıyer
Author: Seda Özdemir
Review: They have more than one product, some of them have very intense aromas in my opinion, but there are also some that are ideal for those who like fresh tastes. It is also nice cold and cold in summer, ideal for summer.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Boyner içi, Pınar Mahallesi, Katar Cd, İstinye Park AVM No:11, Sarıyer
Author: Sevda Çeliktepe
Review: I tried it in the winter, but when I came across it again at the beginning of summer, I accidentally drank it again and I can say that I became addicted to it.

Name: Bobalogy Bubble Tea - City Center Outlet Avm
Address: Zafer, Doğan Araslı Blv. No: 79/1 D:1, Esenyurt
Author: Simge Yel Toprak

Name: Turkish Delights & Spices & Teas
Address: Beyazıt, Grand Bazaar, Yağlıkçılar Sk. No:33, Fatih
Author: Chingkhei Nongthomabam
Review: Very bad ,go scammed ,rude employees,very dirty did not like it at all

Name: Turkish Delights & Spices & Teas
Address: Beyazıt, Grand Bazaar, Yağlıkçılar Sk. No:33, Fatih
Author: Kumari dipti
Review: Must visit place if you are looking for sultan tea and there one of the special tea.

Name: Turkish Delights & Spices & Teas
Address: Beyazıt, Grand Bazaar, Yağlıkçılar Sk. No:33, Fatih
Author: Nelson C S Oliveira
Review: Amazing place to visit but a little bit expensive.

Name: Turkish Delights & Spices & Teas
Address: Beyazıt, Grand Bazaar, Yağlıkçılar Sk. No:33, Fatih
Author: Ayla
Review: The best lukum in turkey

Name: Turkish Delights & Spices & Teas
Address: Beyazıt, Grand Bazaar, Yağlıkçılar Sk. No:33, Fatih
Author: Tania Vivanco
Review: Wonderful service, very friendly, excellent quality products, I recommend stopping by! Not to be missed in the Grand Bazaar!

Name: Tea-Coffee
Address: Hobyar, Konak Han, Ankara Cd. No:13, Fatih
Author: Yavuz Maden
Review:
Nice guys with full hearts, brother.
🙂👍

Name: Tea-Coffee

Author: MUSTAFA BUĞRA ÜSTÜNDAĞ

Name: Poppin' Bubble Tea & Coffee - Göktürk
Address: Göktürk Merkez, Belediye Cd. No: 26/C, Eyüpsultan
Author: Ada
Review: It didn't have the bubble I wanted, but it was nice too.

Name: Poppin' Bubble Tea & Coffee - Göktürk

Name: Poppin' Bubble Tea & Coffee - Göktürk

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 3C İç kapı, 1.Kat, No 262, Sarıyer
Author: Abdulrahman Almunayes
Review: Inside Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, 1st floor, next to gratis. Try Caramel Bubble Tea! Also try their little Cookies.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 3C İç kapı, 1.Kat, No 262, Sarıyer
Author: Mina Ryuk
Review: Orderd two and I really like it for the taste and the worker was very sweet I went there twice and every time I see her helping out people by telling them what tastes good and also there was two kids and she helped them as well so shoutout to her

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 3C İç kapı, 1.Kat, No 262, Sarıyer
Author: Microtonal Guitar - Tolgahan Çoğulu
Review: Delicious mango bubble tea

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 3C İç kapı, 1.Kat, No 262, Sarıyer
Author: Muhammad Bukhari
Review: Boba is weird unlike normal boba (chewy black is alright rest all soggy?) Drinks are overly sugary even tho I got the least sugary option.

Name: Boble Bubble Tea
Address: Ayazağa, Vadistanbul Shopping Mall, Azerbaycan Cd. 3C İç kapı, 1.Kat, No 262, Sarıyer
Author: Ebi Djr
Review: One of the best bubble tea I had

Name: Cennet Baklava-Turkish Delight-Spice-Tea
Address: Mollafenari, divanyolu caddesi no 80, Fatih
Author: Ali Akhavan
Review: Don't purchase from this store. I bought some lokum and discovered worms inside the next day. This shop must be shut down. I have forwarded the photos to Turkish authorities. The seller says this is because their lokum is organic, what a joke!!

Name: Cennet Baklava-Turkish Delight-Spice-Tea
Address: Mollafenari, divanyolu caddesi no 80, Fatih
Author: Alzhan
Review: I do NOT recommend, soggy baklava, the fewest amount of nuts possible and also look at the picture. No idea how the rating is so high

Name: Cennet Baklava-Turkish Delight-Spice-Tea
Address: Mollafenari, divanyolu caddesi no 80, Fatih
Author: Andreea Lucaci
Review:
The most disgusting thing ever. We bought 3 boxes of turkish delight and all 3 have worms and ants on the inside. All boxes had full plastic bags over them, so the worms and ants must’ve been there from the moment we bought them. Absolutely disgusting. Waste of money. Don’t buy from here!

Update: How dare you say that? There was 3 of us who bought delight from your store that night, and all 3 different people (5 boxes of delight in total) had those disgusting creatures inside! It’s shameful that you take no responsibility whatsoever for the quality of your products! Don’t you dare blame the customer when you know that it’s your own fault! It’s easy to say that your customers don’t store them properly because it sounds good, right? As we mentioned in the private messages, your products were stored appropriately, as it is not the first time we’ve bought delight. It is however the first time that we bought worms with the delight. I truly hope that Turkish public authorities will pay attention to the quality of your products and hold you accountable for it!

Name: Cennet Baklava-Turkish Delight-Spice-Tea
Address: Mollafenari, divanyolu caddesi no 80, Fatih
Author: Gherman Adrian
Review: I recently visited this shop in Istanbul to purchase some baklava and spices. While the baklava was delicious, I was extremely disappointed and disgusted by the quality of the spices. Upon opening the packages at home, I discovered they were infested with insects and worms. This is completely unacceptable and a serious health hazard. I would not recommend buying anything from this store, especially not the spices. Quality control and hygiene standards need a major improvement here.

Name: Cennet Baklava-Turkish Delight-Spice-Tea
Address: Mollafenari, divanyolu caddesi no 80, Fatih
Author: Awkward Niqaabi
Review: I've never been too fond of Turkish delight but out of all the places I've tried Turkish delight from, this place has by far the best tasting ones. I regret not buying more. Would definitely recommend.

Name: Tos Tea
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Ortaklar Cd. 20/C, Şişli
Author: Ersagun Demirel
Review: Portions looks little. Could be more satisfied. Opens all the time. Bad day saver.

Name: Tos Tea
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Ortaklar Cd. 20/C, Şişli
Author: Sercan Arpacı
Review: Nice pace to have a bite. Good staff and service.

Name: Tos Tea
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Ortaklar Cd. 20/C, Şişli
Author: Güneş Aykaç
Review: Doesn't worth it.

Name: Tos Tea
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Ortaklar Cd. 20/C, Şişli
Author: Bülent TUNÇ
Review: I ate the Patso hot dog. The product was nice and satisfying. A caring business was clean and spacious. Personally, I am satisfied. If you want a change, I can say that there is variety in the menu.

Name: Tos Tea
Address: Mecidiyeköy, Ortaklar Cd. 20/C, Şişli
Author: Dilara Kebenc
Review: It's a place I've been coming to for breakfast for a long time. Their food is quite delicious. The staff is friendly, the service quality is very good, the prices are quite reasonable. I recommend it.

Name: O Coffee
Address: Türkali, Yeniyol Sokağı No:12/A, Beşiktaş
Author: kedicik çilek
Review: Beautiful atmosphere, and extremely kind staff. I usually have very specific and complicated coffee orders and Ali made the drinks perfectly. The staff (especially Ali) were all very sweet and respectful. One of the best cafes in Istanbul specifically due to the kindness and friendliness of the staff! Just a thanks to the O coffee team and especially Ali for such a wonderful and friendly experience! Will be returning soon and recommending the place to friends!

Name: O Coffee
Address: Türkali, Yeniyol Sokağı No:12/A, Beşiktaş
Author: Moath G
Review:
It’s a café a little bit far from the main street is not suitable for families
Very bad and slow service, especially if you don't turkey-
I asked them to turn on the air conditioner before I ordered and they agreed
So I sit, after that I told him to turn on the air conditioner he said yes but not turn on

And when he poured the coffee into the cup, he took the rest of it and did not put it on the table!! And I read many reviews about the rudeness of the cafe staff ! And I agree with it, for example, I was taking off my shoes, then he came to me and ask me take on again! How see my shoe and can’t see me waiting for the dessert and the air conditioner!!

Name: O Coffee
Address: Türkali, Yeniyol Sokağı No:12/A, Beşiktaş
Author: Nesa MS
Review: Like their coffee and place at first. Ambiance and coziness. Later their coffee made our stomach go bad. I don’t know exactly why but could be about the milk they’re using. Another problem is that they didn’t give us enough details about their coffee plus dessert campaign. So we were confused while paying the bill. I didn’t like that at all.

Name: O Coffee
Address: Türkali, Yeniyol Sokağı No:12/A, Beşiktaş
Author: Hala Alzeat
Review:
So lovely and tasty sweet!
The service is amazing too💕
Thank for the wonderful experience 🌸

Name: O Coffee
Address: Türkali, Yeniyol Sokağı No:12/A, Beşiktaş
Author: Tamara Czdgb
Review: Nicely decorated coffee shop but that is about it. I went there specifically for an ice latte and that was the worst item there - watery, no flavour, they forgot or they didn't understand I want whipped cream on top, when they brought it to the table it had no ice in it (everything had already melted), they also didn't have sweetener. Strawberry cheesecake was ok but nothing memorable. I drank only half of the coffee and I asked the waiter for a smoothie. Surprise, surprise my smoothie came with whipped cream on top and tasted milky. When I have asked what is going on because I have not ordered a milkshake the waiter said that this is how it's done in Turkeys - with yoghurt. Just shocking! I left it there and left.

Name: Coffee Department
Address: Ayvansaray, Kürkçü Çeşmesi Sk. No:5, Fatih
Author: Yousef Dablin
Review:
Wouldn’t say I was blown away although the coffee options here were relatively safe. I had two pour overs. One I finished the other I left on the table. Count drink it at all. Customer service was very bland and stale  (yes I don’t speak the lingo although, staff do understand English and coffee.)
Would I go out of my way to have a coffee here? No. Although, if I was near by I defiantly would give it a swirl.

Name: Coffee Department
Address: Ayvansaray, Kürkçü Çeşmesi Sk. No:5, Fatih
Author: Richard Smittenaar
Review: Great pour-overs. Went here about six times, and I was really impressed by how their coffee as brewed actually has all the tasting notes from the little cards that go with the beans. I could consistently taste all the elements. Great brewing.

Name: Coffee Department
Address: Ayvansaray, Kürkçü Çeşmesi Sk. No:5, Fatih
Author: Alexander Vitshas
Review: At the moment this coffee department is the best coffee shop in Istanbul we visited. Very wide selection of coffee beans to brew. We try Costa Rica, Salvador and Columbia and all of them were amazing. Very friendly baristas telling about taste and aroma we can found in  cup of fresh brewed v60. Actually ten out of ten.

Name: Coffee Department
Address: Ayvansaray, Kürkçü Çeşmesi Sk. No:5, Fatih
Author: Zeynep Demir
Review: First and foremost, I have to say that their coffee is excellent. I’ve visited twice, enjoyed a cup of coffee, and also purchased some beans, both of which I found exceptional. However, as someone who loves cats and doesn’t mind their presence in cafes, I was concerned to see a cat sitting on the dining table, which raises hygiene issues. Additionally, I was surprised to find that their coffee is priced higher than specialty coffee shops in London.

Name: Coffee Department
Address: Ayvansaray, Kürkçü Çeşmesi Sk. No:5, Fatih
Author: A n D
Review:
Amazing place and amazing service! I spilled my coffee (on my wife) and they replaced both of our coffees for free. The baristas have been doing this for a long time and they are the best in Istanbul! Highly recommended.

Update: came back again today and experienced the amazing Coffee department service and quality once again! Uğur made an amazing Japanese pour over from their Ijen Lestari beans. Highly recommend you come in and try this before it’s gone.

Name: The Rabbit Hole Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Küçük Ayasofya, Şht. Mehmetpaşa Ykş. No:48A, Fatih
Author: Eeraj Qaisar
Review: One of Istanbul's best cafes. The drinks are top notch, pastries wonderful and breakfast items fresh and flavorful. The ladies managing the show here are experts - attentive and polite. Location is wonderful and easy to get to. And the cats outside are well behaved.

Name: The Rabbit Hole Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Küçük Ayasofya, Şht. Mehmetpaşa Ykş. No:48A, Fatih
Author: Anahata Mindfulness
Review: This café was next to my rented flat and it became my little moment of calm before or/and after a day in Istanbul. Excellent service, good food and decent prices. Good music. The team is lovely and speaks English. I recommend this haven of peace.

Name: The Rabbit Hole Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Küçük Ayasofya, Şht. Mehmetpaşa Ykş. No:48A, Fatih
Author: Sadaf Azimi
Review: Excellent coffee, service and atmosphere. I came here almost every day of my trip. The coffee is large and frothy and the location of the cafe in a quiet corner next to the mosque makes for a tranquil atmosphere. The cute cats can’t be forgotten either :) The owner is especially kind and, when I asked for lemon, provided it to me free of charge every morning.

Name: The Rabbit Hole Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Küçük Ayasofya, Şht. Mehmetpaşa Ykş. No:48A, Fatih
Author: Erwin Scholz
Review:
Stumbled across this place as unfortunately Old Town Coffee closed earlier than I was awake.

Turns out the owner of Old Town Coffee was here visiting his friend as well 😄

This was a good omen.

Great little place that's worth a visit, nice people, great food and good pricing as well.

Name: The Rabbit Hole Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Küçük Ayasofya, Şht. Mehmetpaşa Ykş. No:48A, Fatih
Author: Sfabu Anton
Review: A relaxing stop, cozy atmosphere and pretty cups. We had some coffee (we are not fans of turkish coffee), some good tea and a diverse breakfast. We ate 2 people from it and it was enough.

Name: North Art Coffee & Breakfast
Address: Cankurtaran, Utangaç Sk. No:21, Fatih
Author: sonam bali
Review: We ordered Vegan breakfast, Lotto, Memling, Banana shake, Istanbul drea and Ice crazy mocha. Everything was so tasty 😋. Staff was really nice. Due to proximity of location it was bit pricey but it was worth to visit. Especially for coffee.

Name: North Art Coffee & Breakfast
Address: Cankurtaran, Utangaç Sk. No:21, Fatih
Author: James V
Review: We are here every morning for breakfast. The staff would recognize us and had very good interactions with them every morning. Amazing coffee and we had the vegetarian breakfast + added eggs to it. Basically a avacado toast with small salad and some vegetables. Would definitely visit again

Name: North Art Coffee & Breakfast
Address: Cankurtaran, Utangaç Sk. No:21, Fatih
Author: chou sei
Review: We came here several times because this shop offered late breakfast. The dishes we tried among breakfast were good, i tried pizza once which was also good. But the spaghetti were honestly very boring and pricey.

Name: North Art Coffee & Breakfast
Address: Cankurtaran, Utangaç Sk. No:21, Fatih
Author: Winona Roxanne
Review: Needed a pick me up after walking around the busy area. This cafe was a wonderful reprieve from all the hustle and bustle of the streets, nestled in a quiet and charming alleyway. The coffee were great and it was one of the best and smoothest iced Turkish coffee I’ve had thus far.

Name: North Art Coffee & Breakfast
Address: Cankurtaran, Utangaç Sk. No:21, Fatih
Author: Ciarán Butler
Review:
I really don’t get this rating… Unless it’s been artificially inflated? Over priced, slow service and honestly, dreadful food.

I had ordered the house Benedict (over fried eggs on brown bread, salty luncheon meat covered in a lumpy hollandaise (?) sauce. Apart from the fact it looked like someone had eaten it before me, then dropped from a height, it was inedible.

“Smashed” avocado is just green paste out of a packet. All egg over done. Sub par coffee.

Servers were nice enough, but one meal was served and finished in the time it took to drop down another’s order.
€75 for 4 people for an awful setting.

Name: Old Java Coffee Roasters Galata
Address: Müeyyedzade, Tatar Beyi Sk. No:8, Beyoğlu
Author: Tanu Joshi
Review: We went there for breakfast and had  crepes, omelette, and Turkey with toast. The service was quick. Coffee was one of the best in Istanbul. The atmosphere was nice and cosy. Would recommend this place.

Name: Old Java Coffee Roasters Galata
Address: Müeyyedzade, Tatar Beyi Sk. No:8, Beyoğlu
Author: Anna
Review:
Nice place for a quick coffee!

We tried a cookie and a brownie with our coffee, it was very soft and chewy, I suspect a little bit underbaked. Nice interior, the toilet could be cleaner. A bit expensive to pay 522 TRY for a coffee and 2 small snacks.

Name: Old Java Coffee Roasters Galata
Address: Müeyyedzade, Tatar Beyi Sk. No:8, Beyoğlu
Author: Christian Preseau
Review:
Based on the reviews, I was planning on coming here just for coffee. But we decided on a whim to order food as well, and I'm really glad we did: both the salads and the local dishes were excellent.

Coffee is also excellent. We tried lattes, espresso, and filter coffee, all of which were great. They also have lots of plant-based milk options, which seems to be rare in this town.

Vibe is really nice and calm. It's order at the counter, but they will bring dishes to your table if they aren't too busy. We ended up coming here multiple times during our trip, as it was a really nice and relaxing start to the day.

Name: Old Java Coffee Roasters Galata
Address: Müeyyedzade, Tatar Beyi Sk. No:8, Beyoğlu
Author: Itsme_ Natalie
Review:
Truly a great hidden gem in the neighborhood. Perfect place for breakfast with plenty of veg options and some local classics with modern twist. Excellent coffee and beans selection; I couldn’t resist but to buy some for home too. Tip: it also has beautiful packaging so it’s perfect as a gift for all coffee lovers.
Arzu, one of the owners, manager, barista all in one was absolutely lovely to talk to and she truly is a face and soul of this place. Definitely coming back on my next visit to Istanbul. Thank you for a great service and experience!
Natalia

Name: Old Java Coffee Roasters Galata
Address: Müeyyedzade, Tatar Beyi Sk. No:8, Beyoğlu
Author: Taylor Pelling
Review:
If you want artisan coffee, this is the spot. They do all varieties of coffee, they make a great flat white and can even do drip coffee if you want.

I found the cooled food items were not that good but that could just be my taste. Lovely location, nice and quiet. Sit on the terrace and enjoy saying hello to the local cats!

They even roast the coffee in the back… delightful shop!

Name: Parsa Coffee Roasters
Address: Kemankeş Karamustafa Paşa, Hoca Tahsin Sk. No:6, Beyoğlu
Author: Tracia Almeida
Review:
Coffee lovers, this is THEE destination for world class coffee.
To top it off, we threw in a twister - oat milk pls!  And they delivered with flying colours. The best in Istanbul, hands-down! Well done team Parsa! Your passion shines through your coffee mastery and your keen eye for detail. Cheers!

Name: Parsa Coffee Roasters
Address: Kemankeş Karamustafa Paşa, Hoca Tahsin Sk. No:6, Beyoğlu
Author: Paulo Sergio Benetti Junior
Review:
I stopped by and tried their filtered coffee, which I think was made with a V60, but it tasted pretty flat—like it came from a super automatic coffee machine. Hoping for better, I tried a few more things, including a brownie, but nothing really impressed

The staff wasn’t rude, but they seemed kind of bored, and the service reflected that

If you’re really hungry or in need of a quick coffee, it’ll do. But honestly, you can find much better spots nearby

Name: Parsa Coffee Roasters
Address: Kemankeş Karamustafa Paşa, Hoca Tahsin Sk. No:6, Beyoğlu
Author: Hamza
Review: The vibe here is really great, calm and relaxing, making you feel comfortable and eager to return. The location is pretty convenient, and they offer a good variety of drinks at reasonable prices, though the cake selection could be better. Overall, it's a perfect spot for hanging out with friends, working on your laptop, or just enjoying a drink.

Name: Parsa Coffee Roasters
Address: Kemankeş Karamustafa Paşa, Hoca Tahsin Sk. No:6, Beyoğlu
Author: Lorraine Monk
Review:
We've been missing our Aussie strong flat whites for months.
Who'd guess we would find an excellent bean and Barista at Parsa Coffee Roasters in Istanbul near the port.
Great flavour, strong but not burnt and HOT!!! Fantastic
Hoping to get another tomorrow.

Name: Parsa Coffee Roasters
Address: Kemankeş Karamustafa Paşa, Hoca Tahsin Sk. No:6, Beyoğlu
Author: John Keenan
Review: Coffee was good. Carrot cake was a bit dry (perhaps at least one day old/stale). Their seating is spacious for indoors & out. It's a pedestrianised street which makes for a relaxing atmosphere except for the mopeds. Prices were OK.

Name: Homestead Coffee And Company
Address: Caddebostan, Caddebostan İskele Sk. No:14 D:1A, Kadıköy
Author: Randel Hambrick
Review: Wow! This coffeehouse is perfection! Cozy and relaxing atmosphere, yummy homemade desserts, and drinks were unique and divine! Grandma’s homemade poundcake and the homemade cinnamon rolls tasted heavenly;I wish I had also tried the cheesecake! It was coming out of the oven when I was ordering and smelled amazing. The coffee menu is diverse and accommodating with various options.  I highly recommend! They also have a great space to work from or gather at.  Staff five stars.

Name: Homestead Coffee And Company
Address: Caddebostan, Caddebostan İskele Sk. No:14 D:1A, Kadıköy
Author: Arber Rozhaja
Review: Homestead Coffee and Roastery is a must-visit for any coffee lover in Istanbul! Their selection of coffee is absolutely amazing, with unique and seasonal drinks that are a real treat. I highly recommend the Autumn Shaken Espresso and the Orange Maple Flat White – both had fantastic flavors and made for a memorable coffee experience. The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends. If you're in the area, make sure to stop by for some exceptional coffee!

Name: Homestead Coffee And Company
Address: Caddebostan, Caddebostan İskele Sk. No:14 D:1A, Kadıköy
Author: King Fong
Review: Excellent café experience. It is rare to find a nice café makes you feel comfortable, with a cup of a very high quality hand brew coffee. The employees are very nice and friendly. I’d definitely come back to this place again when I come visit Istanbul!

Name: Homestead Coffee And Company
Address: Caddebostan, Caddebostan İskele Sk. No:14 D:1A, Kadıköy
Author: K
Review:
Absolutely amazing! The prices are good and the friendly service makes going here a treat. We will definitely keep coming back every time we can.
Coffee selection is top notch, they even have single origin options. We had the poor over and it was delicious ❤️ Plus, the cheese cake was sooo good! Wow.

Name: Homestead Coffee And Company
Address: Caddebostan, Caddebostan İskele Sk. No:14 D:1A, Kadıköy
Author: S Fischer
Review:
I visited Homestead Coffee last week and was pleasantly surprised by the experience. The shop is located in a quiet street and has such a cozy and modern ambiance and delicious coffee! The price was reasonable for the quality and quantity of food that I ordered.
It is a hidden gem and will definitely be back when I visit Istanbul again!

Name: Collective Coffee
Address: Ataköy 7-8-9-10, kisim, Defne sokak Yosun 18 No1f10 ic kapi, no 137, Bakırköy
Author: Zak H
Review: Their brunch quality was amazing, and their coffee is next level. I really enjoyed it. They also have amazing sweet collection that I highly recommend. If you’re into coffee, you should pay them a visit. English communication is a challenge fyi.

Name: Collective Coffee
Address: Ataköy 7-8-9-10, kisim, Defne sokak Yosun 18 No1f10 ic kapi, no 137, Bakırköy
Author: oblivion
Review: You can have a wonderful fresh breakfast dishes with egg like you go to a restaurant in New York, SoHo. It is very nice to have a different taste in Turkey. Breads, cakes and croissants are also so damn good and delicious!!

Name: Collective Coffee
Address: Ataköy 7-8-9-10, kisim, Defne sokak Yosun 18 No1f10 ic kapi, no 137, Bakırköy
Author: khalil sk
Review:
The place was nice, yummy food and nice vibes & they have specialty coffee !
It gets a bit crowded after noon.
For Photography/Videography enquiries please get in touch with me on Instagram: Khalil_Sk

Name: Collective Coffee
Address: Ataköy 7-8-9-10, kisim, Defne sokak Yosun 18 No1f10 ic kapi, no 137, Bakırköy
Author: Ipek Yilmaz
Review: Favorite coffee/breakfast place. Cozy and clean. Huge portions and there’s so many options on the menu. The staff is welcoming. Everything is freshly baked daily and the coffee tastes amazing. A hidden gem.

Name: Collective Coffee
Address: Ataköy 7-8-9-10, kisim, Defne sokak Yosun 18 No1f10 ic kapi, no 137, Bakırköy
Author: Iman
Review: Amazing, the croissants are just like the ones I had in Italy. If it was closer to home, I would definitely come everyday. I cannot wait to come again to try the other items on the menu.

Name: Coffee Baroque
Address: Bereketzade, Kuledibi, Büyük Hendek Cd. No:19 D:B, Beyoğlu
Author: Dina Nazari
Review: Has the most amazing San Sebastián cheesecake with stunning views of Galata Tower. We were served by Alayna who was lovely. She also took great pics of us by the tower. Great atmosphere, views and food, definitely recommend if you want to grab photos of the cheesecake by the tower.

Name: Coffee Baroque
Address: Bereketzade, Kuledibi, Büyük Hendek Cd. No:19 D:B, Beyoğlu
Author: Beky Boo
Review: After walking up the steep climb to the Galata Tower, we were met by the friendliest staff: Sila and Emir outside the Baroque cafe! The most delicious Turkish tea and their freshly made San Sebastián cheesecake was the most delicious one we have had in Istanbul so far! Well worth the trek up the hill, make sure you stop at this place.

Name: Coffee Baroque
Address: Bereketzade, Kuledibi, Büyük Hendek Cd. No:19 D:B, Beyoğlu
Author: Ale. 10
Review: It's a really good place with an amazing view of the Galata Tower, I just came here for the desserts but the waitress was really humble. Highly recommended!

Name: Coffee Baroque
Address: Bereketzade, Kuledibi, Büyük Hendek Cd. No:19 D:B, Beyoğlu
Author: Darshana Ratnayake
Review:
Nice small coffee shop near galata tower.   Just 30 second walk fro tour.
Lots of cakes, sweets and coffee types are available .
Very delicious, hot and nice. Price is reasonable too. staff is so much friendly and helpful .. all can speak english .

Restaurant is clean. They always play famous songs.. haha. Thats nice

Name: Coffee Baroque
Address: Bereketzade, Kuledibi, Büyük Hendek Cd. No:19 D:B, Beyoğlu
Author: wael shihadeh
Review: They speak English 👌. Good hospitality and very cosy place . Very tables available . "San-Sebastian" sweet was stunning and so delicious, especially with wirh topping 😍 ✨️ 💖 💕 ❤️ ♥️ 😍 . Recommended

Name: Story Coffee & Roastery
Address: Rasimpaşa, Halitağa Cd. no 61, Kadıköy
Author: Lina W
Review:
I work remotely full-time and I really enjoyed working from this coffee shop when I was in Istanbul!

Other customers who were here the same time I was were also working from this coffee shop. Good internet speed. Quiet atmosphere to get some work done. Comfortable seating. Dog-friendly too.

I would say the only downside is the matcha powder was not the greatest (it was bitter due to the colour).

Name: Story Coffee & Roastery
Address: Rasimpaşa, Halitağa Cd. no 61, Kadıköy
Author: Philip Berg
Review:
Wonderful coffee shop with one of the best lattes I’ve had in a long time. Baristas are warm and friendly.

Lovely patio in the back that is well shaded in the morning

Name: Story Coffee & Roastery
Address: Rasimpaşa, Halitağa Cd. no 61, Kadıköy
Author: James Gnan
Review: Story Coffee is one of my favorites. It has friendly staff, a clean environment, and excellent coffee. My favorite are the American-style cinnamon rolls. They’re truly a must have. It is almost always calm in the mornings, and has good WiFi.

Name: Story Coffee & Roastery
Address: Rasimpaşa, Halitağa Cd. no 61, Kadıköy
Author: H F
Review: Lovely coffee shop with indoor seating and a beautiful back garden. The staff were friendly and the WiFi here was quite fast and stable (a rarity in Turkey), so it was great to relax here and get some work done.

Name: Story Coffee & Roastery
Address: Rasimpaşa, Halitağa Cd. no 61, Kadıköy
Author: Hédi Aradi
Review: it is located a little further away, so when I was there fortunately  it was not so crowded, pleasant place  with a company or study as well.  The stuff are lovely, their coffee characteristic because it is also a roaster on site!  I tasted the brownie, it was a little sweet for me (matter of the taste of course) but the texture was pleasant! They also have plant base milk and cozy interiors! 🌱

Name: Coffee Manifesto
Address: Caferağa, Güneşli Bahçe Sok No:40A, Kadıköy
Author: Marc MRD
Review: The coffee is delicious. The location is in the middle of kadikoy's market so it's a wonderful location. The cafe in general is nice and recommended.

Name: Coffee Manifesto
Address: Caferağa, Güneşli Bahçe Sok No:40A, Kadıköy
Author: mark strizek
Review: Excellent coffee. Single origin done well. Excellent barista with good English, better than our Turkish. Clean toilet available.

Name: Coffee Manifesto
Address: Caferağa, Güneşli Bahçe Sok No:40A, Kadıköy
Author: Dan Rowe
Review:
Best coffee in Kadiköy, and has vegan options too.

Always has a very good range of coffee available, and the v60 pour over in particular is very good here. I also noticed they have decaf beans which is rare in Istanbul.

Update: tried the Mexican decaf v60. Very good coffee.

Name: Coffee Manifesto
Address: Caferağa, Güneşli Bahçe Sok No:40A, Kadıköy
Author: Martin McAdam
Review: Great coffee and a white chocolate cheese cake. Delightful helpful staff. There is a clean toilet inside. Nice to sit out in the chilly sun (Mar 23).

Name: Coffee Manifesto
Address: Caferağa, Güneşli Bahçe Sok No:40A, Kadıköy
Author: Sophie M
Review: Nice little coffee shop tucked away in the corner. It's in the heart of Kadikoy, down one of the many, busy pedestrian streets with bars and food places. Very friendly staff too.

Name: Poiká Breakfast & Coffee
Address: Alemdar, Hacı Tahsin Bey Sk. No:7/A, Fatih
Author: Ahmad Kato
Review: My wife and I were looking for a spot to eat, I got on google to see what’s around and this place had my interest. It’s a little hidden lowkey spot. We ordered an egg pie and a couple meat pies. The food was delicious and freshly made. The service was excellent, we were welcomed and greeted with a smile. Clean place and smells great. The coffee was really good!! My wife and I loved it and were big coffee people. We also ordered a matcha latte but I recommend the coffee. Excellent spot for a quick bite I highly recommend it you won’t be disappointed!!

Name: Poiká Breakfast & Coffee
Address: Alemdar, Hacı Tahsin Bey Sk. No:7/A, Fatih
Author: Kwang Sujinno
Review: A nice cafe with good breakfast options and excellent service. It’s a great place to start your day, but I wouldn’t recommend sitting outside due to a strong smell of cat pee.

Name: Poiká Breakfast & Coffee
Address: Alemdar, Hacı Tahsin Bey Sk. No:7/A, Fatih
Author: Eloise Jupp
Review: If you are an Australian and you like decent coffee and a light, healthy brekky, this is the place for you. The granola bowl was lovely, and the coffee was the best we've had in Turkey (we don't like Turkish coffee at all). The omelette with cheese was also lovely.

Name: Poiká Breakfast & Coffee
Address: Alemdar, Hacı Tahsin Bey Sk. No:7/A, Fatih
Author: Vivek Bariya
Review: This place serves the best breakfast – it's yummy and good for you. Plus, you can sit outside and enjoy the view of the street and sunshine. It's super clean too! I totally recommend it to everyone looking for a great breakfast spot.

Name: Poiká Breakfast & Coffee
Address: Alemdar, Hacı Tahsin Bey Sk. No:7/A, Fatih
Author: Shakera Ali
Review: This was my regular place for breakfast/ brunch when I was in Istanbul. Staff are lovely and always smiling, place is clean and food is delicious! My favourite was the omelette with sucuk and granola bowl. Coffee was delicious. I would highly recommend

Name: daRoute Coffee
Address: Binbirdirek, Celine Hotel, Peykhane Cd. No:28, Fatih
Author: Mory Rollins
Review: An amazing hidden gem of a café, easily missed from the street. We walked past the entrance at least once a day, for 4 days before spotting it. So glad we found it though. Service was friendly, quick, and attentive. Presentation was superb, and the food was amazing. If we were here longer we would definitely go back.

Name: daRoute Coffee
Address: Binbirdirek, Celine Hotel, Peykhane Cd. No:28, Fatih
Author: Alex Schofield
Review: Great cafe. Loads of coffee choices and non coffee cold drinks. I had flat white, white choc mocha, cool lime cocktail, all nice. Service is very friendly and attentive in a beautiful inner courtyard. Its a bit hidden down a covered walkway so many people would pass the entrance easily. Menu has various cake/cheesecakes which taste great. Not much for lunch options however.

Name: daRoute Coffee
Address: Binbirdirek, Celine Hotel, Peykhane Cd. No:28, Fatih
Author: A-Cute Hunger
Review:
Here's a review for "daRoute Coffee" in Istanbul:

Located just a 5-minute walk from the main square, daRoute Coffee is a hidden gem you might easily pass by, so keep an eye out for its circular signage.

The ambience is truly unique; a walk through a tunnel corridor leads you into a beautiful and serene glass-covered courtyard, offering a peaceful escape from the bustling tourist areas outside.

The coffee here is excellent, and the tiramisu is absolutely divine. The staff are incredibly helpful, hospitable, and charming, always willing to go out of their way to enhance your experience.

A worthwhile stop if you're in the vicinity!

Name: daRoute Coffee
Address: Binbirdirek, Celine Hotel, Peykhane Cd. No:28, Fatih
Author: maurice edwards
Review:
If you want to leave the hustle and bustle and sit down for just a drink this is a hidden gem. Hidden from the street through a cool ancient tunnel to reach a beautiful setting which is bright and cozy like an oasis garden. Great variety of drinks and something sweet to have prices are very reasonable. Staff are amazing.

Dont miss out

Name: daRoute Coffee
Address: Binbirdirek, Celine Hotel, Peykhane Cd. No:28, Fatih
Author: Rolitta .S
Review:
Beautiful atmosphere, great coffee and very respectful and accommodating staff.
The entrance is cozy, you go through an ancient looking tunnel to reach the spacious seating area with a bonsai in the middle adding organic vibes.

Name: World House Coffee Co.
Address: Şahkulu, Galip Dede Cd. No:85/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Сема Тахмаз
Review:
As a coffee addict, cold brew 7/10 apple pie 8/10 .
Prices are good considering the location. Ambiance is nice.

Name: World House Coffee Co.
Address: Şahkulu, Galip Dede Cd. No:85/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Betül Akgül
Review: Nice coffee shop and atmosphere. But the coffee wasn’t good at all unfortunately. Couldn’t finished it

Name: World House Coffee Co.
Address: Şahkulu, Galip Dede Cd. No:85/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Aleksandrina Grigorjeva
Review:
Nice atmosphere, I was curious how is the coffee but my coffee latte was not good at all...price 3.16 euro
cakes in my opinion too expensive- 6,60 euro, after coffee I didn't want to take a risk and order a desert

Name: World House Coffee Co.
Address: Şahkulu, Galip Dede Cd. No:85/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Abdullah Waqdan
Review:
They provide a good service with a smile, the coffeeshop is cosy with warm atmosphere.
A nice stop on the way while shopping or touring the area.
Good coffee, good dessert choices.

Name: World House Coffee Co.
Address: Şahkulu, Galip Dede Cd. No:85/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Vanessa McCormack
Review: This is such a lovely coffee shop. It’s cute, the staff are friendly, the tea was excellent and there are plenty of cats to make friends with !

Name: NO:12 Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Alemdar, Prof. Kazım İsmail Gürkan Cd. No:12, Fatih
Author: Anmol Gupta
Review: I recently visited No. 12 Coffee on a hot afternoon and tried their caramel frappuccino which was simply amazing - Perfectly sweet and creamy and the perfect amount of coffee to balance it out. It paired wonderfully with the rich brownie. Both treats were delicious. The atmosphere of the cafe is cozy, inviting and fun making it a great spot to relax. Highly recommend it for anyone looking for a great coffee and dessert experience!

Name: NO:12 Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Alemdar, Prof. Kazım İsmail Gürkan Cd. No:12, Fatih
Author: Georgia Grundy
Review: Great pit stop for an iced coffee in the heat and the cafe has air conditioning! ❤️🙏🏻 I'm vegan - only cafe I could find on the go offering dairy free milk. They have alpro oat milk but also offer almond on the menu. Additional fee for dairy free milk.

Name: NO:12 Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Alemdar, Prof. Kazım İsmail Gürkan Cd. No:12, Fatih
Author: Thanet Thungsrisuk
Review: Nice Coffee. Nice Place to take a rest after long strolling around the walking street. With Lovely ambient music. Such a good vibe place.

Name: NO:12 Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Alemdar, Prof. Kazım İsmail Gürkan Cd. No:12, Fatih
Author: Gemma Armorgie
Review: Lovely drinks! Brilliant coffee and highly recommend the frozen strawberry! Will be coming back. Friendly and helpful staff

Name: NO:12 Coffee Sultanahmet
Address: Alemdar, Prof. Kazım İsmail Gürkan Cd. No:12, Fatih
Author: Rafhaan Shah
Review: Nice coffee shop as part of the hotel. Very friendly staff and well made drinks, hot and cold.

Name: Coffee No.4
Address: Alemdar, Molla Fenari Sk. No:4, Fatih
Author: Veit Brnk
Review:
Cozy little café, slightly away from all the tourist hustle and bustle.

Seating available inside the café, in front, and across the street.

Large selection of Turkish specialties. I highly recommend the Ottoman Turkish coffee, Turkish coffee made with hot sand, and the Turkish pistachio coffee.

The service is very helpful and friendly, and the prices are quite fair. We will definitely come back again!

Name: Coffee No.4
Address: Alemdar, Molla Fenari Sk. No:4, Fatih
Author: Karen Ceballos
Review: Spent a nice evening in this coffee shop with Turkish coffee and pistachio coffee which were boiled on sand (very cool to see). The service was also excellent, the shopkeeper was very kind and even indulged us when we asked if we could take a video of the coffee being prepared on sand. Highly recommended.

Name: Coffee No.4
Address: Alemdar, Molla Fenari Sk. No:4, Fatih
Author: James Southworth
Review:
We spotted the menu here on our first night and just had to come back to try it out.

The pistachio coffee is like nothing I’ve ever tasted, traditionally made and absolutely delicious - I also got a passion fruit lemonade, and mango lemonade, both were fresh and refreshing.

The coffee was that good we bought a bag to take home with us and I wore an extra layer to fit it in my bag. Well worth it!

Name: Coffee No.4
Address: Alemdar, Molla Fenari Sk. No:4, Fatih
Author: Adam
Review:
Was skeptical about pistachio coffee but it turn out to be delicious.
The Turkish coffee was also great and well made , not too bitter, not too hot.

Name: Coffee No.4
Address: Alemdar, Molla Fenari Sk. No:4, Fatih
Author: Shirin ZK
Review:
I came here on my last day in Istanbul to have some rest before my next flight.
The staff here are super friendly and welcoming.
They explained to me about all the options on the menu with patience.
They also have a station with all sorts phone chargers.
Highly recommended :)

Name: dods coffee
Address: Teşvikiye, Teşvikiye Fırın Sk. No: 7/B, Şişli
Author: Khaled Nasser
Review: advertised itself as a specialty coffee spot, but unfortunately, it was a huge disappointment. The quality of the coffee was far from what you’d expect from a place claiming to specialize in it. It lacked depth, flavor, and any of the characteristics you'd associate with well-crafted coffee. It was so bad that I couldn't even finish my drink. Honestly, it didn’t feel like specialty coffee at all, and it’s definitely not worth the hype.

Name: dods coffee
Address: Teşvikiye, Teşvikiye Fırın Sk. No: 7/B, Şişli
Author: Mohamed Bucheeri
Review:
Very weird coffee place
Tried the v60
Tastes like water
Worst i ever tried
Asked my friends to lower their voice and to change their seats although no one was there so they left and went to the place next door
On the other hand music was load?
Im confused how did the place got a good rating
Some people are not capable of running a business
The owner was very rude without a reason

Name: dods coffee
Address: Teşvikiye, Teşvikiye Fırın Sk. No: 7/B, Şişli
Author: Anfal
Review: They have a good cold brew

Name: dods coffee
Address: Teşvikiye, Teşvikiye Fırın Sk. No: 7/B, Şişli
Author: Tarneem
Review: One of the best lattes I’ve had in Istanbul. Definitely worth the try.

Name: dods coffee
Address: Teşvikiye, Teşvikiye Fırın Sk. No: 7/B, Şişli
Author: Erdi Toy
Review:
One of the best coffee shops in Istanbul. Cozy athmosphere, friendly barista, and
great americano

Name: Twins Coffee Roasters
Address: Gümüşsuyu, İnönü Cd. No: 23/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Chantal
Review:
The bar is really cool
The drinks are nice, the snacks are a perfect match to the drinks.
Good atmosphere and perfect to have a evening there or to look a sport match. Go there and have a nice summer night and the owner is more then handsome and his energy is more then beautiful  🫶🏻

Name: Twins Coffee Roasters
Address: Gümüşsuyu, İnönü Cd. No: 23/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Alena Pl
Review: We found this place when we were passing by to our hotel. The owner is amazing, he chatted with us and recommended very tasty vine. The atmosphere is really good. The staff is very polite 🫶

Name: Twins Coffee Roasters
Address: Gümüşsuyu, İnönü Cd. No: 23/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Pitinun Phungsripheng
Review: nice place for coffee in the morning, wine/beer in the evening

Name: Twins Coffee Roasters
Address: Gümüşsuyu, İnönü Cd. No: 23/A, Beyoğlu
Author: ninasen93
Review: Was there in the morning and was surprised by nice choice of deep house music , the mood was set ! Plus the coffee was wonderful and barista smiled a lot. Appreciate it :)

Name: Twins Coffee Roasters
Address: Gümüşsuyu, İnönü Cd. No: 23/A, Beyoğlu
Author: John MCKEOWN
Review: Fantastic place to meet up, drink and mingle. Great cocktails and top notch Turkish wine selection to taste and purchase. Staff is superb and helpful. Great vibe ... thankfully unpretentious and welcoming.

Name: Good Coffee Roasting Co.
Address: 19 Mayıs, Okur Sk. No: 16A, Kadıköy
Author: Anastasia Morozova
Review:
Found this coffee shop through an instagram recommendation and fell in love completely!

The staff are the star of the show and their thoughtful approach for coffee and service earned huge respect from me.

It’s hard to find a coffee place in Turkey where they make actually good specialty coffee. I mean it. This shop has a special place in my heart now 🧡

And all of the above made me become a regular customer there :) 10/10 recommend

Name: Good Coffee Roasting Co.
Address: 19 Mayıs, Okur Sk. No: 16A, Kadıköy
Author: Roberto De Gennaro
Review: Very nice place. The cappuccino was 42 TL ($2.26) which is not cheap considering the average prices for food in the area but it isn't expensive compering it to other coffee bar prices. The coffee was also very good and well presented. The person who served the coffee could speak English and it is a good thing as not many people in this area do speak English. The place also has many plug sockets ideal for remote workers.

Name: Good Coffee Roasting Co.
Address: 19 Mayıs, Okur Sk. No: 16A, Kadıköy
Author: Rafah
Review: It is the best specialty coffee I have tried in Istanbul. The coffee is not only good, I give it a rating 1000/10 , I highly recommend to try (COLOMBIA MONTEBLANCO)

Name: Good Coffee Roasting Co.
Address: 19 Mayıs, Okur Sk. No: 16A, Kadıköy
Author: TinaG
Review:
A coffee shop with a Melbourne Touch.

As Google isn't widely used in Turkey, I came across Good Coffee Roasting Co. on Google as one of the higher rated cafes in the city. Mustafa, the owner (who by the way speaks fluent English) is SUPER friendly and knowledgable when it comes to coffee. Don't be afraid to ask him to explain the beans and his recommendations. He opened this cafe around 9 months ago with the concept of "simplicity rather than making things overly complicated." There is a beautiful patio outside with plenty of seats available. You can find sandwiches and toasts as well as sweets and vegan pastries. They also have plant/vegan milk substitutes. If you feel like having tea, they have a selection of teas as well. I highly recommend this place if you are in search of a nice coffeeshop with AMAZING coffee.

Name: Good Coffee Roasting Co.
Address: 19 Mayıs, Okur Sk. No: 16A, Kadıköy
Author: Tugce Bayrakdar Turgut
Review:
WARNING! Zeyno’s cookies are highly addictive!!

The best coffee shop in town. I can easily say that it is even in the top 5 of the Melbourne coffee shops.

If you want to taste world standards of coffee do not search anywhere else. Do yourself a favour and visit here.

Friendly and happy staff. Simple and elegant interior design. House roasted and freshly ground coffee. Best snacks and deserts.

It is obvious that a coffee enthusiast is running the business!

Little tip: Try Nitro

Name: Exit Music Coffee & Records
Address: Feneryolu, Mazhar Osman Sk. No:32, Kadıköy
Author: P. luna
Review:
Short review:

Small, cozy, Great music taste, lovely staff, Tasty coffee.

Busy in weekdays, Not so busy on saturday, closed on sunday.

Long review:

I've been long searching for a cafe in istanbul that played live music, DJ performance and had great coffee, i just never found any.

Years of search later i came across this, and due to my busy schedule i never had time to visit it till now.

And im glad i did, its honestly a very nice cafe, the atmosphere, the music, vinyls, coffee smell, colors, and decor is very cool.

And its located in a small shop in a beautiful alley, there are places to park your car on the street, in fact the entire neighborhood is very quiet you would enjoy a walk there.

As for the cafe, like i said its not big, rather small, couple of seating area, and out as well there are few seat and few benches.

I was alone when i visited so it was a bit awkward, they have your typical selection of coffee, i got a cappuccino, and it was amazing, and i had a small chat with the cafe's hostess, very friendly too.

The coffee was good that i got over my awkwardness of being alone in the cafe.

There is box of vinyls should you wish to buy some, if im not mistaken clothes too.

I haven't checked on bathrooms but there should be one.

Oh and they have wifi too if you didn't have internet.

Price wise its very great too.

My final verdict on this visit:

First off im coming again for sure, its a far visit for one small cafe but i loved it so much ill come back again.

And if you wanted great music with your coffee, this place is for you.

They are full of customers on weekdays, empty on saturday like my visit today, and closed on sunday.

They open until 8 PM.

If id suggest anything, do visit them with a friend, its a very lovely little place.

Name: Exit Music Coffee & Records
Address: Feneryolu, Mazhar Osman Sk. No:32, Kadıköy
Author: Amanzhol Kellett
Review: Great coffee shop also selling records, vintage clothes and other retro bits, run by a friendly owner

Name: Exit Music Coffee & Records
Address: Feneryolu, Mazhar Osman Sk. No:32, Kadıköy
Author: Ozge Dursun
Review: Literally the best coffee around. Also possible to find rare or popular records, great music and pet friendly staff. Sit around all day, listen to great music, nibble on snacks and all is good.

Name: Exit Music Coffee & Records
Address: Feneryolu, Mazhar Osman Sk. No:32, Kadıköy
Author: Kerem Yalçın Yılmaztekin
Review: Hand picked LP archive. Small and neat place. Great coffee, great music, smiling people. Perfect for vinyl junkies and coffee addicts.

Name: Exit Music Coffee & Records
Address: Feneryolu, Mazhar Osman Sk. No:32, Kadıköy
Author: Gün İrigül
Review: Such a perfect little cafe. So cosy great coffee and always great music.

Name: Sunday Coffee Bar
Address: Teşvikiye, Ahmet Fetgari Sk 44/B, Şişli
Author: Mert A. K.
Review: Coffee and service are very good. The people are very friendly. I like chilling in the chairs downstairs and watch the street. They don’t allow you to open your laptop there though, only upstairs. The space upstairs is a nice quiet spot to study and work.

Name: Sunday Coffee Bar
Address: Teşvikiye, Ahmet Fetgari Sk 44/B, Şişli
Author: Batuhan Bakanay
Review: Love this place so much. There is no Arab! No muss no fuss. A very lovely coffee shop.

Name: Sunday Coffee Bar
Address: Teşvikiye, Ahmet Fetgari Sk 44/B, Şişli
Author: Mel G
Review: Absolutely love this little cafe! Very polite, professional and timely staff. Lovely seating areas, fantastically decorated/great interior design (please don’t change!), very clean lavatory and not to mention this cafe has sockets to charge your electronic devices at almost every seating area! Also they have taken full precautions against COVID-19. Fully recommend, 5/5 venue & atmosphere

Name: Sunday Coffee Bar
Address: Teşvikiye, Ahmet Fetgari Sk 44/B, Şişli
Author: Awad Alshehri
Review: I had one of the best latte at this coffeehouse.  It's amazing and you can have a great time at here

Name: Sunday Coffee Bar
Address: Teşvikiye, Ahmet Fetgari Sk 44/B, Şişli
Author: Fehaid Alotaibi
Review: Very normal coffee shop they don’t know how to make a proper coffee latte

Name: Kava Coffee
Address: Hacımimi, Necatibey Cd. No:63/A, Beyoğlu
Author: H Lal
Review:
Coffee is very yummy, the beans roast is very well done. This coffee place being within the Karakoy area makes it enjoyable to walk around.

Unfortunately I wish the rudeness of the waiter can be improved!

Name: Kava Coffee
Address: Hacımimi, Necatibey Cd. No:63/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Wafa
Review: The coffee is so good and the employe respectfully, all the black and iced latte are perfect I RECOMMEND IT

Name: Kava Coffee
Address: Hacımimi, Necatibey Cd. No:63/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Sean Philipp
Review: A decaf option and it’s quite good. Upstairs is quiet with outward facing seats which look intended for working. Great price too.

Name: Kava Coffee
Address: Hacımimi, Necatibey Cd. No:63/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Bryan Wicaksono
Review: A nice and minimalist coffee shop with excellent service and great coffee. Be sure to pay this one a visit

Name: Kava Coffee
Address: Hacımimi, Necatibey Cd. No:63/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Florian Kern
Review: The coffee is good, but the breakfast made me sad. I had eggs with mushrooms, salad and cheese. The cheese and the salad was old, the mushrooms came from a can. It was the sadest food I had in Istanbul and it’s a shame they serve this to tourists.

Name: Coffee Department Nişantaşı
Address: Teşvikiye, Sezai Selek Sk. No:28/30 - B, Şişli
Author: The Dude
Review:
One of the best specialty coffee in Istanbul. Nice selection of beans for drip. Unfortunately, just one choice for expresso.

Love the atmosphere. Definitely a nice place to catch up with some readings or light work on your laptop.

Highly recommended if you're in the area. Tip: don't even think about driving here as the parking will be nearly impossible

Name: Coffee Department Nişantaşı
Address: Teşvikiye, Sezai Selek Sk. No:28/30 - B, Şişli
Author: Dan Rowe
Review:
Excellent coffee, as with their sibling store in Balat. Great choice of coffees prepared very well, and with detailed background and tasting notes available (in English and Turkish).

The only thing I’d improve is their cake selection!  (Update: lemon cake is excellent)

Highly recommended for anyone looking for specialty coffee, even with all the local competition.

Name: Coffee Department Nişantaşı
Address: Teşvikiye, Sezai Selek Sk. No:28/30 - B, Şişli
Author: Mohsen Sarakbi
Review:
Excellent coffee and service. The place is great for being away for a bit. Calm atmosphere and tasty coffee. They sell coffee beans as well. It's nice.
They recommended a variety of coffee types with different tastes, each comes with a card shows its features. You can choose any for filter coffee choice or buy it.
A must try for coffee lovers!

Name: Coffee Department Nişantaşı
Address: Teşvikiye, Sezai Selek Sk. No:28/30 - B, Şişli
Author: NY NY
Review:
Nice vibe, Great coffee, Excellent service!!
This immediately became my favorite coffee shop in Istanbul ✨

Name: Coffee Department Nişantaşı
Address: Teşvikiye, Sezai Selek Sk. No:28/30 - B, Şişli
Author: ipiesings
Review: The filter coffee was excellent not too sour. Just my liking. The place is also nice, as it just faces the street and especially in the morning on weekdays it’s nice to people watch on the terrace. It was early when I visited, so the person working there was kind of out of it and not quite interested in my questions😅

Name: Norm Coffee
Address: Cihangir, Güneşli Sok. 39/A, Beyoğlu
Author: G S
Review:
Norm Coffee has been serving in Cihangir for a long time, and I've always tried to visit it, though not very frequently, since its opening day.

The owner, Cem, is very hospitable, has excellent communication with the customers, and you can tell he is good at his job from the fact that he has created a cult place in Cihangir over the years.

If you ever find yourself in the area, make sure to have a coffee and enjoy it.

Name: Norm Coffee
Address: Cihangir, Güneşli Sok. 39/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Anastasia Yevtushenko
Review:
It was 5 minutes before they were closing and I ordered espresso tonic. They were out of tonic but barista went to the grocery shop to buy one. It was very nice and I was extremely pleased :)

The place itself is bright and modern. You can buy coffee beans here as well as chocolate and some other local food.

One of the best coffee places in Beyoğlu!

Name: Norm Coffee
Address: Cihangir, Güneşli Sok. 39/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Emily Rufca
Review:
Unfortunately this is my worst dining experience during my month in Istanbul:( I ordered a Japanese latte and chocolate/orange cake piece for 14usd!! The drink was a watery ice coffee and the cake was dry, not worth eating even 😩. The prices charged also didn't match the menu, latte said 180 and was charged 250. Insanity. The cake was charged 215. Insanity.

Staff was friendly though, so that's what the stars go towards.

Name: Norm Coffee
Address: Cihangir, Güneşli Sok. 39/A, Beyoğlu
Author: Nijat Gafarson
Review: Coffee was not bad, yet attitude of staff leaves much to be desired; I kindly asked them during a midday when cafe was almost empty if they can turn down their loud music for a literally 10min since I had some urgent phone call, they unfortunately declined. They have also asked a bit rude to wear a mask INSIDE OF CAFE which is indeed not forbidden if we are sharing the same table. Please note, that me and my friend haven’t been approached as foreigners yet as locals. Maybe they are showing more kindness to the foreigners & expats living here. Won’t come to this place anytime soon. Left a bit unpleasant attitude towards us.

Name: Norm Coffee
Address: Cihangir, Güneşli Sok. 39/A, Beyoğlu
Author: hashim alsadah
Review: Really loved the place, tiny and cozy. The coffee wasn't great though

Name: Roastery House Coffee
Address: Topkapı, Şht. Albay Başaran Sk. 12/A, Fatih
Author: Kin Yan
Review:
I tried to search a good cafe offering good coffee nearby my Airbnb.  And Roastery House Coffee is my favourite.  Coffee, price, staff and music are much better than cafes in touristy places.  I enjoyed a very good morning there.

From my observation, they have regular and local customers from people working nearby.  It seems owner knows every customer and talk to each one, including me.  And thank you for letting me to try the Turkish coffee at the end of the meal!  It’s good combination with chocolate

Name: Roastery House Coffee
Address: Topkapı, Şht. Albay Başaran Sk. 12/A, Fatih
Author: Silent Visitor
Review: Nice coffee, delicious sweet, I recommend v60 and Japanese siphon.

Name: Roastery House Coffee
Address: Topkapı, Şht. Albay Başaran Sk. 12/A, Fatih
Author: Moe Abdu
Review:
Nice coffee, cozy place, friendly staff, especially Mr Mohammed Ali,
Good luck 👍🏼

Name: Roastery House Coffee
Address: Topkapı, Şht. Albay Başaran Sk. 12/A, Fatih
Author: Wael Donia
Review: Very friendly hosts, excellent service & great coffee 🤩😋

Name: Roastery House Coffee
Address: Topkapı, Şht. Albay Başaran Sk. 12/A, Fatih
Author: Ana Grozea Jauntingtrips[.]com
Review: Didn't enjoy the coffee at all. It was kind of bitter. I asked for a flat white, it was basically turkish coffee with some milk, but had nothing to do with a flat white.

Name: Mundo Novo Coffee & Patisserie
Address: Şahkulu, Müeyyedzade, Serdar-ı Ekrem Cd. No:1, Beyoğlu
Author: Joan Perez Guallar
Review: Charming little café! Despite its small size, it has a welcoming atmosphere. The gluten-free brownie was delicious, and they offered a variety of other gluten-free treats. The barista was incredibly friendly. Definitely coming back!

Name: Mundo Novo Coffee & Patisserie
Address: Şahkulu, Müeyyedzade, Serdar-ı Ekrem Cd. No:1, Beyoğlu
Author: Dorcas Siahaan
Review: best coffee shop, best services, good coffee and hot chocolate and best gluten free cheese cake. Love it and sure will come back.

Name: Mundo Novo Coffee & Patisserie
Address: Şahkulu, Müeyyedzade, Serdar-ı Ekrem Cd. No:1, Beyoğlu
Author: Laurent Langlais
Review: Extremely friendly people, fantastic gluten free cheesecake and cute little place. A great choice to rest after having climbed the Galata tower.

Name: Mundo Novo Coffee & Patisserie
Address: Şahkulu, Müeyyedzade, Serdar-ı Ekrem Cd. No:1, Beyoğlu
Author: Utkan Pazar
Review: Comfy little place nearby Galata. The barista is very skilled and it is a preferable place to take a coffee break. If you only drink and don't eat anything it costs around 40-70₺. Will definitely visit again. By the way orange is the conceptual color of the place. Loved it as well !

Name: Mundo Novo Coffee & Patisserie
Address: Şahkulu, Müeyyedzade, Serdar-ı Ekrem Cd. No:1, Beyoğlu
Author: Xrusa Kgl
Review: An amazing place to enjoy your coffee, your tea and its excellent dishes next to Galata tower! But above all I have to say a big thank you to the manager of the store for his help and his perfect service in a problem of mine that arose from an accident. Thank you for your humanity and your constant smile that filled me with optimism despite my problem.Çök teşekkür ederim hersey için !

Name: Fill Coffee Shop
Address: Bozkurt, Eşref Efendi Sk. NO:64/A, Şişli
Author: Manon Cassier
Review: Thé food was really good (vegan smoothie bowl & the mushroom toast). Thé drinks were also very lovely (iced latte's, chai latte & home made lemonade). The woman who works here is very very friendly and was very social to us. We felt welcome :). One of our favourite places in Istanbul!

Name: Fill Coffee Shop
Address: Bozkurt, Eşref Efendi Sk. NO:64/A, Şişli
Author: Elena B
Review: This is a lovely and cozy place with a very welcoming and pleasant owner.  The food is delicious, the honey is incomparable.  This cafe is home to cute cats that are taken care of by the owner. A chubby black cat adds a homey feel to this place ❤️❤️❤️

Name: Fill Coffee Shop
Address: Bozkurt, Eşref Efendi Sk. NO:64/A, Şişli
Author: Natalia Shoroh
Review: I truly like this place! There’s so kind and warm atmosphere . And owner is really nice person with lovely energy. And I definitely recommend her pumpkin pie! It’s amazingly delicious ! And also I love their cats 😸🧡🧡🧡

Name: Fill Coffee Shop
Address: Bozkurt, Eşref Efendi Sk. NO:64/A, Şişli
Author: Марья Панфилова
Review: Best place in this area to have breakfast. Dishes are very tasty. Coffee too. Friendly staff and cozy cat😸

Name: Fill Coffee Shop
Address: Bozkurt, Eşref Efendi Sk. NO:64/A, Şişli
Author: augusto perim
Review:
Very cozy place! Nice environment and really nice staff.
Food is good and the limoned was incredible!!!!! If you are around, I think it is worth it to try.
The only small problem is that there was only 1 person working and it took my a while to order.

Name: Kara Kedi Coffee Eatery
Address: Halaskargazi, Lotus Walk AVM, Halaskargazi Cd. No:38-66, Şişli
Author: S A
Review: If this place was closer to my hotel, I think I would be there every single morning! I love the place. I love that even though it’s a coffee shop it’s located inside the mall they still have outdoor seating. Plenty of tables so much to see and people watch, which is one of my favorite hobbies by the way. Back to the review itself I loved it. The coffee that I had was, iced. I also had a fresh made apple juice which was so amazing very similar to the one I make at home! The attention of the servers👌🏻👌🏻👌🏻! You can’t go wrong picking this place!

Name: Kara Kedi Coffee Eatery
Address: Halaskargazi, Lotus Walk AVM, Halaskargazi Cd. No:38-66, Şişli
Author: Ari Zingillioglu
Review:
Desserts okay, coffee is good. The atmosphere is nice and call if you need to escape from city drama.

Didn’t like the croissant much. It was so doughy, not crunchy at all.

Overall score 4/5 for a Coffee Shop

Name: Kara Kedi Coffee Eatery
Address: Halaskargazi, Lotus Walk AVM, Halaskargazi Cd. No:38-66, Şişli
Author: ousama awer
Review:
We waited a bit long ... Then  I called for the waiter to bring me the menu which was ok.

But please be careful they are adding to your bill 10% service fees which is totally illegal. And when we asked about it he said he will bring the manager which he never came !!

It's a scam and not mentioned in the menu

Name: Kara Kedi Coffee Eatery
Address: Halaskargazi, Lotus Walk AVM, Halaskargazi Cd. No:38-66, Şişli
Author: Mr Fire
Review:
Great place to enjoy
And the prices are good too
Great service from staff, understanding and also helpful
Premium and comfortable seats
Good job

Name: Kara Kedi Coffee Eatery
Address: Halaskargazi, Lotus Walk AVM, Halaskargazi Cd. No:38-66, Şişli
Author: Polina Urman
Review: The atmosphere was excellent and the service was quite fast. We had a brunch and were very happy with it. They offer a variety of different croissants, as well as more conventional breakfasts. Coffee was also good, I've never seen such a huge foam on a cappucino. Additional points for cute tissues with cats!

Name: Altkat Coffee Community
Address: Caferağa, Dalga Sk. No:19, Kadıköy
Author: A Kim
Review: Friendly staff, decent selection of pastries but no cooked food. Free wifi but you need to ask for the password. It’s bizarre that the cafe is self serve ie you collect at the counter considering how much you pay for the desserts. The dessert are tasty but the coffee is mediocre.

Name: Altkat Coffee Community
Address: Caferağa, Dalga Sk. No:19, Kadıköy
Author: Sara Gzara
Review:
Cozy and charming café with excellent coffee and patisserie.

Worth going to just for the deserts alone. I’ve tried their pistachio melange and it was to die for. Their cortado was excellent and the barista was very attentive and kind.

The décor and music are very homelike, laidback and inviting. Small inconvenience, it’s quite small with mostly outdoors seating and so not appropriate for working/studying or sitting for hours at a time especially in the colder months. However if you’re like me and you hate the huge open spaces which don’t feel personal or cosy, well this place is right up your alley!

Name: Altkat Coffee Community
Address: Caferağa, Dalga Sk. No:19, Kadıköy
Author: Simon Etchells
Review: Great Cafe to enjoy breakfast. Staff very friendly and lovely little hidden balcony!!

Name: Altkat Coffee Community
Address: Caferağa, Dalga Sk. No:19, Kadıköy
Author: Kürtős Kalács
Review:
Best V60 coffee I’ve had in Istanbul. So flavorful yet smooth.

And the staff is super friendly and not in an awkward way

Name: Altkat Coffee Community
Address: Caferağa, Dalga Sk. No:19, Kadıköy
Author: Shaden Zarraq
Review:
One of my favorite coffee places in Moda, beautiful interior though very small, only one table inside
Coffee is really good with good prices
Brownie is so delicious
Americano is reaally good

Note: very loud at the weekend
Menu attached

Name: Ravenista Coffee
Address: Meşrutiyet, Hacı Mansur Sk. 18/A, Şişli
Author: Maria Nayir
Review: Since I only drank a cup of herbal tea and some strawberry smoothie, I am only rating and commenting on the place. It was quiet and peaceful, not crowded at all. The gentleman who runs this place was very nice and kind. There is a very nice little garden in the backyard. It feels like you're having nice conversations in the garden of your friend’s house. The only downside was the cat odors, the furniture was a bit old and the couch was full of cat fur and sadly I have allergy on that.

Name: Ravenista Coffee
Address: Meşrutiyet, Hacı Mansur Sk. 18/A, Şişli
Author: Isabelle Ji
Review: Cute cafe with a peaceful back patio! Ordered a cortado and Turkish coffee — both delightful. But most importantly, there are several cats hanging out and being content, thanks to the store owner for taking care of the cats!

Name: Ravenista Coffee
Address: Meşrutiyet, Hacı Mansur Sk. 18/A, Şişli
Author: Artur Mitt
Review: It's an amazing place in Istanbul. Relaxing and cozy, it's a great place for breakfast. Run by a family, they help you to feel at home. The club sandwich and fried eggs are great! It's definitely worth a visit for coffee or tea. Homemade cheesecake is simply amazing.

Name: Ravenista Coffee
Address: Meşrutiyet, Hacı Mansur Sk. 18/A, Şişli
Author: Luka Gurdulić
Review:
Coffee and lemonade is super tasty, the little cute terrace in the back with siamese cats feels like home, cozy vibes.
The owners are a married older couple and are very friendly, all about this place is really nice to have a cup of coffee and relax. Highly recommended to come visit

Name: Ravenista Coffee
Address: Meşrutiyet, Hacı Mansur Sk. 18/A, Şişli
Author: Sun Kisser
Review:
Super cute space with the sweetest owner.
She’ll do her best to find something you’ll like on the breakfast menu!

I’d say take a Turkish coffee rather than cappuccino- but my husband’s egg breakfast sandwich was yummy and tuna salad fresh and filling!

Thank you for the hospitality Ravenista!

Name: Newtown Specialty Coffee Bahçeşehir
Address: Doğaparkı Cad. No:13-15AC Bahçeşehirpark Cadde A Blok No:9, Başakşehir
Author: Luna
Review: the atmosphere was honestly one of the best in istanbul. I sat therefor hours with my friend today talking over a cup of good coffee. the music and the decor and the tasty coffee honestly make a great combination. the bathrooms were clean and the staff were really nice as well. would come here again

Name: Newtown Specialty Coffee Bahçeşehir
Address: Doğaparkı Cad. No:13-15AC Bahçeşehirpark Cadde A Blok No:9, Başakşehir
Author: Owen Stroll
Review:
Arguably, the best coffee I've had in Istanbul. Their pour over V60
is fantastic and always expertly made. The staff are genuinely great people and the atmosphere is wonderful. The walnut cake...god damn...so good. I dream about that walnut cake.
